description: An introduction to embedded systems and DC circuitry.  Embedded systems focusing on the C programming language, number systems, and data/logical operations. DC circuitry focused on basic circuit theory including voltage, current, ohm’s law, and basic circuit components. Includes laboratory work (e.g., with multimeters and oscilloscopes) and circuit prototyping.

title: Circuit Theory I
description: An introduction to linear circuit theory; analysis of linear circuits containing resistance, inductance, and capacitance. Mutual inductance and transformers.

title: Microcontrollers
description: Architecture, assembly language programming, serial and parallel input/output, interface circuits, and applications.

title: Design of Digital Systems
description: Design of combinational and sequential systems including asynchronous circuits; testing of digital systems. Emphasis is placed on hardware description languages, computer-aided design tools, and simulations.

title: Undergraduate Research Experience
description: Open to students pursuing undergraduate research projects.

title: Honors Research in Electrical Engineering
description: Individual research problem selected with approval of faculty advisor. Open to students in the College of Engineering honors program. A report is presented orally and in writing during the last semester.

title: Electronics Laboratory
description: Design, simulation, construction and testing of electronic circuits.

title: Circuit Theory II
description: Time-domain transient response of second-order circuits. Circuit analysis using Laplace transforms. Frequency response, filters, transfer functions, two-port networks, and convolution.

title: Linear Systems
description: Time- and frequency-domain analysis of signals and systems. Concepts include signal classification, convolution, Fourier series, Fourier transforms, filters, signal sampling, and discrete transforms.

title: Electric Circuits for Engineers
description: Principles of direct-current circuits, alternating-current circuits, analog and digital electronics.

title: Electronics I
description: Fundamentals of electronic components (e.g., diodes, MOSFETs, BJTs, op amps) and electronic circuits.

title: Electronics II
description: Continuation of Electronics I, with emphasis on performance measures, including frequency response. Additional topics include Miller's theorem, cascoding, and the application of negative feedback.

title: Control Systems Design
description: Modeling, analysis, and design of control systems. Topics include feedback control; time response and stability of dynamic systems; introduction to root locus and frequency response design.

title: Applied Scientific Computing for Engineers
description: Mathematical and programming techniques for solving various classes of numerical problems.  General topics, such as the precision of floating point numbers are also discussed. Statistics, linear algebra and optimization are the primary classes of numerical problems addressed.

title: Computer Networking
description: Introduction to application, transport, network and link layer protocols, with emphasis to Internet TCP/IP protocols.

title: Electromagnetic Theory I
description: Vector analysis, electrostatics, magnetostatics, Faraday's Law, Maxwell's Equations, transmission line analysis, plane waves, Poynting's theorem, and applications.

title: Introduction to Biomedical Engineering
description: Overview of engineering applied to clinical medicine and the life sciences. Topics include sensors and instrumentation to acquire physiologic data, imaging techniques, biomechanics, health care information technology, assistive technology, telemedicine, home care, emergency services, battlefield medicine, and biomedical career opportunities.

title: Energy Conversion
description: Three-phase systems, magnetic circuits, transformers, and DC and AC machines.

title: Wind Energy Research
description: This course provides an opportunity to pursue one or more longer-term research questions related to wind engineering, including small-wind siting and installation and meteorological-tower installation and data analysis.

title: Senior Design Experience I
description: Integrates communications, both verbal and written, with ethics and a collaborative design project to emulate a technical professional environment. Introduces design theory, project management, team dynamics, and socio-economic context to design.

title: Senior Design Experience II
description: Second semester of the senior design experience that implements and demonstrates the project design begun in ECE 590. It includes oral presentations for design reviews and technical documentation such as Project Plan, Concept of Operations, Analyses, Architecture and Requirements, Design Descriptions, and User Manual.

title: Power Electronics
description: Design and analysis of semiconductor-based circuits such as AC-DC converters (rectifiers), DC-DC converters and DC-AC converters (inverters), and their applications in adjustable speed motor-drives, power supplies, and renewable energy conversion systems.

title: Electronic Instrumentation
description: Applications of electronics in the design of analog and digital systems for the measurement of physical variables and in the transduction of these variables into a useful form for both recording and control.

title: Microcomputer Systems Design
description: Design and engineering application of 32-bit microcomputers to instrumentation and control. Investigate the relationship of the C language and assembly language. Timing and other interfacing problems will be covered.

title: Hardware Security Attack Techniques and Countermeasures
description: Exploration and approaches to address security vulnerabilities, execute attacks, and implement countermeasures against specific hardware attacks. Topics includes reverse-engineering, counterfeiting/cloning, side-channel attacks, fault injection, and physical tampering (e.g., hardware Trojans, mod-chip).

title: Real-Time Embedded Systems
description: Interconnection of peripherals, such as CAN networks, DA/AD converters, and Timers. Implementation of device drivers on top of micro-kernels. Build a simple real time distributed embedded system.

title: Introduction to Computer Graphics
description: An introduction to the software, hardware, and algorithmic aspects of graphics generation. Programming assignments will provide experience in implementing interactive interfaces via application-level graphics libraries.

title: Advanced Digital Design Using Logic Synthesis
description: Applications of hardware description languages (HDLs) for the design of complex digital systems. Topics include designing and simulating using HDLs, logic synthesis into FPGAs and ASICs, optimization techniques, timing issues, hardware verification, and design for testability.

title: Computer Engineering Design Lab
description: The Design and construction of simple I/O devices and a microcomputer system using simple to complex programmable devices.

title: Digital Electronics
description: Design of high-performance digital electronics and systems. Topics include design metrics, device modeling, transmission-line models for wiring, signaling conventions, transmitter and receiver circuits, noise analysis and management, power distribution, timing and synchronization techniques.

title: Digital Signal Processing
description: Difference equation characterization of digital signal processing systems, transient and steady-state analysis of digital filters using the Z-transform, spectral analysis of digital signals, and design and implementation of digital filters.

title: Multimedia Compression
description: Introduction to multimedia creation and representation. Design of multimedia systems, which incorporate audio, image and video. Topics will include the analysis and design of multimedia compression, streaming, delivery, security and authoring. Emphasis will be placed on current multimedia standards and applications.

title: Computer Design I
description: Concepts of computer design.  Information representation, instruction sets and addressing modes.  Arithmetic and logic unit design for fixed and floating point operations. Hardwired and microprogrammed control design. Concepts of pipelining, CISC and RISC architecture. Memory system design including virtual memory, caches and interleaved memories. I/O design methods, interrupt mechanisms, DMA and system integration.

title: Communication Systems I
description: Introduction to the analysis and design of analog and digital communication systems. Topics include analog and digital modulation schemes, digital encoding of messages, mathematical modeling of communications systems, noise in communication links and calculation of performance measures for practical links.

title: Communication Systems II
description: Error-correction code design and analysis with emphasis on block and convolutional coding and decoding; Trellis-coded modulation. Modeling of bandlimited systems and pulse-shape design, optimal receiver design for bandlimited systems, linear and nonlinear equalization. Multiple-access techniques, orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing and receiver design for CDMA schemes. Simulation of practical digital transmission systems.

title: Design of Communication Circuits
description: The design of communication circuits and systems operating from baseband to UHF frequencies. Topics include tuned-RF amplifiers, oscillators, mixers, LC and ceramic bandpass filters, and demodulators. Projects involve the design and performance testing of a complete radio receiver.

title: Engineering Applications of Machine Intelligence
description: Study of machine intelligence and fuzzy logic concepts and applications in engineering problem domains. As a term project, develop a fuzzy expert system for a specific problem domain that runs on a personal computer and develop the supporting documentation.

title: Wind and Solar Engineering
description: Wind and solar energy resource assessment, operation of renewable-energy systems, photovoltaic and wind-turbine physics, and design of complete systems.

title: Power Laboratory
description: Introduction to energy and power converters. Course includes lecture and laboratory experience in aspects of modeling and analysis of electric machines (synchronous and induction machines as well as transformers), power quality (harmonic measurement), control of DC-DC and DC-AC converters, and motor-drive systems.

title: Power Systems Design
description: A comprehensive study of modeling of the electric power system components and computer simulation of interconnected power systems in steady state. Vector-matrix descriptions are emphasized.

title: Power Systems Protection
description: Analysis of symmetrical and unsymmetrical faults on power systems using symmetrical components technique. Study of protective relaying for protection of power systems against faults. Vector-matrix descriptions and computer solutions are emphasized.

title: Problems in Electrical Engineering
description: Problems in Electrical and Computer Engineering

title: Optoelectronics
description: Applied geometric and physical optics, optical radiation, and the interaction of light and matter. The theory and application of photodetectors, lasers, and other photoemitters. Introduction to fiber optical waveguides, sensors, and systems.

title: Integrated Circuit Design
description: Study of silicon integrated circuits with emphasis on CMOS analog and digital applications. The course covers basic device structure and modeling, circuit analysis, system design, IC design methodology and economics plus IC fabrication processes. Computer-aided tools are used to simulate and layout circuits designed by student groups.

title: Electroacoustics
description: Basic principles of sound; modeling of, and analogous circuits for, mechanical and acoustical systems; microphones and loudspeakers; Thiele-Small parameters; the analysis and design of, and measurements on, common loudspeaker systems and crossover networks. Projects involve the design, simulation, construction and performance-testing of a complete loudspeaker-and-enclosure system.

title: Audio Engineering
description: Solid-state and hollow-state analog electronics applicable to high-fidelity audio. Emphasis is on the analysis, design and construction of audio amplifiers. Projects include design, implementation, and performance-evaluation of a reference-quality audio power amplifier.

title: Analog Electronics
description: Analysis, design, and evaluation of BJT and CMOS operational amplifiers. Core topics include frequency compensation and closed-loop stability, slew-rate optimization, wide-bandwidth design, low-noise design, and macromodeling for simulation.

title: Integrated Circuit Devices and Processes
description: Integrated circuit fabrication processes including oxidation, diffusion, ion implantation, lithography, etching, crystal growth, process monitoring, and device characterization. Design of discrete and IC devices through laboratory experiments and computer simulations.

title: Mixed Signal Measurements
description: Signal classification, noise and uncertainty, TRMS conversion, quantization and ADCs, repetitive sampling and signal recovery techniques, vector voltmeters, basic network analyzers.

title: Control Systems Analysis and Design
description: Use of classical analysis techniques for control system compensation. State space control theory fundamentals are presented in addition to an introductory treatment of several major systems areas.

title: Advanced Microcomputer System Design
description: Design and engineering applications of 16 and 32 bit microprocessors. Utilization of peripheral and co-processor chips.

title: Real-Time Embedded Systems Design
description: Design and implementation of a comprehensive team project of a complete embedded real-time system.

title: Discrete-Time and Computer-Control Systems
description: Analysis and design of discrete-time, sampled-data, and computer-control systems using discrete-state equations and Z-transforms.

title: Fault Diagnosis in Digital Systems
description: Hazards, fault detection in combinational circuits, and sequential machines using path sensitizing and fault-matrix methods, state table analysis, etc.; system reliability through logical redundance.

title: Computer Design II
description: Study of alternate computer hardware structures. Investigation of engineering tradeoffs in implementation of alternative instruction sets and computing structures. Emphasis will be placed on a quantitative approach to cost/performance evaluations including simulation of hardware structures.

title: Wireless Communications
description: This course exposes seniors and first year graduate students to important physical layer concepts in wireless communications. Topics include: cellular architecture, large scale and small scale fading channel models, diversity receivers, DS-CDMA transmitter and receiver design, multi-user detection, multi-carrier CDMA and OFDM performance analysis.

title: Antennas and Microwave Circuits
description: The design of communication circuits and systems operating at microwave frequencies. Topics include antennas, transmission lines, microstrip matching networks, S-parameters, frequency synthesizers, and downconverter components such as LNAs, mixers, and microstrip bandpass filters. Projects involve design, simulation with electronic design automation tools, and laboratory measurements.

title: Control Theory Applied to Bioengineering
description: Development of mathematical models used in the study and analysis of physiological control systems providing techniques for varying pertinent biological parameters.

title: Theory And Techniques Of Bioinstrumentation
description: Theoretical aspects of biological signals, electrodes, transducers, digital imaging and computer-based data acquisition directed toward ECE and other science department majors.

title: Bioinstrumentation Design Laboratory
description: Design and testing of hardware and software for acquiring and analyzing biological signals.

title: Power Seminar
description: Speakers from industry, academia, and government present topics related to power systems engineering.

title: Advanced Power Electronics
description: High-frequency switching dc/dc converters, resonant converters, analysis and control of power electronic systems, and thermal management.

title: Topics in Instrumentation
description: Selected topics related to the general field of electronic instrumentation.

title: Advanced System Theory
description: State space description and analysis of continuous and discrete time dynamic systems including optimal control solutions. Both linear and nonlinear systems are considered.

title: Advanced Hardware Security Attack Techniques and Countermeasures
description: Advanced exploration and approaches to address security vulnerabilities, execute attacks, and implement countermeasures against specific hardware attacks. Topics includes reverse-engineering, counterfeiting/cloning, side-channel attacks, fault injection, and physical tampering (e.g., hardware Trojans, mod-chip).

title: Computer Engineering Methods For Analysis,Simulation/Dgn I
description: Computer-aided and numerical techniques applicable to problems in electrical and computer engineering. Emphasis is on implementation of these techniques on the computer.

title: Network Theory
description: Introduction to the theoretical tools, methods and algorithms to analyze networks, investigate their structure, and their behavior and properties, as well as several practical applications in computer networks, social networks and power grids.

title: Parallel Processing
description: Parallel processing application in signal and image processing. Array processors, pipeline processors, systolic and wavefront arrays, interconnection networks, performance analysis.

title: Sequential Machines
description: Theory and mathematical framework of digital hardware will be developed. Limitations and fault detection of these machines will be explored using the theoretical basis of sequential machines.

title: Computer Engineering Methods For Analysis, Simulation,Dsgn II
Topics in Computer Engineering
description: Selected topics relating to current developments in computer engineering. Topics may include computer architectures, computer networking, multiprocessing, and computer interfaces.

title: Advanced Topics in Electromagnetic Theory
description: Mathematical development of electromagnetic wave theory.

title: Applied Probability Theory & Random Processes
description: Applications of probability theory, random variables and stochastic processes; tools relevant to both modeling uncertainty and inferencing in the presence of uncertainty; probabilistic methods applicable to problems in electrical and computer engineering arena including power systems, communication systems and networks, biomedical systems, etc.

title: Transform Processing of Digital Signals
description: Orthogonal transforms in digital signal processing with emphasis on one- and two-dimensional signals, generalized Wiener filtering, feature selection in pattern recognition, and elements of adaptive filtering techniques.

title: Digital Image Processing
description: Basic concepts and techniques of image formation, representation, analysis, restorations, enhancement, coding, segmentation, and description. Object recognition using shape descriptors and syntactic techniques. Image processing applications in remote sensing, computer vision, and medical diagnosis.

title: Neural Networks in Engineering
description: Engineering applications of artificial neural networks and machine intelligence. Particular emphasis will be placed on determining appropriate applications of alternate computing approaches and establishing efficient hardware support to implement these computational approaches.

title: Therapeutic Medical Devices and Systems
description: This course will cover: (1)  the fundamental principles of electromagnetic energy interactions with biological tissue, and (2) the design and analysis of electromagnetic energy-based therapeutic medical devices and systems. Topics covered include: tissue-energy interactions; neuromuscular electrical stimulation; rationale and applications of thermal therapy; devices for radiofrequency and microwave hyperthermia; thermal ablation; integration with medical imaging systems.

title: Advanced Topics in Electric Energy Systems
description: Subjects of current interest such as computer methods, distribution and transmission systems, systems planning and economics, extra high voltage transmission, exotic power sources.

title: Power Quality
description: Description, analysis, modeling, and solution of difficulties relating to distortion of the waveshape in alternating-current power systems. Problems of voltage regulation. Focus on harmonics, noise, filtering, and communications interference in power systems.

title: Power Systems Operation and Control
description: Specific topics include economic dispatch, unit commitment, control of generation, power system security, and state estimation. Several analytical and computational techniques are used to solve different problems related to above mentioned topics.

title: Distribution System Engineering
description: Analysis, design and planning of electric distribution systems. Standard terminologies related to distribution systems are covered.

title: Power System Stability and Control
description: Introduction to power system stability and control. System device modeling and control issues are introduced for the purpose of studying various aspects of power system stability. Transient, small signal, and voltage stability topics are covered. Several stability analysis projects for multimachine systems are included.

title: Advanced Electrical Theory
description: For advanced study in specialized areas by M.S. students.

title: Solid State Electronic Devices
description: Introduction to quantum mechanics, crystal structures, and the semiconductor material properties. Diodes, bipolar transistors, and field-effect transistor structures. Analysis of second-order effects in transistors.

title: Graduate Seminar in Electrical and Computer Engineering
description: Presentation and discussion of current advances and research inelectrical and computer engineering. Required of all graduate students in electrical and computer engineering.

title: Research in Electrical Engineering
description: Special research problems in electrical engineering.

title: Master's Report
description: Topics selected with approval of major professor and department head.

title: Master's Thesis
description: Topics selected with approval of major professor and department head.

title: Advanced Topics in Control Theory
description: Study of advanced topics in optimal, time-varying, and stochastic control theory, or other recent developments in the control systems area.

title: Advanced Topics in Computer Engineering
description: Selected topics related to advanced computer hardware design, performance measurements, sequential machines, and/ or advanced computer architectures.

title: Advanced Topics in Communications
description: Selected topics related to the design and performance analysis of communication systems. Topics may include advanced modulation techniques, optimum receiver design, nonlinear channels, multipath analysis, diversity systems, and others.

title: Detection & Estimation Theory
description: Theoretical foundations of detection and estimation theories. Bayesian decision theory with applications to signal detection in discrete time; concept of sufficient statistic and minimum variance unbiased estimation; Bayesian estimation; best linear unbiased estimation; Kalman filtering and its variants; filtering, smoothing and prediction applications across various engineering fields.

title: Information Theory
description: Information as a measure of uncertainty, zero-memory and Markov sources, coding of information sources, channels and mutual information, reliable transmission via unreliable channels, error correcting codes.

title: Advanced Topics in Digital Signal Processing
description: Selected topics related to adaptive digital filtering techniques; special purpose hardware for digital filtering; two-dimensional signal processing and classification.

title: Advanced Digital Filtering
description: Advanced treatment of the theory of digital filtering and digital signal processing. Emphasis is on analysis of random signals.

title: Advanced Topics in Bioengineering
description: Study of complex physiological system simulation and analysis techniques, modern experimental and clinical electronic bioinstrumentation systems. Topics selected according to graduate student's interests.

title: Dissertation Research
description: Topics selected with approval of major professor and department head.

title: Introduction to Early Childhood Education
description: Examination of the diversity in contemporary early childhood roles and settings, awareness of professional competencies and standards of practice, and recognition of philosophical and historical foundations of the profession.

title: Problems in Early Childhood Education
description: Independent or small group study of early childhood development and education.

title: Early Childhood Development
description: Principles of growth and development of children from conception across the early childhood years, including familial, societal, and other ecological factors affecting young children's development.

title: Infant Toddler Development and Programs
description: Study of infants and toddlers from birth through 36 months including physical, cognitive, language, social, and emotional growth and development. Examination of best practices, responsive caregiving techniques, environments, infant/toddler learning approaches, health, safety, and licensing requirements.

title: Infant Observation Lab
description: Observation of the behavior and development of children from infancy through toddlerhood.

title: Pre-Professional Experiences in Early Childhood Unified
description: Observation and participation in diverse early childhood settings serving children with and without disabilities, aged birth through kindergarten.

title: Interaction Techniques with Young Children
description: A developmental approach to the acquisition of interaction techniques conducive to healthy emotional and self-concept growth in the child from birth through kindergarten.

title: Exceptional Development in Early Childhood
description: Exceptional development in early childhood (birth through kindergarten), including developmental delays, physical and sensory impairments, communication disorders, mental retardation, learning and behavioral disorders, and health related concerns: emphasis on IEPs and IFSPs and the family's role in the assessment/referral/intervention process.

title: Environments in Early Childhood
description: Planning, promoting, and maintaining physically and psychologically safe and healthy learning environments for young children. Fostering active, healthy lifestyles and enhanced quality of life for young children through understanding major concepts for nutrition and health education, human movement and physical activity.

title: Administering Early Childhood Programs
description: Acquaints students with the multi-faceted dimensions of the director's work and enables them to acquire the knowledge and develop the skills needed to be successful as an administrator of an early childhood program.

title: Language Development
description: Survey of the development of speech and language skills in children.

title: Professional Seminar in Early Childhood
description: Examination of programs for young children, including philosophical and theoretical foundations. Implementation and evaluation of program models and related issues and research.

title: Research and Inquiry in Early Childhood Education
description: Study and application of fundamental concepts, principles, and methods of educational research in early childhood education with focus on preparing students to become reflective practitioners of action research and critical consumers of research.

title: Teaching and Learning in Early Childhood Education
description: Application of developmental and educational theories to teaching and learning in early childhood education programs serving children aged birth through kindergarten, including children with special needs.

title: Developmental Curriculum in Early Childhood Education
description: The planning and application of developmental curriculum across the content areas to enhance the development and learning of young children.

title: Curriculum For Cognitive And Language Development/Young Children
Curriculum For Emotional, Social, And Physical Development Young Children
Foundations of Coaching Adults
description: Introduces coaching in early childhood settings.  Special attention is given to adult development and learning theory, mentoring and coaching models, reflective supervision practices, interpersonal skills and the unique needs of early childhood environments.

title: Reflective Practice: Infants and Toddlers
description: Application of developmental principles and evidenced-based practice to the diverse learning environments for infants and toddlers with and without special needs across content areas including the integration of the family and community and public policies that affect programs for young children.

title: Reflective Practice: Preschoolers and Kindergartners
description: Application of developmental principles and evidenced-based practice to the diverse learning environments for preschoolers and kindergarteners with and without special needs across content areas including the integration of the family and community and public policies that affect programs for young children.

title: Preschool Lab
description: Advanced application of principles and techniques that support the planning, Implementation, and evaluation of early childhood programs for young children.

title: Early Childhood Practicum I: Infants and Toddlers
description: Development and implementation of developmentally and functionally appropriate learning experiences, in a variety of formats, addressing content in the areas of early language and literacy, mathematics, science, social studies, the arts, and technology for infants and toddlers.

title: Early Childhood Practicum II: Preschoolers
description: Development and implementation of developmentally and functionally appropriate learning experiences, in a variety of formats, addressing content in the areas of early language and literacy, mathematics, science, social studies, the arts, and technology for the preschool child.

title: Early Childhood Practicum III: Kindergartners
description: Development and implementation of developmentally and functionally appropriate learning experiences, in a variety of formats, addressing content in the areas of early language and literacy, mathematics, science, social studies, the arts, and technology for the kindergarten child.

title: Early Childhood Practicum 4: Home Visiting
description: Development and implementation of developmentally and functionally appropriate experiences, within the context of an individualized family plan, addressing development and content in the areas of early language and literacy, mathematics, science, social studies, the arts, and technology for infants and toddlers in home based settings.  Admission to teacher education required.

title: Emergent Literacy
description: Exploration of literacy development from birth through kindergarten, including concepts relevant to pre-emergent, emergent, and developing literacy; procedures for designing developmentally and culturally appropriate experiences; factors impacting literacy acquisition and assessment; and intervention procedures for children at-risk for reading and writing problems.

title: Applied Learning in Early Childhood Programs
description: Practical experience working in an early childhood setting with emphasis on program planning and relations with staff and families.

title: Home, School, and Community Partnerships
description: Strategies to enhance home-school communication with specific focus on families as the primary decision-makers for young children, with or without special needs. Emphasis on the development of family focused and culturally sensitive collaborations involving parents, schools, and the community to support child development, learning, and well-being.

title: Student Teaching in Early Childhood Unified
description: Design, implementation, assessment of developmentally and individually appropriate evidence-based curriculum for children with and without disabilities birth through kindergarten.

title: Problems in Early Childhood Education
description: Independent study of relevant aspects of early childhood development and education.

title: Seminar in Early Childhood Education
description: Interpretation and evaluation of information on varied topics relating to early childhood education and development.

title: Topics in Early Childhood Education
description: Review of recent research and theory related to early childhood education and development.

title: Child Care: Components and Issues
description: Professional development and leadership; skills, services, and resources supporting quality early childhood care and education.

title: Challenging Behaviors in Early Childhood
description: The application of developmental principles and evidence-based practices to design and implement a positive learning environment for young children, including children with disabilities. Creating an environment that supports young children's social emotional competence, teaches social skills, and promotes self-regulation is emphasized.

title: Assessment of Young Children
description: Theory and practice of individual assessment of handicapped and normal children, infancy to age eight, including cognitive, language, fine and gross motor, social, and self-help skills. Focus on selection, administration, interpretation, and evaluation of screening and comprehensive evaluation instruments for assessment and individual program planning.

title: Early Childhood Program Administration
description: Professional practice and organizational management of early childhood care and education programs; financial and personnel management, program development, and community relations.

title: Play Facilitation
description: The emphasis of this course is the empirical study and practice of play as an educational, evaluative, and therapeutic intervention with young children.

title: Home Visiting Programs for Young Children and Families
description: Examination of the empirical literature that supports the provision of early intervention and education services to families with young children through a home visitation model; focus on programs that use home visitation; an understanding of the key principles in early intervention; and the development of skills necessary to implement a coaching model of service delivery to enhance family and child outcomes.

title: Child Development
description: Behavioral characteristics and developmental processes in childhood and adolescence. Analysis of developmental trends and issues in terms of research evidence and theoretical expectations.

title: Infant Behavior and Development
description: Study of the infant as a developing individual within the family; examination of the theories and research relevant to development from conception through the second year.

title: Theories of Human Development
description: Theories of development relating to physical, social, and psychological patterns of individuals' growth and interaction with the family and the community.

title: Advanced Program Development
description: Analysis of the process and application of child development theory to early childhood program planning.

title: Practicum in Early Childhood Education
description: Supervised experience in providing help and/or instruction in the several areas of human development and family studies presented in terms of the special interests of the students. Consent of practicum supervisor is required for each.

title: Practicum in Parent Education
description: Supervised experience in providing help and/or instruction in the several areas of human development and family studies presented in terms of the special interests of the students. Consent of practicum supervisor is required for each.

title: Readings in Early Childhood Education
description: Implications of research findings in preparation for professional work and research in early childhood education.

title: MS Research in Early Childhood Education
description: Individual research problems which may form the basis for the master's thesis or report.

title: Electronic And Computer Engineering Technology Seminar
Basic Electronics
description: A survey course designed to provide an overview of basic direct and alternating current circuits and an introduction to linear and digital electronics. Laboratory exercises reinforce circuit theory and provide skills in the use of common electronic instruments.

title: Circuits I
description: An introductory course in basic circuit theory emphasizing the analysis of passive circuit networks containing resistance, capacitance, and inductance operating in direct current conditions. Topics include equivalent circuits, network theorems, capacitance, RC-circuit response, inductance, RL-circuit response, and computer simulation.

title: Linear Circuit Applications
description: Analysis and design of analog circuits including differential amplifiers, oscillators, linear and switching power amplifiers, applications of operational amplifiers, advanced semiconductor devices, and heat sinks.

title: Semiconductor Electronics
description: An introductory course in electronic devices. Topics include PN-junction theory, diodes, transistors, transistor biasing, transistor modeling, operational amplifiers, voltage regulators, and field-effect transistors (FET). Additional topics include Miller's Theorem and Network Analysis

title: Digital Logic
description: Study of basic logic elements including gates, flip-flops, counters, and registers. Includes Boolean algebra, logic reduction methods, and digital logic applications. Emphasis on computer simulation and PLD implementation of logic circuits.

title: Topic in Electronic and Computer Engineering Technology
description: Provides an opportunity for faculty to present electronic and computer engineering technology topics.

title: Circuits II
description: Analysis of passive networks containing resistance, capacitance, and inductance operating in alternating current conditions. Includes sinusoidal waveforms, polar and rectangular complex algebra, inductive and capacitive reactance, impedance networks, power factor correction, resonance, magnetic circuits, and an introduction to three-phase power distribution. Calculus relationships between linear bilateral electrical components are also discussed.

title: Electric Power and Devices
description: Industrial applications of direct and alternating power, devices, and systems. Topics include electrical and electronic power devices, controllers, servomechanisms, and actuators; DC and AC motors and generators, motor speed control and drive systems; electrical power distribution, and industrial electronics applications.

title: Electronic Communication Systems
description: A survey of analog and digital communication techniques and systems including modulation, transmission line concepts, radio-wave propagation, antenna theory, and the effects of noise.

title: Industrial Controls
description: A study of electronic circuits and systems encountered in industrial environments. Topics include power control devices and applications, power system design, sensors, transducers, PLCs, computer-based data acquisition, and automatic control concepts.

title: Industrial Control Topics
description: Study of the applications of electronic circuits and systems in industrial environments. Topics include control systems and devices, control system modeling and simulation.

title: Electronic Manufacturing
description: A practical course in the details of electronic system design and fabrication. Topics include printed-circuit board design, layout, and fabrication; electronic-system design principles; 3D modeling, fabrication, packaging, and assembly techniques for electronic systems; and through-hole and surface-mount technologies.

title: Microprocessor Fundamentals
description: Concepts of microprocessor architecture, programming, and interfacing. Topics include assembly and C language programming, data conversion methods, and microprocessor-based system development tools.

title: Digital Circuits and Systems
description: Study of digital circuits and systems, i.e., systems controlled by microprocessors and microcontrollers. Students design and build digital circuits for the solution of engineering problems. Digital circuit design, and interfacing techniques are emphasized.

title: Microcontroller Applications
description: Examines the hardware and software of microcontroller controlled systems designed for a specific purpose (i.e. embedded systems). Students will learn how to interface microcontrollers to various sensors, actuators and communication devices as well as learn the programming associated with these devices.

title: Electromagnetic Applications
description: Emphasizes the physical understanding and visualization of Maxwell's Equations leading to the design of linear bilateral circuit components, waveguides and passive sensors for use in the radio-frequency (RF) regime.

title: Communication Circuits Design
description: An introduction to the theory and design of electronic circuits for communications emphasizing the implementation and analysis of common radio-frequency (RF) building blocks. Topics include s-parameters, the Smith chart, component behavior, RF test equipment, computer simulation, filter design, impedance matching, amplifiers, oscillators, mixers, and demodulators.

title: Telecommunication Systems
description: An introduction to data communications and a survey of modern communication systems. Topics include Fourier analysis, data encoding, data link control, fiber-optic systems, cellular systems, satellite systems, and the modern telephone system.

title: Signals and Systems
description: A study of various network topics including Laplace transforms, signal flow graph models, transfer functions, network response, and differential equations and linear approximations of physical systems. The theory of control systems and their applications are discussed.

title: Digital Systems and Computer Architecture
description: Development of advanced digital design techniques. Topics include VHDL-based design, simulation, and synthesis; testing and validation; system-level interfacing; and computer architecture.

title: Electronic Design I
description: Application of electronic principles and the design methodology to solving a significant design problem in a team context. Includes determining customer requirements, exploring and choosing design alternatives, scheduling, and project management. Significant milestones are the project's conceptual, preliminary, and critical design reviews, which require written and oral presentations.

title: Electronic Design II
description: A continuation of ECET 480. Includes the implementation, testing, and delivery of the project initiated in ECET 480 Electronic Design I. Significant milestones are the project prototype, design report, and final presentation.

title: Problems In Electronic And Computer Engineering Technology
Advanced Topics in Electronic and Computer Engr Technology
description: Provides an opportunity for faculty to present advanced electronic and computer engineering technology topics.

title: Contemporary Economic Issues
description: An introduction to economic analysis and thinking on current issues that our society as well as other nations face. Using essential tools of economics such as opportunity cost, marginal analysis, and demand and supply, we examine some contemporary issues in an issue-oriented manner, rather than technically oriented. For students who want to familiarize themselves with current national and international economic issues and concerns. Not intended to be taken by economics majors or minors.

title: Principles of Macroeconomics
description: Basic facts, principles, and problems of economics; determination of the level of output, employment, and the price level; the monetary and banking system; problems and policies of economic instability, inflation, and growth; principles of economic development; other economics systems.

title: Principles of Macroeconomics Honors
description: Basic facts, principles, and problems of economics; determination of the level of output, employment, and the price level; the monetary and banking system; problems and policies of economic instability, inflation, and growth; principles of economic development; other economics systems.

title: Principles of Microeconomics
description: Basic facts, principles, and problems of economics including study of the determination of prices; the determination of wages, rent, interest, and profit; theory of the firm; monopoly and government regulation; international economic relations.

title: Introductory Seminar In Industrial And Labor Relations
Honors Seminar in Economics
description: For sophomores in honors program-scheduled irregularly. Readings and discussions.

title: Sophomore/Junior Seminar in Economics
description: An introduction to economics as a science and a profession. The course introduces students to the skills and tools that make economics an attractive and enjoyable field as well as an overview of economic data and current debates.

title: Honors Project
description: Open only to Arts and Sciences students who are active members of the University Honors Program.

title: South Asian Civilizations
description: Interdisciplinary survey of the development of civilization in India, Pakistan, Sri Lanka, Bangladesh, and Afghanistan, including geography, history, religion, culture, language, literature, current economy, and significant social movements.

title: Introduction To The Civilization Of South Asia 2
The Japanese Economy
description: Analyzes Japan's growth, productivity change, income distribution, government policies, agriculture, industrial structure, labor relations education and technology, and international trade and finance. Emphases will be on U.S.-Japanese competition and comparisons.

title: Intermediate Macroeconomics
description: An examination of the behavior of the economy as a whole, including an analysis of the national income account, consumption, investment, money, interest, the price, level, the level of employment, monetary and fiscal policy, and economic growth.

title: Intermediate Microeconomics
description: An examination of the theories of consumer behavior and demand, and the theories of production, cost, and supply. The determination of product prices and output in various market structures, and an analysis of factor pricing. Introduction to welfare economics.

title: Intermediate Microeconomic Theory
description: A mathematical approach to intermediate microeconomics. Emphasis is placed on the use of optimization techniques to examine consumer demand, production and cost, behavior of the firm, market structure and welfare.

title: Human Resource Economics
description: An introduction to the economic forces influencing wage and employment determination, income differentials, unemployment, and the production and acquisition of human capital. Emphasis on public policy, labor unions, and other relevant institutions.

title: Sports Economics
description: An economic analysis of professional and amateur sports. Pay determination of professional athletes, monopsony power of owners, discrimination, the importance of leagues, competitive balance, antitrust issues, collective bargaining, the financing of sports stadiums, and current economic issues in sports.

title: Environmental Economics
description: Economics of environmental market failure and the efficient use of exhaustible and renewable resources. Topics include the application of markets and government policies to greenhouse warming, air and water pollution, and recycling.

title: Money and Banking
description: Nature, principles, and functions of money; development and operation of financial institutions in the American monetary system, with emphasis on processes, problems, and policies of commercial banks in the United States.

title: Fisc Op Govt
description: Designed for students who plan careers related to state or local government. Selected topics in state and local taxation and expenditure.

title: Comparative Economics
description: The transition by Russia, Ukraine, Eastern and Central Europe, and Central Asia to market economics; economic reform in China, India, and other countries; and Marxian critiques of capitalism.

title: Managerial Economics
description: Microeconomic topics applicable to understanding and analyzing firm behavior: optimization, demand, estimation, production, and cost theory. Applications to business problems.

title: Urban and Regional Economics
description: An examination of the determinants of the economic performance of urban and regional economies, including theory, problems, and policy.

title: Senior Seminar in Economics
description: Topics for class discussion include history of economic thought, research methods in economics, and current economic issues. Students will prepare and present papers written with faculty guidance.

title: Monetary, Credit, and Fiscal Policies
description: Advanced study of monetary, credit, and fiscal policies including the goals of aggregative economic policy, conflicts among goals, and measures to resolve conflicts; money markets; targets of central bank control; the relative strength of monetary and fiscal policies; rational expectations; hypothesis and policy ineffectiveness debate; terms structure of interest rates.

title: Problems in Economics
description: Individual study is offered in international trade, labor relations, money and banking, public finance, transportation, general economics.

title: Topics in Economics
description: Courses on special topics to be taught on demand.

title: Economic Applications of Game Theory and Strategic Behavior
description: Basic principles of game theory including Nash equilibria, repeated games, and strategy with incomplete information.

title: Python Coding forÂ Economics
description: Introduction to quantitative methods in economics. Works with Python programming language and covers topics such as using and coding functions, working with loops, object-oriented programming, understanding and building classes, data visualization, data management, and optimization.

title: Labor Economics
description: Economics of the labor market--theory and empirical evidence. Labor force composition and trends, labor supply, labor demand, human capital, wage differentials, migration, trade unions, and current issues.

title: Contemporary Labor Problems
description: Emphasis on current research and public policies dealing with such matters as full employment, poverty, discrimination, social security, unemployment insurance, health care, minimum wages, training, and education.

title: Introduction to Econometrics
description: An introduction to the analytical and quantitative methods used in economics. Applications to specific problems with an emphasis on computer analyses.

title: Principles of Transportation
description: Examines the transportation market from the shippers' point of view by examining the impact of transportation on business firm decisions such as location, markets, and prices. Also covers the costs, prices, and service characteristics of railroads, motor carriers, water carriers, oil pipelines and airlines. The role and impact of government in the transportation market is examined from both a promotion and regulation perspective.

title: Public Finance
description: Course seeks answers to questions such as: Which goods should be provided by the private sector and which by the public sector (government)? With what criteria are public expenditures evaluated? What is an equitable and efficient tax system? Who bears the tax burden? What aspects of existing taxes need reform?

title: Industrial Organization and Public Policy
description: An examination of measures and determinants of industrial concentration, and an analysis of market structure, conduct, and performance, and policies related to performance.

title: International Trade
description: Principles of international trade, determinants of the value of trade between any pair of countries, sources of gains from trade, main factors determining the pattern of trade, commercial policy, the political economy of trade and strategic trade policy.

title: Development Economics
description: Factors affecting the economic modernization of the less-developed countries. Emphasis on capital formation, human capital, investment allocation, technical progress, income distribution, population growth, and the international economics of development.

title: International Finance and Open Economy Macroeconomics
description: Introduction to international finance and open-economy macroeconomics, including the foreign exchange market, the balance of payments, international financial markets, and recent and ongoing financial crises. Covers core theoretical material, the course will examine current policy issues.

title: Economics Forecasting
description: Focus on forecasting macroeconomic variables such as sales activity, tax revenues and other important variables. Techniques of forecasting using econometrics.

title: Health Economics
description: This course applies the tools of microeconomics to the health-care sector. It analyzes the behavior of consumers and providers of medical care, the functioning of insurance markets, and the roles of government and the private sector. International comparisons are made.

title: Undergraduate Research Methods in Economics
description: Development of a research proposal and completion of a research project under the guidance of a faculty member. Instruction in research design, methods and data. The independent-work phase of the course will be structured with weekly targets for completing economic theory, data/case study description, methods description and analysis and interpretation.

title: Seminar in Economics
description: Seminars of special interest will be offered on demand.

title: Intermediate Macroeconomics
description: An examination of the behavior of the economy as a whole, including an analysis of the national income account, consumption, investment, money, interest, the price, level, the level of employment, monetary and fiscal policy, and economic growth.

title: History of Economic Thought
description: Development of economic ideas and doctrines and the relation of these to conditions existing when they were formulated.

title: Economic Analysis for Business
description: Microeconomic and macroeconomic theory with selected business applications. Theory of consumer and producer behavior and price determination in individual markets. Theory of aggregate economic behavior and the effects of fiscal and monetary policies.

title: Microeconomic Theory
description: Demand, cost, and production theories; price and output determination in different market structures; the theory of factor market pricing; an introduction to general equilibrium and welfare analysis.

title: Applied Econometrics
description: Emphasis on applied econometrics methods. Topics include simple and multiple linear regression as well as other econometric models used in economics. Diagnosis and remedies of potential problems with linear models and data, leading to quantitative answers to empirical questions.

title: Mathematical Economics
description: Application of mathematical tools of concrete problems in micro- and macro-economics; mathematical treatment of models of consumption, production, market equilibrium, and aggregate growth.

title: Topics in Monetary Theory
description: Emphasis on recent literature of monetary economics; Federal Reserve control of the money stock; the demand for money; money and economic activity; monetary targets and indicators.

title: Macroeconomic Theory I
description: An overview of macroeconomic theory including Classical, Keynesian, and Monetarist theory. An Introduction to modern analysis in macroeconomics with an emphasis on dynamic methods and general equilibrium analysis.

title: Advanced International Economics
description: Theoretical and policy issues related to the international monetary system, capital movements, exchange rate systems, the U.S. balance of payments, and trade of underdeveloped countries.

title: Econometrics I
description: Fundamentals of econometric analysis. The classical linear model, multi-collinearity, specification error, distributed lags, non-normal disturbances, dummy variables, generalized least squares, autocorrelation, heteroscedasticity. Applications to specific problems.

title: Public Sector Analysis
description: Conditions for economic efficiency in the public sector; public good production functions; nonmarket decision making; rationale for public sector growth; systems analysis, cost-benefit and related techniques of allocating public goods.

title: Managerial Economics
description: Economic analysis of production, cost, and demand functions. Application of economic models to managerial decision making.

title: Topics in International Trade and Development Economics
description: Covers key topics of current interest international trade and development economics, including new theories of firm-level comparative advantage and offshoring, outsourcing, and multinational activities.

title: Graduate Seminar in Economics
description: Course will provide seminars on specific topics in economics.

title: Problems in Economics
description: Advanced individual study is offered in selected subject matter.

title: Macroeconomic Theory II
description: A study of advanced topics in macroeconomic theory including general equilibrium analysis, economic growth, business cycle theory, and other developments in macroeconomic theory.

title: Topics in Macroeconomics
description: Exploration of seminal and current research in macroeconomic topics such as growth, business cycles, or public policy. Topics will be related to the research interests of the professor and students.

title: Macroeconomic Modelling
description: Examines current topics in macroeconomics with an emphasis on empirical modelling. The course will be structured to allow the student an in-depth look at influential articles in the literature through presentations and required summary papers, and apply similar skills in an assigned original research paper.

title: Labor Economics Seminar
description: A critical analysis of wage theories, collective bargaining, and unemployment problems.

title: Location of Economic Activities
description: An examination of the theory of location including central place theory, location of the individual producer, industrial location patterns, and urban land-use models. Also includes application of theoretical models to current urban problems.

title: Advanced Labor Economics
description: An examination of studies in labor supply, labor demand, immigration, and current topics in labor economics.

title: Econometrics II
description: Continuation of Econometrics I. Stochastic regressors, asymptotic results, instrumental variable estimation, systems of equations, maximum likelihood estimation, nonlinear least squares, and other miscellaneous topics in econometrics.

title: Time Series Econometrics
description: Discussion of recent advances in tome series econometrics. Economic forecasting, vector autoregressive modeling, unit root testing, cointegration, and other topics.

title: Microeconometrics
description: A survey of recent advances in microeconometrics, including panel data, treatment effects, and other topics, to provide students with the analytical tools used in economic research.

title: Advanced Microeconomic Theory I
description: An examination of demand, production, and cost theories; a discussion of duality theory and the application of the Le Chatelier principle; an analysis of price and output determination in different market structures.

title: Advanced Microeconomic Theory II
description: A study of advanced topics in economic theory, including general equilibrium theory, welfare economics, and risk and uncertainty.

title: Industrial Organization
description: Theory of industrial organization and its analytical techniques. Game theory; monopoly pricing; product and quality selection; vertical control; static and dynamic theories of oligopoly; product differentiation, price dispersion, and advertising; asymmetric information and strategic behavior.

title: Empirical Industrial Organization
description: Analysis of market outcomes resulting from behavior and strategy of economic agents in imperfectly competitive markets, using econometric methods. Topics may include:  models of consumer demand and methods of estimation; identifying and measuring market power and market effects of mergers; static models of entry and industry structure; single-agent dynamic structural models; dynamic oligopoly games; production, technology and industry structure; and other recent advances in empirical industrial organization.

title: Theory and Methods of Regional Economic Analysis
description: A consideration of differences in regional and urban growth; comparison of alternative growth theories; methods of analyzing regional economics such as input-output analysis, linear programming, industrial complex, and spatial interaction models.

title: International Trade Theory and Policy
description: Discussion of recent literature in trade theory and policy under imperfect competition. International trade in imperfectly competitive international markets, strategic trade policies, political economy of trade protectionism, welfare effects of trade liberalization and economic integration, and other issues.

title: Independent Study in Adult and Leadership
Principles Of Teaching Adults In Extension
International Education
description: Contemporary overview of the field of international education and an introduction to three of its parts. Comparative education, intercultural education, and development education.

title: Practicum in Adult Learning and Leadership
description: Related occupational or professional experiences in approved industry, school, Cooperative Extension Service, or similar agency setting under faculty supervision.

title: Women, Education, Leadership, and Work
description: Emphasizes the collective and individual educational needs of women in and out of the work force and the part that occupational/educational preparation contributes to their participation in the work force.

title: Adult Learners and Integrating Technology into Curriculum
description: This course includes an in-depth study of methods for integrating innovative technologies into the curriculum for adult learners. With an in-depth understanding of adult learners, students will analyze learning theories and appropriate technologies for relevance, effectiveness, and alignment with course curriculum.

title: Readings in Adult Learning and Leadership
description: Readings in research and application in specialized areas in education.

title: Introduction to Adult Learning and Leadership
description: A survey of adult learning and leadership field. Consideration given to articulation with other levels of education. Identification of changing needs within the field are reviewed.

title: Educational Gerontology
description: For both the practitioner and those interested in educational gerontology as a field of inquiry. It will examine education for and about aging, with particular reference to the role, needs, and ability of persons in the later years as learners.

title: Designing Classroom Instruction and Curriculum for Adult Learners
description: This course focuses on the systematic approach to instructional design for adult learners. A comprehensive discussion of all major components of curriculum design from instructional objectives, course sequencing, instructional strategies, formative assessments and summative evaluations will be included. A major focus will also be on transfer of learning.

title: Topics in Adult Learning and Leadership
description: Examination of current topic in area of specialization of faculty.

title: Characteristics of the Adult Learner
description: For teachers and administrators in adult and occupational programs who need a familiarity with the major characteristics of adulthood which affect the adult as a learner.

title: Hospital and Industry Adult Learning and Leadership
description: An introduction to principles, roles, organization, procedures, and problems of adult learning and leadership in hospitals, industry, and related agencies.

title: Problems in Adult Learning and Leadership
description: Independent study of specific problems in the areas of adult learning and leadership.

title: Introduction to Community Educational Development
description: A comprehensive review of factors related to community change and the role of educational programs in dealing with them. Emphasis is on educational and economic problem-solving approaches and change-implementing programs.

title: Foundations of Social Justice Education: Research, Theory, and Practice
description: This course focuses on the principles of social justice education that promote equitable, sustainable, and transformative change in various formal and non-formal educational settings across the lifespan. The course integrates theoretical and practical aspects of social justice in local, national and international settings such as K-12 formal education, higher education, adult education, community development, and family education, and agricultural education.

title: Reflective Practice in Social Justice Education
description: This course focuses on the application of principles of social justice education in informal and formal educational settings. Student-conceptualized and -implemented theory-to-practice social justice education projects serve as the capstone or culmination of the graduate certificate, with projects presenting powerful opportunities for students to foster social change and positively impact the communities in which they work.

title: Critical and Social Issues in Adult Learning
description: Designed for adult learning and students and college or university personnel in sociology and related fields dealing with adults, this course focuses on the economic historical and sociocultural context in which learning takes place. Included is an in-depth examination of the relationship between adult learning and culture.

title: Principles of Teaching Adults
description: This course will explore the theory and practice of teaching adults. Collaborative and active learning techniques will be emphasized in face-to-face and online environments. Students will examine the strengths, weaknesses, and appropriate application of a variety of methods to different adult education environments.

title: International Adult Education and Literacy
description: This course provides an introduction to the foundations of global, political, social-economic and educational issues facing international adult education and literacy students for English as a Second Language learners.

title: Teaching Online in Adult Learning
description: This course examines online adult learning theories, online pedagogies, current research, and trends in e-education for the workplace and higher education.

title: Theory and Practice of Continuing Education
description: Specific instruction on facilitating continuing education programs; emphasis on serving the institution, part-time students, community, and other interests.

title: Program Planning in Adult Learning and Leadership
description: An examination of the basic situations in which adult education occurs and fundamental steps by which learning is made more effective in those situations.

title: Teaching and Leading Multigenerations of Adults
description: Examines the traits of generations as defined as age cohort that shares unique formative years. The context of teaching and leading multigenerations will be addressed. Students will learn how to apply concepts to foster communication and collaboration of all generations present.

title: Interpersonal and intrapersonal Dynamics in Adult Learning and Leadership
description: This course explores various psychological and sociological factors that impact leadership. Through examining topics like verbal and nonverbal communication, active listening, learning and presentation styles, emotional intelligence, conflict, and motivation, students gain a deeper understanding of how these factors affect their personal leadership styles and impact adults they are leading.

title: Digital Leadership Development in Adult Education
description: Provides an essential introduction into digital leadership development (DLD) in adult education. Helps students acquire the skills and competencies required of adult educational leaders and administrators to make well-grounded decisions in providing leadership on planning and designing digital learning environments and experiences. Also discusses social, ethical, and human issues related to digital leadership development and adult learners.

title: Leading Adults in a Globalized and Diverse World
description: This course provides an introduction to the foundations of adult education leadership in the context of managing a culturally diverse workforce. Concepts of globalization as well as cross- cultural and international environments as they relate to adult education leadership are emphasized through theory to practice projects and research.

title: Developing Teams and Adult Leaders
description: This course will examine how teams and leaders can be developed using adult learning and leadership theories and principles.

title: Group Dynamics in Adult Learning and Leadership
description: This course focuses on group and team behavior and processes in the adult education environment. Various factors that impact group behavior, processes, and effectiveness will be examined and participants will learn skills needed to more effectively manage and facilitate groups and teams of adults to achieve organizational objectives, accomplish tasks, and fulfill individual members' needs.

title: Quality Programs and Staff Management in Adult Learning and Leadership
description: Utilizes the use of adult education theories of program administration to enhance student understanding of staff and team dynamics.  Focuses on developing interpersonal skills so that students can be more effective leaders, problem solvers, motivators, coaches, and change managers.

title: Volunteer Leadership Education
description: Introduce students to managing and leading volunteers using an adult model. Volunteers are very similar to employees both have to be managed, coached and trained.

title: Experiential Learning and Leadership Dynamics
description: Framed in the theory of experiential learning, students will apply theories and principles learned throughout the leadership dynamics program to a professional problem/setting. Students will present their experiences to a panel of academic and professional practitioners.

title: Social Media and Adults in the 21st Century
description: This course addresses the most popular social media and helps students understand the promise and challenge that social media has brought to 21st century working adults. Example topics include but are not limited to Social Media and adult learning, Social Media in the workforce, and new technology literacy in 21st century.

title: Adult Learning and Motivation
description: Core course of adult learning perspectives and motivation for learning.

title: Self-Directed Learning
description: Self-directed or independent learning is an important aspect of adult education because it represents a significant portion of all learning by adults. The concept was instrumental in distinguishing the fields of adult education. During this course, students will explore self-directed learning from both an academic and a personal perspective. Topics include its history and development, its research base, areas of current interest and implications for adult education practice.

title: The Historical and Contemporary Community College
description: Focus on developing an understanding of historical and contemporary issues that have shaped and continue to impact community colleges.  Students will develop and improve skills that are outlined in the leadership competencies published by the American Association of Community Colleges.

title: Field Study: The Historical and Contemporary Community College
description: Field studies exposes students to a systematic approach to identify and analyze historical issues and issues facing contemporary community college leaders.  The implications of issues for community colleges are to be considered from a leadership perspective.

title: Access, Equity, and Success
description: Students will explore the assets, experiences, and institutional challenges of community college students based upon personal characteristics such as race/ethnicity, socioeconomic status, age, gender, sexuality, disability, military status, and religion; and the national ""best-practice"" strategies currently being used to increase enrollment, persistence, and program completion for these diverse student groups.

title: Field Study: Access, Equity, and Success
description: Provide an opportunity for students to reflect about challenges in changing community college cultures and structures from a traditional student-deficit model to a student-asset model accounting for the increasing diversity among community college students.

title: Aligning Vision, Planning and Resources
description: Study ways to understand, work with, and improve upon the financial model of community colleges and will help develop skills needed to do so.

title: Field Study: Vision, Planning and Resources
description: Students will identify and analyze financial issues being faced by community colleges throughout the United States. Students will explore methods to analyze and develop viable financial solutions to address the identified issues. The legacy mission of community colleges has grown in tandem with expectations. Yet public funding of most community colleges has decreased, is less predictable, and has not kept pace with enrollment growth. These pressures compel fresh thinking about mission, priorities, resource generation, and cost containment.

title: Effective Leadership and Theory
description: Students will be introduced to leadership issues in higher education through individual, interpersonal, team, and leadership theory and practical applications. Acentral premise of this course is to examine effective leadership practices and theories as lenses for examining leadership issues and comparing and contrasting practical applications in community colleges and competencies by their leaders while considering the terrain in which they function.

title: Field Study Effective Leadership
description: Students will analyze community college cases studies and leadership issues and make recommendations as to how best meet the challenges described. Students will differentiate among leader competencies and lenses and assess their potential relevance to addressing community college issues and priorities. Students will also examine issues impacting colleges and implications for effective leadership development.

title: Effective Leadership Institute
description: Students will travel to urban and rural community colleges and districts to meet with their respective chancellors, presidents, and leadership teams. Students will interact with board of trustee members, state educational association leaders, and other national community and technical college leaders.

title: Innovative Higher Education Programs for Adults
description: Designed to provide a conceptual understanding of innovative systems and structures currently serving higher education needs of adult learners. The course will provide a historical basis for the development of these programs as well as future trends. Included will be discussions of corporate colleges, external degree programs and distance/online learning.

title: Fostering Desired Culture: Fundamentals & Strategies for Org Development
description: Focus on helping students to create organizational structures designed to thrive by fostering entrepreneurialism and innovation throughout the organization.  This course will also focus on consideration of inclusion and equity in organizational design and development.

title: Field Study Organizational Development
description: Students will engage with community college leaders throughout the country concerning contemporary organizational challenges.  Using research, students analyze a community college situation and make recommendations to the leader as to how best address the contemporary organizational challenges.

title: Creating a Culture of Evidence and Inquiry
description: Students will explore how to develop and foster of cultures of evidence and inquiry from enrollment to outcomes. Other important topics include development and use of actionable information to make decisions and measure effectiveness, and  robust common measures to benchmark and improve institutional performance

title: Field Study Enrollment to Outcomes
description: Designed to enable participants to apply the principles of evaluation to developing a program evaluation for a student success initiative.

title: Principles of Workplace Learning
description: This course provides an overview of various aspects of workplace learning initiatives as found in the related professional literature and from various practice-related venues. Major topics include evolution of the process of workplace learning and the profession of training; assessing and analyzing workplace learning needs; designing and delivering workplace learning programs; and measuring and evaluating the impact of workplace learning initiatives.

title: Adult Learning,Training, and Development in Organizations
description: Examines through adult education principles and techniques, training and development in relation to adult learning and as one of the central functions of human resource development (HRD). The focus of the course will be on expanding the students’ knowledge related to organizational learning and learning organizations from educational principles; training and development concepts, frameworks, methods, and processes; and planning data-informed learning, training, and development programs, with a particular focus on adult education context.

title: Managing Change in Adult Learning and Leadership
