description: Introduces students to the process, models, and theories of change with the goal of increasing students’ effectiveness in dealing with multiple aspects of personal and organizational change, inclusive of understanding conditions that may require change, increasing awareness of multiple ways that organizations change, understanding the role of change agents, as well as understanding and managing resistance to change.

title: Program Evaluation in Adult Education Learning and Leadership
description: This course will provide an overview of various methods and strategies for evaluating educational programs for adults. Students will have the opportunity to examine the literature that is related to the program evaluation process in adult education settings. Opportunities to study the manner in which the process of program evalution is conducted in modern programs for adults in governmental, educational, and private and public sector organizations will also be provided.

title: The Role, Organization, and Function of Community Colleges
Community/Junior College Curriculum
description: Evaluation of community college curricula; reasons for revision, aims and objectives. Designed to familiarize student with the entire curricular offerings of the comprehensive community college.

title: Significant Literature of Adult Learning
description: A seminar studying the significant literature of adult learning from a current and historic perspective.

title: Introduction to Education Research
description: Introduce students to educational research methods, paying particular attention to the ways in which research can be used to understand and improve daily education practice, particularly in higher education. Key differences between qualitative, quantitative, mixed methods, and action research designs are highlighted.

title: Field Study: Education Research
description: The purpose of this Field Study in introductory educational research methods is to enable participants to apply concepts learned in EDACE 882 Introduction to Educational Research to critiquing various journal articles reporting primary research and further developing their understanding of research questions and various research methodologies, as well as, to provide opportunities to be consumers of research.

title: Seminars in Adult Learning and Leadership
description: These seminars will consider research and professional development on the special interests of the students in the several fields of education represented.

title: Foundations of Adult Learning and Leadership
description: A study of adult learning and leadership historical perspectives, contemporary institutions and programs, teaching-learning process, administrative practices, and conceptual roles.

title: Educational Value Choices
description: Students explore the prevalence of value choices confronting educational leaders in a community college setting, the influence personal values have on educational value choices, and the how educational leaders can maintain integrity in navigating the choices.

title: Field Study Ed Value Choices: Access, Equity, and Success
description: Focuses on the practical application of leadership integrity in approaching educational value choices within a community college setting.

title: Policy Formation and Public Process
description: Designed to provide students with a thorough background to the principal public policy issues that are impacting American community college education.  The background and analysis will be viewed through a specific theory of public policy and decision making as a means of structure.

title: Field Study Policy and Public Process
description: The opportunity to: (1) examine the theory of public policy and political decision making; and (2) work in-depth on a chosen issue of public policy related to community college education

title: Effective Governance and Leadership
description: Investigate the contributory roles of all employees and employee structures within the framework of college governance.

title: Field Study Governance and Leadership
description: Analyze identified problems in college governance; identify the role and responsibilities of key players in the development and implementation of policies and strategies; develop both detailed and wide-ranging response strategies to identified problems; and, implement a shared collective vision of problem solution.

title: Leadership for Transformation
description: Explore change and transformation theory and practice in contemporary community colleges. A widely-used change process will be used as an anchor from which to examine in depth real-world transformation efforts underway in the community college.

title: Field Study Leadership for Transformation
description: This field experience exposes students to change process, transformation theory and practice in contemporary community colleges. Students will meet with current community college leaders who will address the change process, transformation, vision and strategy, champions, innovation, communication and celebration of successes in a community college setting.

title: Designing a Comprehensive Plan for Success
description: Designed to review and incorporate the tenets of a leadership framework for designing a desired transformation in a community college or district.  Upon completion of this course, students will be able to identify, describe, and design the key components for planning, implementing, and evaluating an institutional transformation initiative to address a complex issue relevant to the college/district and the communities, state, or region it serves.

title: Field Study Plan for Success
description: Provide an opportunity for students to reflect about challenges in introducing and institutionalizing desired culture change and effective best practices to develop the envisioned future. Students will identify and articulate leadership and organizational skills necessary for transformational work.

title: Implementing Leadership Competencies
description: Students will identify the major competencies necessary to become a more effective and efficient leader and how these competencies align with those identified by the American Association of Community College Leaders (AACC). The course includes practical application of leadership, completing a self-assessment of core leadership competencies, and developing a personal leadership growth plan. Also, students will work in teams to develop a leadership institute that can be used in a community college setting.

title: Field Study (Institute) Leadership Competencies
description: Provides students with the opportunity to participate daily in discussions, develop a leadership institute, engage in reflection activities, and participate in a process for effective interviewing.

title: Organization and Administration of Adult Learning and Leadership
description: A critical study of organizational procedures and administrative practices as related to the implementation and maintenance of effective program for adults.

title: Adult Development and Learning
description: Study all pertinent adult development theories.

title: Introduction to Doctoral Studies
description: Provides an overview of the course sequence and dissertation process. The objective is to start students preparing for their dissertation early so they can finish in a timely manner. This course also addresses work-life balance between being a leader, a student, and having a family and personal life outside of work.

title: Dissertation Development
description: Introduction to the essential organizational and writing skills required for completing a dissertation. Upon completion of this course, students will be able to identify and describe the key steps in developing and writing the dissertation proposal and dissertation.

title: Field Study Dissertation Development I
description: Review the main components of the dissertation. Students will select the research topic under investigation and develop an argument that demonstrates the need for the study.

title: Field Study: Dissertation Development II
description: Designed to provide an overview of the writing and organization skills necessary for the completion of a literature review and research methodology germane to writing a dissertation. Upon completion of this the course, students will be able to design and conduct a literature review and research methodology that addresses the research topic under investigation.

title: Scholarly Writing
description: Enables students to develop the writing skills they need to become successful writers in their academic and post-academic careers. Course topics focus on students' disciplines and include annotated bibliographies, critiques, and literature reviews.

title: The Dissertation Process
description: Dissertation proposal development is one of the major challenges encountered toward earning a doctoral degree. This course outlines the basic components of a dissertation study with a focus on the proposal the dissertation's first three chapters. It then focuses on developing and writing chapter one. It is important that each student has a general idea for his or her dissertation research topic prior to the class start date. In addition to working with the course instructor, each student is expected to work closely with their major advisor.

title: Advanced Seminars in Adult Learning and Leadership
description: These seminars will critically consider recent research in the designated fields. The emphasis will be upon individual studies and small group interaction. Enrollment is restricted to those students who have been admitted to the doctoral program in education and who have completed substantial amounts of graduate study in the designated fields.

title: Internship in Adult Learning and Leadership
description: Field experiences in the development of programs in cooperating schools and educational or related agencies under the supervision of College of Education graduate faculty member.

title: Educational Implications Of Growth And Development
Interaction And Guidance For The Paraprofessional
Exceptional Student In The Secondary School
The Exceptional Child In The Regular Classroom
Remediation Education For The Emotionally Disturbed
Mental Hygiene In The School And Community
description: Mental Hygiene In The School And Community

title: The Exceptional Child In The Regular Classroom
Remediation Education For The Emotionally Disturbed
Introduction To Education Of The Gifted
Curriculum Development For The Mentally Retarded
Practicum In Education Of Exceptional Children
The Impact Of College On Students
description: The Impact Of College On Students

title: History And Philosophy Of Higher Education
Psychological Bases Of Educational Thought & Practice
Research Methods And Treatment Of Data
Introduction To Education Of The Gifted
Organization And Administration Of The Guidance Serv Program
Psychological Bases Of Educational Thought & Practice
Internship In Educational Administration And Foundations
Field Experiences In Home Economics Education
Teaching Participation In The Secondary School/Prof Dev Sem
Principles Of Teaching Adults In Extension
Principles And Philosophy Of Vocational Education
Practica In Business And Office Occupations
Practica In Home Economics Related Occupations
Adminisrtration And Supervision Of Vocational Education
Organization Problems In Teaching Farm Mechanics
Principles Of Teaching Adults In Extension
Practica In Business And Office Occupations
Practica In Home Economics Related Occupations
Problems In Adult And Occupational Education
In-Service Educucation For Beginning Home Economics Teachers
Young Farmer And Adult Farmer Education In Agriculture
Theory And Practice Of Continuing Education
Curruculum Development In Vocational Home Economics
Research in Organization and Presentation of Home Economics
Organization And Administration Of Adult Education
Organization And Administrtion Of Occupational Education
Advanced Seminars In Adult & Occupational Education
Research In Adult And Occupational Education
Healthy and Safe College Life I
Healthy and Safe College Life II
Healthful and Safe College Life
description: Addresses pertinent health education topics that impact college students and explores the effect of lifestyle choices on academic achievement. Topics include sexual health, mental health, nutrition and fitness, alcohol/drugs/tobacco, injury and illness prevention education.

title: Supplemental Instruction
description: Peer-facilitated supplemental instruction that supports learning in affiliated course. Practice and development of study skills, learning strategies, and specific learning goals.

title: Study Skills and Strategies
description: Assists first-year students in developing effective study skills, collaboration skills, and multicultural competencies. Prepares students for the independent learning and decision-making skills needed for their sophomore year and beyond.

title: Foundations of College Student Success
description: Introduction to academic strategies that promote college student success. Topics include academic skills, including communication and critical thinking, time management, academic and career planning and goal setting, and community building.

title: Academic and Career Decisions
description: Addresses general principles of academic and career choice through lectures, class discussions, and individual research. Topics include decision-making models and principles; exploration of interests, abilities, and values through career assessments; and academic and career investigation using interactive software, library materials, online resources, and experiential learning opportunities.

title: Development and Integrity
description: Provides a general overview of ethics and ethical decision making in academia and professional life.  It is designed to help students better understand issues about academic misconduct, as well as several approaches to thinking about and resolving ethical dilemmas.

title: Career and Life Planning
description: Applies theory research concerning assessment interest and career choice-making to individual's planning and decision-making. Focuses on increasing understanding of the complexities of the world of work and on skills of integrating such understanding with each person's planning experience, characteristics, motives, and values in the career exploration process. Reviews resume writing, interviewing skills, and job search techniques.

title: Leadership Training Seminar
description: General principles of leadership as applied to small groups. Study of the role of the leader, group processes and interaction, defining group goals, and techniques of observation. Workshop and supervision in small group leadership.

title: Educational Implications Of Growth And Development
Interaction and Guidance for the Paraprofessional
description: Application of a systematic approach to interaction skills in a paraprofessional helping relationship. Includes background knowledge of listening skills and practice in emitting skills which influence interaction quality.

title: Peer Health Education and Leadership
description: This course is intended as an academic exploration of social health and wellness concerns pertinent to the college student population. Using the principles of public health, peer educators will explore the importance of health-promoting behaviors in the prevention of disease. Students will examine social factors that may influence behaviors between their peers and obtain background knowledge in listening and communication skills.

title: Principles of Assessment
description: Principles of development, administration, evaluation, and constructive instructional use of paper-pencil, product, and performance assessments. Focus on norm-and criterion-referenced uses of teacher-made and published measures as an integral part of effective decision making in education.

title: Readings in Counseling and Educational Psychology
description: Readings in research and application in specialized areas in education.

title: Topics in Counseling and Educational Psychology
description: Examination of current topic in specialization of faculty.

title: Problems in Counseling and Educational Psychology
description: Selected students are permitted to secure specialized training appropriate to the needs of the individual. The student's project may involve intensive library investigation in a special field or the collection and analysis of data pertinent to a given problem. All work is done independently under the direction of a faculty member. As many conferences are held as necessary to assure successful completion of a project and nbsp.

title: Stress Management for Teachers,Counselors, & Administrators
description: Systematic training in stress-management strategies and techniques for the professional educator and for use in classroom and counseling settings. Includes knowledge of self-directed and instrumental techniques, psychophysiology of stress, issues in stress management, and role of teacher and counselor in delivering stress-management training.

title: The Impact Of College On Students
History and Philosophy of Higher Education
description: History and development of higher education with a study of the philosophy, objectives, and functions of various types of institutions.

title: Using Tests in Counseling
description: Focus on the use of tests as an integral part of counseling. Emphasizes interpretation of scores, issues of psychological and educational measurement, and selection and evaluation of instruments.

title: Research Methods in Education
description: This course is designed to provide students with an overview of quantitative and qualitative research methods in education and to equip them with the knowledge to be competent consumers and producers of educational research.

title: Statistical Methods in Education
description: An introductory yet comprehensive survey of common statistical analyses encountered in educational research. Computer oriented.

title: Principles of Student Affairs Administration
description: Principles of practice, current professional issues, and future trends in college and university student affairs administration

title: Survey Research
description: Evaluation, interpretation, use, and production of survey research in education.

title: Fundamentals of Program Evaluation
description: Theory, approaches, and fundamental skills for planning and implementing program evaluation; focus on research (and evaluation) designs and methods; includes evaluation professional standards and ethics.

title: Counseling Adolescents
description: This is a foundation course for secondary school counselors and addresses issues relevant to secondary school counseling programs.

title: Counseling Theory
description: Theories, methods, and problems in counseling, relating the counseling process to dynamics of human behavior.

title: Development Across the Lifespan for School Counselors
description: Advanced studies in physical, intellectual, emotional, social, and personality development across the lifespan with the focus on the importance of these factors to the educational process.

title: Student Success and Learning in Higher Education
description: The purpose of this course is to provide an in-depth knowledge of the factors and circumstances that lead to college student success and learning in higher education. The most recent research in the field will be reviewed, meaning that the topics receiving the most attention via research will form the basis of the content for the course.

title: Learning Principles
description: Exploration of learning theories and an overview of research on factors that enhance academic performance, including psychosocial variables, learning styles, and learning strategies. The course covers strategies for enhancing student academic performance, ranging from assessment to implementation.

title: Diversity in Higher Education
description: Addresses diversity in higher education administration and practice. Examples of topics examined in the context of diversity include recruitment and retention, program development, campus climate, and multicultural student organizations.

title: Current Issues in Higher Education
description: Current issues in higher education are examined. Students learn to identify current issues and how professional administrators effectively address these issues. Individual topics or issues that are part of the larger campus climate are studied.

title: Counseling Techniques
description: Practice basic helping skills common to the counseling profession using live supervision and videotape/transcription. Additional skills developed include problem conceptualization, goal setting, and selecting theoretically appropriate counseling interventions.

title: Foundations of Academic Advising
description: Examines the foundations of academic advising as essential components of student success and retention in higher education. Topics include developmental advising; research on academic advising; models and delivery systems; advising skills, including diverse populations; and evaluation, assessment, and reward systems for advisors and advising programs.

title: Interpersonal Relations for Academic Advising
description: This course focuses on developmental communications/interpersonal relations skills for academic advisors. Topics to be addressed include influences on the helping process, such as personal characteristics, verbal and nonverbal responses and behaviors, and ethical considerations.

title: Individualized Learning Experience in Higher Education
description: The focus of this course is for students to gain a valuable experience in the summer to enhance the application of classroom learning to practical experience. Students can choose from three options to meet course requirements: complete a summer internship (e.g., NASPA, ACUHO-I, NODA, other institution), participate in a rotational program with Kansas State's Division of Student Life, or develop a special project with a faculty member (e.g., curriculum development, research activity) that results in a final product. The format of this course will allow the sharing of information with each other so that all may learn from others' experiences regardless of location.

title: Student Development Theory
description: Study of the American college student and how he/she is influenced by institutional policies, practices, and other environmental variables. Special attention will be given to contemporary student development theory and research.

title: Assessment of Academic Advising
description: The role of quality academic advising is key to student success, graduation, and completion as identified in the research and literature on the undergraduate student experience. However, the quality and the effectiveness of the academic advising experiences must be assessed. This course will focus on a review of the foundations of academic advising, the key terms and processes of assessment, and the strategies and processes used to assess academic advising.

title: Multicultural Aspects of Academic Advising
description: This course introduces students to the various cultural, ethnic, racial, and linguistic issues associated with advising practice.

title: Career Development School Counselors
description: Addresses the knowledge and competencies necessary for school counselors to use education, career, and labor market information resources, and career guidance and counseling techniques, methods, and technology in developing programs, services, and activities to meet the career development needs of students.

title: College Student Athletes
description: Study of college student athletes. Topics include the role of intercollegiate athletics, pressures and challenges on student athletes, student development theory, life skills programming, National Collegiate Athletic Association policy, diversity, and gender issues.

title: Professional Counseling Orientation and Ethical Practice
description: Designed to provide an overview of ethics and legal issues in professional counseling. To understand different levels of analysis in evaluating ethical dilemmas and how to serve the client's needs while applying and implementing appropriate legal and ethical guidelines. Effective documentation, consultation, self-care, counselor transparency, and client participation to safeguard against the threat of litigation.

title: Counseling Children
description: The nature and philosophy of counseling in the elementary school; the function of specialized child appraisal and counseling techniques in the unique interrelationships of the professional school counselor and the teacher in the team approach to elementary school counseling.

title: Counseling Program Management
description: This course addresses the issues, knowledge, and competencies relevant to planning, implementing, and evaluating comprehensive school counseling programs to meet the needs of all students.

title: Group Processes
description: Designed to acquaint students with group procedures as basic tools in counseling, guidance, and other education services.

title: Trauma and Crisis Counseling
description: Study of children and adults and the educational, psychological, social, and career development issues they face as a result of trauma and post-traumatic stress. Particular emphasis will be given to counseling theories and best practices for working with students and clients experiencing these issues.

title: Management of Counseling Services for Adults
Trends in Career Development
description: This course addresses the role of work in people's lives across the life span, with an emphasis on the application of career development theories, research, practice, and resources to higher education. Topics are presented to help professionals in higher education facilitate students' academic and career development, planning, and decision-making within the context of the changing world of work.

title: Current Issues in Intercollegiate Athletics
description: Examines policies and procedures of the NCAA, roles and responsibilities within a department of intercollegiate athletics the historical relationship between intercollegiate athletics and higher education institutions, the creation of the NCAA and its policies, roles and responsibilities within a department of intercollegiate athletics, and relevant research. Topics include student-athlete health and well-being, marketing, fundraising, external relations, sports information, coaching, and academic support.

title: Counseling Mental Health Disorders
description: An introduction to mental health disorders for counselors. Topics include assessment of mental health symptomology, using a diagnostic system to understand mental health issues, counseling strategies for working with individuals with diagnosed mental health issues, the impact of common medications used for mental health disorders, techniques for differentiating disordered and non-disordered behaviors, and promotion of mental wellness.

title: Leadership, Advocacy, and Consultation for Counselors
description: This course acquaints students with the major models of consultation that may be used by counselors for intervention with individuals and organizations. Techniques, issues and ethical considerations are also addressed.

title: Administration of College Student Personnel Services
description: Planning, budgeting, personnel supervision and evaluation, office management, administrative use of computers, program evaluation and related applications in the primary college student personnel services.

title: Practicum in Counseling
description: This is a developmental counseling skills course addressing influences on the helping process such as personal characteristics, theoretical orientation, verbal and nonverbal behaviors, and ethical considerations. Includes research applications, case conceptualization, evaluation, simulation, and supervised individual and group practice in the field.

title: Practicum in Higher Education
description: Supervised practical experience in a higher education setting.

title: Seminar in Guidance Services
description: Intensive discussion of a problem of current professional interest based on study of pertinent original literature.

title: Counseling Internship
description: This supervised internship includes activities that a regularly employed school counselor would be expected to perform. The student will engage in individual counseling, group work, developmental classroom guidance, use within an ethical framework and a sensitivity to individual characteristics.

title: Psychological Bases Of Educational Thought & Practice
Experimental Design in Educational Research
description: Philosophy, planning, and evaluation of research in education. Experimental designs appropriate for educational research with special emphasis on multivariate procedures. Computer oriented.

title: Higher Education Law
description: This course will provide students with an overview of legal issues in higher education, with an emphasis on legal developments and trends in student affairs administration in particular.

title: Theories of Vocational Counseling
description: A historical and contemporary analysis of systems and theories of vocational psychology and their implications for use in the counseling setting.

title: Higher Education Finance
description: The purpose of this course is to provide an overview of finance in higher education. Current challenges related to public funding of higher education, from both the federal and state government, will be addressed in this course. Additional focus will be devoted to the structure and function of financial aid systems as well as to options for the reform of those systems.

title: Enrollment Management in Higher Education
description: Examines enrollment management theories, concepts, and research and the application of these to higher education administration. Students demonstrate enrollment management strategies through the development of a comprehensive enrollment management plan for use in a college or university setting.

title: Higher Education Administration
description: Administration theory applied to the organization and administration of colleges and universities; special reference to structure, governing boards, administrative roles, decision-making, and analysis of selected problems.

title: Approaches to Academic Advising: Linking Theory, Research, and Practice
description: This is an advanced study of foundational and emerging academic advising approaches. It aims to deepen student knowledge of the interdisciplinary scholarship and theory that underpin these approaches. Approaches will be from critical perspectives including applicability with different student groups and at different institutions; an evaluation of outcome research; and an exploration of assessment strategies.

title: Ethical Issues and Practice in Academic Advising
description: This course explores ethical theories and principles, moral development frameworks, and value issues confronting academic advising leadership. It is based on the premise that all advising professionals are ethicists that make decisions every day. The course incorporates a comprehensive, interdisciplinary approach to ethical theories and practice in academic advising leadership.

title: Research in Academic Advising
description: This course examines specific applications of theoretical, quantitative, qualitative, and mixed research methods in academic advising. The course provides a framework for the development of a research proposal directly related to critical questions in the field.

title: Administration of Academic Advising
description: Examines and applies relevant theories, concepts, and models of academic advising, as well as issues and other factors related to the administration of academic advising in higher education.

title: Advanced Student Development Theory
description: In-depth examination of the major young adult and adult development models and their implications within the context of student affairs in higher education.

title: Multicultural Counseling
description: Adaptations of generic counseling skills to meet the needs of diverse populations.

title: Multicultural and Social Justice Issues in Counselor Education
description: An examination of multicultural and social justice issues related to the practice of counselor education and supervision. Emphasis on evidence-based counseling practices for a diverse and pluralistic population.

title: Legal and Ethical Issues in Counseling
description: Examination of ethical standards developed by professional organizations of counselors. Current interpretations of standards and applications are developed through case studies, essays, reading, and literature review.

title: Teaching and Learning in Counselor Education
description: This course focuses on teaching and pedagogy of counselor education to improve delivery, teaching, and assessment of learning across modalities. This course focuses on understanding how learning works to effectively engage and teach counselors.

title: Advanced Group Counseling
description: The examination of selected group counseling theories and their relevance for the practice of group counseling in a variety of settings.

title: Practicum In Group Counseling
description: Supervised group counseling experience in a variety of settings.

title: Leadership and Advocacy in Counselor Education and Supervision
description: An examination of leadership and advocacy issues related to the practices of counseling, counselor education, and counselor supervision. Emphasis on the development of ethical and culturally relevant leadership and advocacy practices.

title: Appraisal and Psychopathology
description: Advanced application of appraisal models and methods for use with clientele. Specific personality tests and classification systems will be addressed.

title: Advanced Counseling Practices
description: Intense supervised practice in counseling. Particular emphasis will be given to the development of skills for intervention into human problems and time-limited case management.

title: Advanced Counseling Theory
description: Reading and analysis of primary sources in major counseling theories. Written reaction papers, presentations, discussion, and development of a major paper on a personal theory.

title: Advanced Seminar in Counseling and Educational Psychology
description: These seminars will critically consider recent research in the designated fields. The emphasis will be upon individual studies and small group interaction.. Enrollment is restricted to those students who have been admitted to the doctoral program in education and who have completed substantial amounts of graduate study in the designated fields.

title: Counseling Supervision Practicum
description: An advanced course in the theory, techniques, and problems of supervising persons being trained as counselors. Course emphasis is on actual supervisory experiences with beginning counselors. Open to advanced doctoral students only with consent of instructor.

title: Internship/Counseling Education & Educational Psychology
description: Studies of and field experiences in the development of programs in cooperating schools and educational or related agencies under the supervision of Counseling and Educational Psychology graduate faculty members. A maximum of 6 credit hours may be chosen from the areas listed.

title: Doctoral Research
description: Individual investigation in the field of a student's specialization.

title: Foundations of Rural Education
description: Examines the historical, political, philosophical, and sociological foundations of education in the United States including the evolution of career and technical education and changing demographics; ethical and legal issues; governance and financial support; and contemporary curriculum issues. Includes critical thinking and reflective decision making about rural educational issues.

title: Foundations of Urban Education
description: Examines the historical, political, philosophical, and sociological foundations of education in the United States including the evolution of career and technical education and changing demographics; ethical and legal issues; governance and financial support; and contemporary curriculum issues. Includes critical thinking and reflective decision making about urban educational issues.

title: Introduction to Game Studies
description: Introductory course on the emerging field of game studies, or the critical study of games and gaming. Students will be introduced to the theoretical foundations of games and play, specifically noting their developmental, cultural, and social value.

title: Teaching as a Career in Rural Contexts
description: Examines the roles, responsibilities, and dispositions of effective rural teachers; characteristics of the rural teaching/learning environment; the design of K-State's teacher education program; ways teachers change during their careers; and self-assessment about the commitment to teaching and preferences to grade levels or subject areas. An overview of the teacher education program is provided, the unit's conceptual framework is discussed, and the portfolio requirements are initiated. The skills of reflection and the dimensions of caring and ethical practice are introduced.

title: Teaching as a Career in Urban Contexts
description: Examines the roles, responsibilities, and dispositions of effective urban teachers; characteristics of the urban teaching/learning environment; the design of K-State's teacher education program; ways teachers change during their careers; and self-assessment about the commitment to teaching and preferences to grade levels or subject areas. An overview of the teacher education program is provided, the unit's conceptual framework is discussed, and the portfolio requirements are initiated. The skills of reflection and the dimensions of caring and ethical practice are introduced.

title: Early Field Experience
description: Includes 45 hours of supervised field experiences examining selected aspects of teaching and the learning environment, including students and learning, content and pedagogy, planning, instruction, and professionalism. Requires written reports, analyses, and reflections for each weekly school visit.

title: Ludology and History of Analog Games
description: Students will become familiar with ludology, the study of game mechanics, and general theory and history dealing with the field

title: Coaching and Officiating Methodology
description: Study of rules, theory, and practices; methods of coaching and officiating.

title: Current Issues in Education: Global Perspective
description: Examines current issues in K-12 education from a global perspective. Introduction to the concept of global education and explore values, beliefs, behaviors, policies and customs from around the World.

title: Core Teaching Skills and Lab
description: Examines instructional planning, differentiating instruction, direct and indirect instructional strategies, strategies to promote student understanding, managing lesson delivery, classroom management, assessing student performance, and working with colleagues and parents.

title: Core Teaching Skills and Lab for Rural Education
description: Examines instructional planning, differentiating instruction, direct and indirect instructional strategies, strategies to promote student understanding, managing lesson delivery, classroom management, assessing student performance, and working with colleagues and parents in rural education settings.

title: Core Teaching Skills and Lab for Urban Education
description: Examines instructional planning, differentiating instruction, direct and indirect instructional strategies, strategies to promote student understanding, managing lesson delivery, classroom management, assessing student performance, and working with colleagues and parents in urban education settings.

title: Game Studies of TTRPGs
description: An exploratory look at the history and evolution of Tabletop Roleplaying Games (TTRPGs). Students will analyze a variety of TTRPG game systems, inquiring into their mechanical systems and narrative elements.

title: Game Studies of Wargames
description: Students will become familiar with ludology, the study of game mechanics, and general theory dealing with wargames, conflict simulation, and serious wargames.

title: Game Studies of Card Games
description: An exploratory look at the history and evolution of card games, specifically the modern evolution of Collectible Card Games (CCG) and Trading Card Games (TCG) genres.

title: Game Studies of Board Games
description: An exploratory look at the history and evolution of "serious" board games, or board games designed for a specific purpose rather than solely for entertainment.

title: PK-12 PE Motor Skills and Game Development
description: Equips future physical education teachers with the knowledge and skills to effectively teach motor skills and facilitate games in K-12 settings. Emphasizing developmentally appropriate practices, students will explore strategies for fostering motor skill acquisition, designing engaging lessons, and creating student-centered game-based learning experiences.

title: Problem-Based Learning and Computational Thinking in K-12 Schools
description: This course will focus on the application of problem-solving practices, computational thinking skills, and the development of critical thinking processes as a way of engaging K-12 students in learning through interdisciplinary projects.

title: Teaching Computer Science in Elementary Schools
description: Using block-coding students will participate in activities designed according to the research-based principles developed by the Raspberry Pi Foundation and the National Centre for Computing Education. Focus is placed upon developing concepts around variable, expressions, loops, and abstraction (VELA). Students will utilize a variety of unplugged to teach computing concepts. Students will also convert these activities into powerful lessons and units focused on real world examples of computing in our society.

title: Teaching Computer Science in Secondary Schools
description: The course will provide participants with the knowledge and ability to develop coding-based units for grades 7-12 which focus on coding in both block and text-based languages. Using Scratch (block coding) and either java or python (text coding) students will develop lessons and units which are designed according to the research-based principles developed by the Raspberry Pi Foundation and the National Centre for Computing Education.

title: PK-12 PE Assessment and Measurements
description: Provides an in-depth exploration of assessment principles and practices in PK-12 physical education. Emphasizing both theoretical foundations and practical applications, students will learn to design, implement, and analyze assessments that measure physical fitness, motor skills, cognitive understanding, and student well-being and developmental skills across student populations.

title: New Literacies in Contemporary Classrooms
description: This course introduces general concepts of new literacies (i.e., the skills, strategies and dispositions needed to adapt to constantly changing information and communication technologies) along with innovative approaches to literacy instruction. In particular, the course will focus on the use of digital tools and multimodal texts to support reading comprehension, written and oral communication, visual literacy, global competence, and creative thinking/ problem solving in K-6 classrooms.

title: Middle-Level Education
description: This course provides an overview of the characteristics of middle schools; the social, psychological, and physical characteristics of early adolescent development; middle-level curriculum; ways to organize for instruction; and the teacher's role in the guidance of students at the middle level.

title: Blick Ii Lab/Content And Reading Methods
Overview of Educational Studies
description: An exploration of transition to the field of education and educational studies,identifying elements of effective transition, instruction, and support. This course identifies how our social and professional backgrounds and previous experiences affect us during thet ransitional process.

title: Applied Linguistics for Diverse Settings
description: This course explores the theoretical underpinnings of language acquisition and linguistics. The main emphasis of the course is on linguistic issues within bilingualism, multilingualism, translanguaging, discourse analysis, literacy development, different levels of language and their implications for instructional modifications at the classroom level.

title: Linguistic Accommodations for Pluralistic Classrooms
description: Focuses on the fundamentals of contextually and linguistically relevant practices for today's pluralistic classrooms. Special emphasis is placed on ways to navigate and create student-informed ecologies of learning and provide meaningful core instruction and the needed accommodations for diverse learners within PreK-12 settings. Through a critical lens, students will examine the historical and contemporary efforts and approaches for meaningful change, societal interactions, acculturation vs. social differentiation, pluralistic perspectives and initiatives, and the importance of transformative practices within our schools and society.

title: Principles and Practices in Community-Focused Education
description: This course focus on the core societal pillars of education, employment, housing, and entrepreneurship as interconnected systems that shape opportunity, participation, and long-term sustainability in contemporary society. Students will analyze their own perspectives, study social and economic change, and develop context-aware professional practices for examining policies, institutional structures, and patterns educational opportunity. Emphasis is placed on evaluating how systems operate, identifying barriers to participation, and considering strategies that support creating opportunities, stability, and responsible civic engagement.

title: English as a Second Language Practicum PK-12
description: As a capstone course, ESL practicum is a portfolio-based learning experience providing students a chance to showcase and highlight their theoretical perspectives regarding ESL, Bilingual Education, Second Language Acquisition, and Contextual and Linguistic accommodations within pluralistic settings. This course will also ask students to make theory to practice connections as they plan modifications and adaptations for all learners within PreK-12 settings.

title: Methods Of Teaching In The Secondary School
Design Principles of Analog Gaming
description: Fundamental design theory and principles of analog games, including board, card, and tabletop games.

title: Introduction to Education Technology
description: Examines the roles that technology, teachers, and learners may take in any educational context; ways of integrating technology into teaching and learning; and issues involved in using technology in K-12 settings.

title: Instructional Systems Design
description: Examines ways to design instructional modules based on key principles of instructional systems design.

title: Evaluating Educational Media Products
description: Employs project-based and collaborative learning strategies to help participants internalize vital considerations and criteria with respect to evaluating educational media products for potential integration into teaching and learning. After being introduced to key concepts, principles, and theories related to learners and their characteristics, learning and cognition, and instructional contexts, standards, objectives, and strategies, participants work towards building their ideal instruments/tools to evaluate the potential of various educational media products to support effective teaching and meaningful learning.

title: Societal Issues in Educational Technology
description: Addresses social and contextual issues pertaining to the integration of educational and communications technologies (ECT) into teaching and learning; examines concerns relating to opportunities for all learners to ECT; explores the impact of ECT adoption and use upon all learners; and addresses designing and developing contextually cognizant, appropriate, and relevant ECT.

title: Effective Communication in Educational Settings
description: This course introduces students to the theories, dynamics, and interventions for effective communication and conflict resolution within educational and/or K-12 settings and among educational stakeholders

title: Leveraging Data in Ed. Settings
description: This course offers the foundational understandings of data in education. The course introduces contemporary concepts of data, big data, and the emerging concept as well as field of study--educational data.The course covers uses of data to inform decisions and actions with an emphasis on educational data and the practice of ethically leveraging data to benefit the broad set of stakeholders, promoting learning environments for all students.

title: Educational Policy
description: This course offers an analytical foundation for students interested in PK-12 education and educational public policy implementation within those settings. Drawing on social science research, the course examines the strengths and limitations of various education reform initiatives at the federal, state, and local levels. We will also apply analytical concepts to school reform issues. This course is designed to introduce students to a variety of educational policy strategies aimed at improving the quality of teaching and learning in PK-12 settings. It aims to help students build a rich framework for analyzing policies of various types, both theoretically and methodologically.

title: Schooling in Contemporary Media
description: An examination of the symbiotic relationship of American public schools and American contemporary media.  The course will consider how schools are reflected in the contemporary media and how the representation of schooling in contemporary media affects public opinion, public policy, and schools themselves.

title: Internship in Educational Studies
description: The internship is the culminating experience for the Bachelor of Science degree in Educational Studies (B.S.E.S.) . Depending upon student academic and career interests, and in consultation with the program advisor and the faculty advisory committee, the student will receive a placement in an appropriate public or private site to serve a professional internship.

title: Ethnographic Capstone Project in Rural Education
description: This project-based course will be completed during the Professional Semester. Students will explore the demographic composition of a school community in a rural setting. They will develop an individual project to demonstrate application of knowledge and skills gained throughout the Rural Education program.

title: Ethnographic Capstone Project in Urban Education
Teaching Participation In The Elementary School
Teaching Participation In The Secondary School
Supervised Practicum For Athletic Coaches
description: Observation and coaching participation under the direction of selected coaches in public school, club, city recreation, or other nonpublic school sport settings.

title: Organization And Processing Of Instructional Materials
Foreign Language Methods For Elementary Schools
Teaching In The Middle/Junior High School
Introduction to Bilingual/Dual Language Education
description: This course focuses on the history and foundations of bilingual/DL education, as well as an in-depth examination of contemporary programming models and trends in bilingual education. The dynamics of bilingualism at the individual, system, and societal levels will also be an emphasis of study.

title: Administration and Supervision of Vocational Education
description: Emphasis on the duties and responsibilities of administrative and supervisory personnel responsible for the promotion, development, and coordination of comprehensive vocational-technical education programs at the local level.

title: Curriculum, Instruction, and Assessment
description: Develops core concepts and skills necessary for effective teaching in elementary and secondary schools. The course examines curriculum, pedagogy, and assessment techniques.

title: MAT Secondary Math Methods
description: Principles of teaching applied to mathematics instruction in the secondary and middle school including: motivation, organization of subject matter, lesson/unit planning, assessment and reporting, organization and management of the classroom, and methodology and materials of the secondary and middle schools.

title: Science Methods and Practicum for Teaching and Learning
description: This course addresses principles of teaching applied to science instruction in the secondary school. These principles include: motivation, organization of subject matter, lesson planning for direct, inquiry, and social/personal instructional emphases; assessment, meeting the needs of individual students; organization and management of the classroom; and methodology and materials of the secondary school science curriculum.

title: MAT Social Studies Methods
description: Principles of teaching applied to social studies instruction in the secondary and middle schools including: motivation, organization of subject matter, lesson/unit planning, assessment and reporting, organization and management of the classroom, and methodology and materials of the secondary and middle schools.

title: MAT Secondary English/Language Arts g
description: Principles of teaching applied to language arts instruction in the secondary and middle schools including: motivation, organization of subject matter, lesson/unit planning, assessment and reporting, organization and management of the classroom, and methodology and materials of the secondary and middle schools.

title: FACS Methods and Practicum for Teaching and Learning
description: This course focuses on the principles of teaching applied to Family and Consumer Sciences instruction; organization of subject matter, unit and lesson planning, evaluation and assessment, classroom management, motivation, learning styles, methodology and materials related to teaching Family and Consumer Sciences in the middle school and secondary classroom.

title: Business Methods and Practicum for Teaching and Learning
description: Principles of teaching applied to business education instruction in the secondary and middle schools including:  motivation, organization of subject matter, lesson/unit planning, assessment and reporting, organization and management of the classroom, and methodology and materials of the secondary and middle schools.

title: Agricultural Education Methods and Practicum for Teaching and Learning
description: Principles of teaching applied to agriculture education instruction in the secondary and middle schools including:  motivation, organization of subject matter, lesson/unit planning, assessment and reporting, organization and management of the classroom, and methodology and materials of the secondary and middle schools.

title: Social Foundations of K-12 Education
description: Examines the historical, political, philosophical, economic, legal, and sociological intersections of public education in the United States of America; emphasizes freedom, democracy, truth, power, and choice as core concepts of social change and social justice.

title: Art Methods and Practicum for Teaching and Learning
description: Principles of teaching applied to art instruction in the secondary and middle schools including: motivation, organization of subject matter, lesson/unit planning, assessment and reporting, organization and management of the classroom, and methodology and materials of the secondary and middle schools. Field-based experience to help the pre-professional teacher practice the incorporation of art with reading methods and demonstrate application of technology into teaching and assessment in the secondary and middle schools.

title: MAT Foreign Language Methods
description: Principles of teaching applied to modern language instruction in the secondary and middle schools including:  motivation, organization of subject matter, lesson/unit planning, assessment and reporting, organization and management of the classroom, and methodology and materials of the secondary and middle schools.

title: Reading in the Bilingual/DL Classroom
description: The course will focus on evidence based instructional literacy and reading skill development among learners in bilingual/DL programs.

title: Literacy and Diverse Learners in the Content Areas
description: An examination of contemporary literacy and diverse learners in the secondary school content areas in light of learning theory and adolescents' stages of development.

title: Teaching Diverse Learners
description: Examines teaching in a culturally diverse society. Emphasizes historically underrepresented students, culturally and linguistically diverse learners, abilities, exceptionalities, gender, religion, socioeconomic status, sexual orientation and preference. Additionally, this course explores environments that are healthy, respectful, supportive, and challenging for all students.

title: Introduction to Educational Technology
description: This course examines the roles that technology, teachers, and learners may take in any educational context: ways of integrating technology into teaching and learning; and issues involved in using technology in K-12 settings.

title: Physical Education and Health Methods and Practicum for Teaching and Learning
ESL/Dual Language Methods
description: An exploration of contemporary approaches, methods, and strategies for the appropriate instruction of second language learners. Also provided is a foundational perspective on ESL/Dual Language approaches, including the communicative, cognitive, and grammatical.

title: Computer Application in Elem Curriculum
description: Theory and practice of using computer software to enhance teaching and learning in specific subject areas. Subjects covered will vary.

title: Fundamentals of Teaching Music
description: Techniques, materials, and experiences for a variety of music classes will be examined through discovery. Furthermore, lesson plan development, writing, and implementation will be examined in detail.

title: Music Methods for Elementary Schools
description: The study of the musical characteristics of children and development of appropriate curriculum, instructional materials and techniques, and related technology for teaching musical understanding, literacy, multicultural connections, and creative improvisation in grades K-6 instrumental, vocal, and general music.

title: Music Program in Middle and Secondary Schools
description: Organization and administration of the comprehensive music program in middle and secondary high schools; including the study of vocal and instrumental ensemble development, as well as techniques, materials and appropriate instructional technologies.

title: Advanced Methods in Music Education
description: Advanced instructional methods and technologies related to the teaching of music in grades preK-12 implemented through field experiences.

title: Game-Based Learning
description: An exploration into how learning and games intersect, emphasizing ludology, game-based learning, and game-based inquiry.

title: Middle-Level Mathematics for Teachers
description: This course reviews math concepts, problems and applications to help prepare teachers to pass the Middle School Mathematics Praxis Exam. The course helps teachers gain pedagogical content knowledge in relation to teaching mathematics.

title: Education Oof the Disadvantaged
description: Consideration of the life-space of the disadvantaged learner and its relationship to curriculum, organization, and interpersonal relationships in schools. The development of realistic, relevant goals for the teacher of the disadvantaged.

title: ESL/Dual Language Linguistics
description: Explores the theoretical underpinnings of language acquisition and linguistics that educators need to understand, in order to better plan appropriately adapted curriculum and instruction for second language learners. The course encompasses problematic aspects of English language learning, the ways in which languages may differ, and certain universal aspects of languages.

title: Gender Implications for Education
description: Implications of the research based on sex-role stereotyping in educational institutions. Examination of resource materials, teaching strategies, and policies intended to facilitate non-sexist educational environments.

title: Practica in Business and Office Occupations
description: Related occupational or professional experiences in approved industry, school, Cooperative Extension Service, or similar agency setting under faculty supervision.

title: Pedagogy of Game-Based Learning
description: Exploration of game-based learning and game-based inquiry as a pedagogical tool for classroom instruction.

title: Culture and Language in Classroom Practice
description: Examines ways teachers can address diversity in their classrooms with attention to issues such as critical reflection on practice; effective collaboration with families; diversification of the learning environment; use and adaptation of the grade-level curriculum; the differentiation of classroom instruction for cultural and linguistic diversity; and advocacy skill development.

title: ESL/Dual Language Assessment
description: An in-depth examination of key issues/challenges in the appropriate language assessment of culturally and linguistically diverse students. Among focal topics in theory, research and practice discussed will be: pre-and post-instructional assessment, authentic and alternative assessment, language testing and placement for programming in ESL/Dual language classrooms.

title: ESL/Dual Language Elementary Practicum
description: The practicum is a portfolio-based experience providing the student with application experiences in ESL/Dual Language methods, assessment, and multicultural competence as well as the opportunity to demonstrate understanding of second language acquisition. Students will be required to spend 60 hours in a school setting where they can practice and implement ESL/BE lessons/methodology.

title: ESL/Dual Language Secondary Practicum
description: The practicum is a portfolio-based experience providing the student with application experiences in ESL/Dual Language methods, assessment, and multicultural competence as well as the opportunity to demonstrate understanding of second language acquisition. Students will be required to spend 60 hours in a school setting where they can practice and implement ESL/BE lessons/methodology.

title: Design Principles of Game-Based Learning
description: Fundamental design theory and principles of game-based learning experiences, including board, card, and tabletop games.

title: Wargames in Learning and Teaching
description: Examines the use of wargames in game-based learning environments, with an emphasis on pedagogical and design applications.

title: Card Games in Learning and Teaching
description: Examines the use of card games in game-based learning environments, with an emphasis on pedagogical and design applications.

title: Board Games in Learning and Teaching
description: Examines the use of serious board games in game-based learning environments, with an emphasis on pedagogical and design applications.

title: Simulations in Learning and Teaching
description:
Examines the use of simulations in game-based learning environments, with an emphasis on pedagogical and design applications.
Students will primarily study the design and mechanics of tabletop simulations in the classroom, as well as their effectiveness as teaching tools, by playing through several simulations. Students will then be expected to design, produce, and test their own simulations with classmates.

title: Multicultural Issues in Teaching
description: Examines the socio-cultural forces that influence the American educational system. Considers curriculum and instruction implications of human differences, and analyzes ways to promote equitable, quality education.

title: Tradebooks Across the Curriculum
description: This course examines the use of children's literature in the K-8 literacy classroom. It focuses on recently published books in several literary genres across curricular areas.

title: Reading/Writing Connections
description: This course examines teaching language arts in an integrated manner, exploring the relationships between reading and writing from a developmental perspective. Teaching methods for a holistic environment are highlighted.

title: Action Research in Education
description: This course introduces the theoretical and practical dimensions of school-based action research about curriculum and instruction topics, and students conduct an action research project. Access to the research site is required.

title: Engineering Design Practices in Secondary Science Teaching
description: This course prepares secondary science teachers to integrate engineering design practices at the same level as scientific inquiry in alignment with the Next Generation Science Standards (NGSS). Integration of practices in the curriculum is required for licensure in science disciplines. Evidence of proficiency includes instructional design, assessment, evaluation, and practicum experiences. Emphasis is placed on specifying criteria and constraints for acceptable solutions; generating and evaluating multiple solutions; building and testing prototypes; and optimizing solutions through an iterative process.

title: Teaching Computer Science in Secondary Schools
description: The course will provide participants with the knowledge and ability to develop coding-based units for grades 7-12 which focus on coding in both block and text-based languages. Using Scratch (block coding) and either java or python (text coding) students will develop lessons and units which are designed according to the research-based principles developed by the Raspberry Pi Foundation and the National Centre for Computing Education.

title: Advanced Approaches in World Lang Ed
description: The study of contemporary approaches, methods, and research-based best practices for world language instruction. Provides a venue to analyze and discuss programmatic and curricular decisions and how that impacts teaching and learning.

title: Assessment in World Language Education
description: The course will study how to create, implement, and analyze world language assessments to enhance instruction and support learning

title: Current Trends and Research in World Language Education
description: Analyze current trends in world language education. Recent research will be stressed. Explore how this applies to curricular and instructional decisions.

title: Contemporary Technology in World Language Education
description: The course will study pedagogical implications of instructional technologies to enhance and support world language education. There will be a focus on the constant evolution of technology and how it can enhance the teaching and learning of world language education.

title: Readings in Curriculum and Instruction
description: Readings in research and application in specialized areas in education.

title: Teaching in Middle Schools
description: Several instructional approaches consistent with the characteristics of the emerging adolescent student (grades 5-9) will be examined in relation to current research. Direct development of alternative curricular programs. Appropriate use of interdisciplinary activities and nontraditional materials will be emphasized.

title: Primary School Education
description: A course for those interested in the kindergarten and primary school child. Emphasis will be placed on curriculum development, pertinent research, and innovative practices in early education.

title: Teaching the Theoretical Foundations of Constitutional Government
description: Examines the theories (e.g., natural rights, classical republicanism, and constitutionalism) and theorists (e.g., Aristotle, Locke, Hobbes, and Montesquieu) that most influenced constitutional thinking in the United States.  Explores how to teach effectively about these theories and theorists.

title: Teaching the Historical Origins of Constitutional Government
description: Examines seminal ideas, documents, and events in the creation of the United States Constitution such as problems with the Articles of Confederation, the characteristics of good government, the debates at the Philadelphia Convention, the Federalist Papers, and the Antifederalist Papers.  Explores how to teach effectively these ideas, document, and events.

title: Teaching the Development of Constitutional Principles
description: Examines the development of political ideas and constitutional thinking since the Founding.  Focuses on how the Bill of Rights, the amendment process, judicial review, and the Fourteenth Amendment are used to empower and limit government.  Explores how to teach effectively about developments in constitutional thought.

title: Teaching the Institutions of Government
description: Examines the powers, limits, and development of political institutions such as Congress, the President, the Supreme Court, and political parties.  Explores how to teach effectively about these institutions.

title: Teaching the Bill of Rights
description: Examines the rights protected by the United States Constitution and the ways that rights protected by the United States Constitution and the ways that rights empower citizens, limit government, and contribute to constitutional government.  Explores how to teach effectively about rights.

title: Topics in Curriculum and Instruction
description: Examination of current topic in area of specialization of faculty.

title: Teaching Citizenship
description: Examines development of the rights, responsibilities, and challenges of citizenship in the United States.  Explores how to teach effectively about citizenship.

title: Teaching the Constitutional Principles of America's Founders
description: Develops constitutional principles; explores perspectives of constitutional interpretation, change, and preservation; raises questions about the immediate and enduring significance of America's founding principles and documents; and introduces sound pedagogical practices to teach about them.

title: Methods and Materials in Civic Education
description: Focuses on the pedagogical aspects of civic education and engagement such as research-based approaches to concept attainment, skill development, discussion, and deliberation as well as analysis of curriculum materials and programs that apply these pedagogies.

title: Engaged Citizens: Public Policy Issues and Processes
description: Examines the teaching of the workings of constitutional government through the lens of the public policy making processes and the roles of individual voters, organized public, the media, and state and local governments in that process.

title: Teaching Social Studies, Reading, and Literacy in the Elementary School
description: Develops knowledge, skills, and dispositions to effectively teach elementary social studies, reading, and language arts. Introduces content, methods, and materials. Explores curriculum integration.

title: Teaching Health, Movement, and Fine Arts in Elementary Schools
description: Develops knowledge, skills, and dispositions to effectively teach health, movement, and fine arts. Introduces content, methods, and materials. Explores curriculum integration.

title: Advanced Methods of Teaching
description: Examines popular theories, important principles, research-based strategies, and best practices associated with effective teaching.  Provides a venue to apply theory to practice; connect ideas and action; and link content and pedagogy.

title: Problems in Curriculum and Instruction
description: Independent study of a specific problem in curriculum or instruction.

title: Brain-Based Literacy Instruction
description: This course provides advanced study of theory and research regarding literacy acquisition related to brain development and function. The intended audience includes K-12 regular classroom teachers, reading teachers, resource room teachers, teacher aides, and other professionals concerned with meeting the literacy needs of children and young adults. The following topics are covered in the course: Cognitive functions, memory systems, sensitive/critical periods for brain development related to literacy, implications for classroom environment and stress upon student performance, executive functions and research-based instructional strategies found to increase student performance.

title: Secondary Content Field Colloquium I
description: This course is designed to explore key concepts within the secondary content field. Focus will be on advancing the development of disciplinary content knowledge and pedagogical content knowledge.

title: Secondary Content Field Colloquium III
description: Class explores the content knowledge of the candidate in regards to the middle and secondary content curriculum, identifies areas of strength and weakness in content knowledge, and provides enrichment and remediation to prepare the candidate for the internship experience.

title: Teaching Practicum
description: Participate in supervised field experience in Kâ€12 schools. Apply principles of curriculum, instruction, and assessment to plan, teach, and reflect.

title: Internship in K-12 Schools
description: Supervised experience teaching in disciplineâ€specific Kâ€12 school classrooms.

title: Curriculum Development
description: An overall view of the entire school curriculum, patterns of organization, outlining of instructional fields, and specific helps in curriculum development for administrators and classroom teachers.

title: Curriculum Construction For Elementary And Secondary Schools
Hist of Education
description: The history of education is explored critically through multiple perspectives. Complex forces governing changes in education are exposed--forces that are felt but unseen. By studying historical contexts, much about schooling today is seen as problematic and open to question--not as inevitable. This study pushes past a passive acceptance of the status quo via a vigorous, fresh analysis.

title: Comparative Philosophies of Education
description: Philosophical thought in education is examined critically through multiple perspectives. The learning environment, the role of the learner, and the role of the teacher are issues central to analysis in the course as they manifest themselves in learning and teaching for social change and social justice. The questions of what are freedom, democracy, truth, power, and choice are central to the examination.

title: Understanding and Teaching Reading
description: Foundational issues in K-12 reading instruction. Focus on the reading process, the nature of the learner, the text, and the instructional setting.

title: Approaches to Literacy Instruction
description: A foundational study of research-based approaches, materials, and methods for effective K-12 literacy instruction.

title: Theoretical Models of Literacy
description: Advanced study of reading with an emphasis on foundational theories and seminal research studies reflecting the complexity of the literacy process.

title: Contemporary Issues in Language Arts
description: An analysis of current methods, issues, and trends in teaching, speaking, listening, and writing through the study of significant literature and research findings.

title: Advanced Methods in the Teaching of K-12 Mathematics
description: Advanced study of selected topics in K-12 mathematics emphasizing new programs, trends, controversial topics, and new recommendations for persistent problems; findings of recent research stressed.

title: Trends In Elementary School Social Studies
History of Mathematics Education
description: This course will examine the issues and forces behind the evolution of mathematics education reform from 1820-present. Historical readings, mathematics curricula, analysis of political and social change, and research in both psychology and mathematics education will be analyzed to establish a foundation for understanding current reform efforts.

title: Research in Mathematics Teaching and Learning
description: This course will focus on seminal and contemporary research in mathematics teaching and learning. Emphasis will be placed on current trends in K-12 classrooms, the relationship between curriculum and instruction to current mathematics education research, critical analysis of research practices, solving and analyzing problems, and designing goals and outcomes for instructional strategies.

title: Contemporary Technologies in Mathematics Education
description: The purpose of this class is to explore the theoretical implications of using technology as a tool for teaching mathematics in K-12 classrooms. The course content will focus on the continual evolution of technology and how this evolution can assist in the teaching and learning of mathematics.

title: Theoretical Models of Mathematics Teaching and Learning
description: This course will focus on the theories of learning, which provide a foundation for the standards for mathematical practice. The course will specifically address historical and contemporary theories related to mathematics teaching and learning.

title: Assessment in Mathematics Education
description: This course will provide participants with assessment tools necessary to enhance learning for diverse student populations. The course will explore ways to document student performance and progress for instructional and accountability purposes. Participants will become familiar with quantitative and qualitative concepts and practices used to evaluate student learning in the classroom.

title: Contemporary Issues in Family and Consumer Sciences Education
description: Advanced study of evolving trends and materials for Family and Consumer Sciences programs; application to teaching and curriculum.

title: Contemporary Issues in Teaching and Learning
description: Advanced study of selected topics and contemporary issues in curriculum and instruction emphasizing new programs, trends, instructional strategies, and curricular issues. Findings of recent research are stressed.

title: Improving Science Teaching
description: Evaluation and implementation of psychological and philosophical foundations will be stressed in improving K-12 science teaching.

title: Literacy Assessment
description: An overview of the collection, analysis, and interpretation of data from formal and informal assessment methods and instruments to enhance literacy instruction by documenting growth and literacy development.

title: Striving Literacy Learners
