description: Explores the identities of "striving literacy learners" across grade levels to address the importance of interest, motivation, gender, culture, and language on learning to read with an emphasis on differentiated instruction to support literacy development and lifelong reading.

title: ELS/Dual Language Methods
description: This course is an exploration of contemporary approaches, methods, and strategies for the appropriate instruction of second language learners. The course examines the theories of Second Language Acquisition and the need to consider the biographies of students within the scope of classroom teaching and learning.  Also explores foundational perspectives on EFL/dual language approaches, including the communicative, cognitive, and grammatical.

title: ELD/Dual Language Culture and Language in Classroom Practice
description: This course examines the many ways in which school educators may build the capacities for cultural and linguistic diversity in the classroom. Special emphasis is placed upon readiness for mutually accommodative professional practices with culturally and linguistically diverse  [CLD] students and families in the following areas: foundations of culture and language; critical reflection on practice; maximizing the assets that CLD students and families bring to learning; school and classroom ecology; curriculum conscientiousness; the differentiation of instruction for cultural and linguistic diversity; and a framework for advocacy in practice.

title: ELD/Dual Language Assessment
description: This course provides an overview of current issues regarding the assessment of CLD students within classroom practice. Examines pre-instructional student assessment; the acculturation process and asset discovery; issues of language proficiency beyond the measurements; focus on pre-assessment, formative, and summative assessment; using interactive grouping configurations and learning strategies as an integral part of the assessment process; assessments for students in special education settings.

title: Organization And Administration Of Reading Programs
Online Course Design Project/e-Portfolio
description: The portfolio as a professional development tool is developed using Association for Educational Communications and Technology standards. An online instructional design course project with assessment is developed and placed on an e-portfolio website.

title: Instructional Systems Design
description: This course examines ways to design instructional modules based on key principles of instructional systems design.

title: Online Teaching and Learning
description: This course examines ways to design and develop online instructional modules based on key principles of instructional systems design.

title: Social/Cultural Issues in Educational Technology
description: This course addresses social and cultural issues pertaining to the integration of educational and communications technologies (ECT) into teaching and learning; examines concerns relating to equitable access to ECT; explores the impact of ECT adoption and use upon diverse social/cultural groups; and addresses designing and developing culturally cognizant, appropriate, and relevant ECT.

title: Educational Technology Change Management
description: This course introduces participants to the knowledge and skills needed to become agents of positive technological change within their workplaces. Topics covered include diffusion of innovations, needs assessment, change management, systemic change, human performance technology, socio-cultural cognizance, and inclusion/equity.

title: World Language Education Practicum
description: Participate in a supervised field experience in a PK-12 world language classroom. Apply the principles of best practice in world language curriculum and instruction planning, instruction, assessment, and reflection.

title: Advanced Study Of The Reading Process
The Social Studies Curriculum in the Secondary School
description: New trends, materials, and ideas in teaching K-12 social sciences, based on recent research and experimental programs.

title: The Language Arts Curriculum
description: The changing scene in the teaching of English: trends, materials, and ideas in literature, composition, and grammar that have emerged from recent research and discovery.

title: Teaching and Learning Models
description: Advanced study of ways to organize the curriculum, instructional strategies, and the teaching environment to promote learning. Various models of teaching are analyzed and critiqued.

title: Seminar in Curriculum and Instruction
description: Intensive discussion of research or problems of current professional interest based on study of pertinent original literature.

title: Educational Technology Proseminar I
description: Examines contemporary issues related to the role of technology in teaching and learning. Topics covered reflect current theory, research and practice within the discipline of educational technology. This is the first of a two-course sequence examining a wide range of issues; the second course is EDCI 888.

title: Educational Technology Proseminar II
description: Examines contemporary issues related to the role of technology in teaching and learning. Topics covered reflect current theory, research and practice within the discipline of educational technology. This is the second of a two-course sequence examining a wide range of issues; the first course is EDCI 887.

title: Master's Project
description: Students select and prepare a project designed to improve their professional practice. It may be the construction of a professional portfolio or project to represent the student's learning throughout the master's degree program.

title: Master's Report
description: A master's report is generally shorter than a thesis and presents the results of a more limited original investigation or a problem or topic within the student's classroom or educational field approved by the candidate's supervisory committee.

title: Master's Thesis
description: A master's thesis presents the results of an original investigation or a problem or topic within the student's classroom or educational field approved by the candidate's supervisory committee.

title: Introduction to Ed.D. Doctoral Studies
description: This course guides Ed.D. students in becoming acclimated to doctoral studies and research. Students are engaged in discussion and dialogue about the purpose of doctoral studies in education, as well as the responsibilities associated with doctoral studies and research: academic integrity, research ethics, and inclusive learning environment. Specifically, students explore doctoral studies as a way to transform ideas, epistemologies, and theories into praxis for addressing problems of practice and policy in schools.

title: Literature Reviews for Doctoral Research
description: This course explores the purpose and development of literature reviews in educational research. Students learn methods of engaging, organizing, and synthesizing literature. Students examine different theoretical frameworks and think about how certain theories can be used to address their problems of practice and policy in schools. Students will begin reviewing literature for doctoral research.

title: Curriculum Theory
description: Theoretical concepts underlying significant curriculum developments. A systematic critique of current curricular theory. Consideration of model generation.

title: Multi-Cultural Curriculum Programming
description: Application of multicultural curriculum principles to total school programming with particular emphasis on the cultural pluralism phenomenon. Includes analytic review of instruments on multicultural/multiracial curriculum evaluation as well as planning skills for equitable thrusts. Primarily involves elementary and secondary focus with some attention to postsecondary programming.

title: Historical Research Methods in Education
description: Historical research is the contextual link of the past to the present. Offering new perspectives and analyses, this course is an introduction to the methodology of historical research in education and an introduction to historical writing about educational issues. A historical research paper/project is the culminating experience.

title: Inquiry I – Statistical Analysis in Curriculum and Instruction
description: Introduction of statistics and statistical analyses in educational research. Covers a wide range of descriptive and inferential statistics. Focuses on understanding concepts and processes of statistics and interpreting the results based on educational research data. First in a two-course sequence.

title: Inquiry II – Qualitative Research in Curriculum and Instruction
description: Focus on acquiring methodological skills used to conduct qualitative research; collecting data using field notes, interviews, observations, and documents; analysis and interpretation of data; and communicating results of qualitative data in writing.

title: Inquiry III - Program Evaluation for Curriculum and Instruction
description: Introduction to basic concepts of program evalution and tools commonly used to evaluate educational programs and activities. Explores different evaluation designs, quantitative and qualitative methodologies commonly used in assessment and evaluation, and skills required to be a critical reader of evaluations.

title: Inquiry IV - Advanced Quantitative Methods in Curriculum & Instruction
description: Advanced examination of statistics and statistical analyses in educational research. Focus on transtion from learning statistics to using and presenting statistics.

title: Inquiry IV - Advanced Qualitative Methods in Curriculum & Instruction
description: Critical consideration of recent research in designated fields. Emphasis on case study research methods.

title: Narrative Inquiry in Education
description: This course examines theoretical underpinnings of narrative inquiry, narrative analysis, narrative data collection, narrative writing, and contemporary narrative research issues in education.

title: Leadership in Literacy
description: A seminar to prepare reading professionals to work as literacy leaders, advocates, and coaches to support district and school literacy professional development.

title: Principles of College Teaching
description: Principles of learning, learning theory, educational objectives, methods and techniques, college students, and evaluation in the classroom. Emphasis upon preservice and in-service help in improving instruction at the college level.

title: Clinical Practicum i Reading
description: Supervised K-12 practicum with candidates working with individuals and small groups of readers using a wide range of evidence-based instructional practices, approaches, and curricular materials.

title: Advanced Study of the Literacy Process
description: Survey of selected theories of literacy processes. Investigation of the interrelationships of the literacy performance: cognitive processes; language; social-emotional factors; and experience. Emphasis upon recent developments in the field.

title: Internship in Reading
description: Professional field experience for candidates to demonstrate expertise by supporting reading development, analyzing building and district needs, developing action plans, and conducting related professional development.

title: Advanced Seminar in Curriculum and Instruction
description: These seminars will critically consider recent research in the designated fields. The emphasis will be upon individual studies and small group interaction. Enrollment is restricted to those students who have been admitted to the doctoral program in education and who have completed substantial amounts of graduate study in the designated fields.

title: Internship in Curriculum & Instruction
description: Studies of and field experiences in the development of programs in cooperating schools and educational or related agencies under the supervision of College of Education graduate faculty members. A maximum of 6 credit hours may be chosen from the areas listed.

title: Research in Curriculum and Instruction
description: Doctoral research demonstrates the candidate's ability to conduct significant original research related to curriculum and instruction, to analyze the information obtained from the research, and to present the results in a dissertation format appropriate to the educational field.

title: Wild Approach To Integrating Environmental Education
Curriculum Materials For Dual Language Learners
Curriculum Construction For Elementary And Secondary Schools
Contemporary Issues In Teaching And Learning
The Role, Organization, And Function Of Community Colleges
Health and Movement Education in Elementary Clasrooms
description: Addresses major concepts of health education, human movement, and physical activities to enable elementary teachers to promote healthy lifestyles for all students in their instruction.

title: Arts for Elementary Schools
description: Methods, materials, and educational philosophy of the fine arts for elementary school teaching. Students will explore art and design media, techniques, and concepts including drama, movement, music, and visual arts. Emphasis will be placed on designing, implementing, and evaluating arts experiences.

title: Block B Practicum: K-6
description: A field experience designed to give students opportunities in applying teaching methods in literacy and science in elementary grades. Includes using technologies to enhance instruction and facilitate professional productivity.

title: Science of Reading I
description: Provides an evidence-based approach to teaching the science of reading in the primary grades focusing on five areas: phonemic awareness, phonics, vocabulary, fluency, and comprehension. Topics include how the brain learns to read, essential components of structured literacy, and the research behind practices that support literacy skills and language development. Addresses literacy assessment, instructional strategies, and resources for teaching decoding, language comprehension, and reading comprehension to meet the needs of all students.

title: Teaching Science: PK-6
description: An introduction to the principles and methods of teaching science in elementary grades, including the nature of science, student learning, curriculum, instructional methods and activities, and student assessment. Includes using technologies to enhance instruction and facilitate professional productivity.

title: Classroom Assessment: PK-6
description: Examines the role of assessment in elementary classrooms in teaching; characteristics of high-quality assessments; diagnostic, formative, and summative assessments; preparation of classroom tests and test items; performance and portfolio assessments; affective traits; assessment of students with special needs and characteristics; grading and reporting; and standardized tests.

title: Curricular & Instructional Considerations for All Learners
description: Examines pluralistic education and the characteristics of all learners in the classroom, including multilingual learners. Curricular and instructional implications of these differences are examined in an effort to differentiate instruction to meet the needs of all learners.

title: Teaching Methods for Mathematics, PK-2
description: Focuses on teaching mathematics in the elementary school, grades PK-2.  Topics include: reform movements in math education, effective learning environments, students' and teachers' views and experiences with mathematics, pedagogy of major mathematical concepts, and the nature of mathematical and reasoning processes.

title: Movement Exploration And Creative Dance For Children
Art Methods for Elementary Schools
description: Principles of teaching applied to art instruction in elementary schools including: motivation, child-development and mark making, organization of subject matter, lesson/unit planning, assessment and reporting, organization and management of the classroom, and methodology and materials of elementary school art.

title: Art Practicum for Elementary Schools
description: Field-based experience to help the pre-professional teacher practice the incorporation of art methods, strategies, and techniques in teaching and assessment in the elementary school.

title: Block C Practicum: K-6
description: A field experience designed to give students opportunities in applying teaching methods in literacy, social studies, and mathematics in elementary grades. Includes using technologies to enhance instruction and facilitate professional productivity.

title: Science of Reading II
description: Extends an evidence-based approach to teaching the science of reading in the intermediate grades building on five areas: phonemic awareness, phonics, vocabulary, fluency, and comprehension. Topics include orthography, morphology, essential components of structured literacy, and the research behind practices that support literacy skills. Addresses literacy assessment, instructional strategies, and resources for teaching writing, language comprehension, and reading comprehension to meet the needs of all students.

title: Teaching Social Studies: PK-6
description: Methods and resources for teaching social studies in pre and elementary grades with the goal of helping students develop the ability to make informed and reasoned decisions for the public good as citizens of a democratic society in an independent world. Includes using technologies to enhance instruction and facilitate professional productivity.

title: Teaching Mathematics: K-6
description: The teaching of mathematics in elementary grades, including the nature of mathematical processes, curriculum, methods of instruction, instructional materials, and the evaluation of outcomes. Includes using technologies to enhance instruction and facilitate professional productivity.

title: Classroom Management and Discipline: PK-6
description: Examines ways to establish and maintain a positive learning environment in elementary classrooms and specific strategies to address misbehavior. Emphasis is placed on creating an effective management system, establishing a positive learning environment, addressing individual and group motivation, and preparing to deal with misbehavior.

title: Teaching Methods for Mathematics, Grades 3-6
description: Mathematics pedgogy for the elementary school, grades 3-6. Topics include: effective learning environments, teaching major mathematical concepts, formative and summative assessment, classroom discourse, and connecting mathematics to students' lived experiences.

title: Supporting Elementary Learners in Reading and Math, Grades PK-6
description: Designed to equip future elementary educators to support elementary learners in developing strong foundational skills in reading and mathematics, particularly for students who are exceptional, in need of support, and at risk. Topics include: Structured Literacy, the Science of Reading, Multi-Tiered Systems of Support (MTSS), and Response to Intervention (RTI) strategies within the context of all learners in the regular education classroom.

title: Teaching Methods for STEAM, PK-6
description: Integrating pedagogical and disciplinary knowledge needed to deliver high-quality STEM/STEAM education in the elementary classroom. Real-world experiences related to key concepts in the Next Generation Science Standards (NGSS) and other content-area standards through inquiry-based, problem-based, and/or phenomena-based approaches.

title: Undergraduate Research Experience
description: Open to students pursuing undergraduate research projects.

title: Independent Study/Elementary Education
description: Selected topics in professional education. Maximum of 3 hours applicable toward degree requirements.

title: Physical Education/Health Methods for PK-6 Schools
description: This class includes both theoretical and practical guidelines for analysis and implementation of children's physical education/health. The course focuses on applications of the Kansas standards for elementary educators PK-6 grades.

title: Physical Education/Health Practicum for PK-6 Schools
description: This course will provide an opportunity for students to plan, teach, and evaluate Physical Education/Health lessons within a placement at the PK-6 school level.

title: Teaching Internship in the Elementary School
description: Observation and teaching participation under the direction of selected elementary teachers.

title: Foreign Language Elementary School Methods
description: Methods of teaching and organization for the foreign language program in the elementary school. A score of "2" or "Advanced" on the Oral Proficiency Interview (OPI) is required.

title: Foreign Language Elementary School Practicum
description: A teaching experience to apply the methods of teaching and organization for the foreign language program in the elementary school.

title: Foreign Language Methods For Elementary Schools
Contemporary Mathematics In The Elementary/Middle Schools
Trends In Elementary School Social Studies
Organization And Processing Of Instructional Materials
Proseminar 1:  Educational Computing, Design, & Distance Ed
Proseminar 2:  Technology Change, Research, And Theory
Design And Evaluation Of Educational Software
Fundamental Histories and Philosophies in Indigenous Education
description: Examines the history of education with Indigenous populations in both Eurocentric and non-Eurocentric contexts. Includes: examining Indigenous knowledges found outside of what is considered mainstream education, critical Indigenous philosophies and frameworks from Indigenous scholars in the academy, the foundations of Native sovereignty in the United States, and pedagogical approaches within Eurocentric and non-Eurocentric spaces.

title: Self-Determination, Sovereignty, and Systems of Indigenous Education
description: Focuses on the evolution of sovereignty for Native nations as it exists in modern educational systems. Includes exploration of the bureaucratic landscape of Indian education in the United States and leading/advocating for Indigenous students and families across educational systems in ways that allow Native nations to build their sovereign capacities to play a more prominent role in the education of their citizens.

title: Leadership, Advocacy, and Action in Indigenous Education
description: Prepares educators working in or with Indigenous communities to help create culturally sustaining learning environments. Explores leadership, advocacy and action at the classroom and program management levels with focus on building working technical knowledge.

title: Applied Project in Educational Leadership for Indigenous Populations
description: Application of theories, principles, and skills learned throughout the Indigenous Education Leadership Certificate program to a professional problem/setting.

title: Educational Change and Technology
description: This course deals with educational change, teaming, and information literacy. Three thrusts are considered: the emerging technologies and educational change, shaping change with technology, and the use of technology/information literacy to transform teaching and learning.

title: Readings/Educational Leadership
description: Readings in research and application in specialized areas in education.

title: Topics in Educational Leadership
description: Examination of current topic in area of specialization of faculty.

title: Problems in Educational Leadership
description: Selected students are permitted to secure specialized training appropriate to the needs of the individual. The student's project may involve intensive library investigation in a special field or the collection and analysis of data pertinent to a given problem. All work is done independently under the direction of a faculty member. As many conferences are held as necessary to assure successful completion of a project.

title: Ethical Dimensions of Educational Leadership
description: The course rests on the assumption that leadership is a subset of ethics rather than ethics being a subset of leadership. We will examine the ethics of what leaders are, what they do, and how they do it. Students will assess the public and private ethics of leaders, the ethical obligations of leaders and followers, the ways in which leaders shape the ethical environment of institutions, and the temptations of power. We will examine ethical issues related to leadership through case studies concerning leaders in a variety of contexts and cultures. The course looks at how leaders convey values through actions, language, and as role models. It aims to expand students' ethical point of view by first considering personal ethics, then moving on to look at leadership and the common good, and finishing with an examination of ethics in learning communities.

title: Historical and Philosophical Analysis of Leadership in Education
description: This course seeks to insure that students understand the historical and philosophical background that forms the foundation for current educational systems, offerings, and practices, with a focus on educational leadership. Students will be expected to gather information and research from both primary and secondary sources, be reflective practitioners, and apply higher level cognitive and affective skills. Abilities in writing and critical analysis will be required as students apply, analyze, synthesize, and evaluate a variety of writings regarding the history and philosophy of education in the United States, as well as international influences.

title: Philosophy of Education
description: A critical analysis of major educational philosophies with discussion of their impact on the problem of education for democracy.

title: Introduction to Educational Finance
description: An examination of P-12 public school fiscal policy construction and daily operational issues, including evaluation of local, state, and federal fiscal support, tax structures, distributional formulas, finance reform strategies, and budget preparation and administration.

title: Data Literacy/Data Informed Decisions in Educational Leadership
description: Examines actionable school mission and vision attentive to values and priorities that include data use, technology, values, equity, diversity, digital citizenship, social-emotional well-being, academic needs, and based on school improvement implementation theory and research. Provides overview of the types of research methodologies, their strengths and limitations, and the contexts to which they are most suitable to school improvement.

title: Strategic Leadership In Education
description: This course deals with an overview of educators becoming strategic leaders at the program, building, and district levels. Two central thrusts are considered: (1) professional and ethical leadership, and (2) information management and evaluation leadership. Emphasis is placed on knowledge, skills and attributes to identify contexts, developing with others vision and purpose, utilizing information, framing problems exercising leadership processes to achieve common goals, and acting ethically for educational communities.

title: Political and Community Leadership in Education
description: A study of the relationship between the school and the community, with special emphasis on the development of a comprehensive community education program. Organizational patterns, financing, program development, and interaction with other community agencies are analyzed.

title: Scholarly Orientation to Graduate Studies
description: This course offers graduate students opportunities to learn how to write in a scholarly way, manage bibliography, understand formatting and citational guidelines of American Psychological Association, conduct index-driven library searches for scholarly sources, and develop an understanding of academic standards of graduate studies in education.

title: Introduction to Educational Facility Planning
description: An introduction to the principles and processes involved in providing an appropriate physical infrastructure for P-12 education. Emphasis is placed on planning, financing, constructing, maintaining, and utilizing physical resources.

title: Educational Law
description: An examination of the legal status of educational institutions in the United States; the legal rights and responsibilities of educators including due process, tort liability, and contracts; student rights; landmark court decisions; federal and state legislation impacting on education, and resources available to assist in developing solutions to legal problems.

title: Strategies for Educational Change
description: This course provides educators with conceptual knowledge concerning the problems and processes of educational change. Case studies of change are analyzed in the attempt to develop models of educational change.

title: The Principalship
description: Analysis of the principal's role as he or she interacts with various referent groups. Applicable to both elementary and secondary administration.

title: School-Community Relations
description: Interrelationships that exist between the school and the community and the role of the teacher and administrator in such relationships.

title: Qualitative Research In Education
description: An overview of theoretical and philosophical perspectives informing qualitative research is presented. In addition, methods (design, data collection, data analysis, and reporting) used in qualitative research for educational settings are examined and applied.

title: District, Building, and Program Leadership in Education
description: This course deals with an overview of educators becoming leaders of instruction at the district, building, and program level. Four thrusts are being considered (1) instruction, (2) curriculum, (3) staff development, and (4) supervisory leadership. Considerable emphasis is placed on knowledge, skills, and attributes of learning leadership. Themes of planning, conducting, and assessing performance are covered in depth.

title: Leadership for Diverse Populations
description: Designed for educators to increase their knowledge of self and others regarding issues of diversity. An emphasis is placed on understanding the role of educators inpromoting practices and policies supportive of all stakeholders in the educational organization.

title: Understanding Theoretical Frameworks
description: Students will explore a variety of philosophical stances that underpin research methods. Examines the nature of reality (ontology) and knowledge (epistemology). Students are expected to develop a personal philosophy of inquiry that will help inform their theoretical and methodological approaches to research.

title: Administrative Leadership in Curriculum
description: This course identifies the major roles and responsibilities of school administrators in curriculum-related activities. Administrative skills necessary for developing and evaluating the curriculum will be examined.

title: Inclusive School Culture in Educational Leadership
description: Examines the use of data to evaluate, cultivate, and advocate for equitable educational resources, technologies, and opportunities that support the educational success and social emotional well-being of each student. Application of knowledge and skills to promote the current and future success of each student and adult and maintain a supportive, equitable, culturally responsive, and inclusive school culture.

title: Administrative Leadership in Staff Development
description: This course focuses on the role of the administrator in developing, implementing, and evaluating staff development programs. Superintendent, building-level administrator, and staff development director leadership skills will be analyzed.

title: Educational Professional Learning and Human Resources in Educational Leadership
description: Examines human resource administration practices in school leadership with the capacity to develop and engage staff in a collaborative professional culture designed to promote school improvement, teacher retention, and the success and well-being of each student and adult in the school. Engagement in research-based strategies for personnel supervision and evaluation.

title: Administrative Leadership in Staff Supervision
description: This course identifies the major roles and responsibilities of superintendents and building-level administrators as supervisors of staff in a K-12 school district.

title: Technology Leadership for Administrators
description: A course designed to provide an in-depth analysis of administrator technology leadership skills necessary for integrating technology in education. The roles and technologies of technology leadership will be studied in the context of staff development, supervision, and curriculum articulation. Considerable attention will be given to strategies necessary for creating district and building technology plans.

title: Seminar In Educational Leadership
description: Intensive discussion of a problem of current professional interest based on study of pertinent original literature.

title: Practicum in Educational Leadership
description: Supervised on-the-job experience in school leadership.

title: The School District Leader
description: An examination of research and practice, looking at the role of the school superintendent through the standards for School Leaders, as developed by the Interstate School Leaders Licensure Consortium (ISLLC) and adapted by the state of Kansas for licensure requirements for the district leadership license. Emphasis is placed on leading school improvement efforts, and forming and sustaining purposeful learning communities.

title: District Leader Internship
description: Satisfies the internship requirement for the district leadership license recommendation. Students are required to document a minimum 150 clock hours guided field experience through a formal internship and other coursework (i.e., additional internship requirements beyond EDLEA 902 are also imbedded in many courses in the licensure sequence to assist in reaching the 150 clock hour requirement).

title: Educational Human Resource Administration
description: Human resource administration practices in education are considered along with the implications of collective negotiations and professional accountability for human resource policies.

title: Organizational Leadership in Education
description: This course is an examination of the knowledge, skills, and attributes necessary to understand and improve organizations. It includes leadership theory, implementing plans, managing resources, and applying appropriate processes and procedures in varied educational settings.

title: Narrative and Arts-Based Inquiry in Qualitative Research
description: Students will be introduced to narrative and arts-based inquiry to inform their qualitative research projects. Students will identify a genre from narrative and arts-based inquiry to prepare representational and methodological reflection pieces to inform their future research. Topics covered include critical autoethnography, creative non-fiction, visual representation, mixed-medium art and more.

title: Advanced Data Analysis in Qualitative Methods
description: An overview and application of data analysis methods in qualitative inquiry. In addition representation of findings, documentation of methods, analytic frameworks, processes of analysis, ethical dilemmas, and human subject approval explored.

title: Data Representation and Writing in Qualitative Research
description: Topics covered in this course would vary each semester based on students' writing projects. Topics may include, but not limited to, building methodological arguments, connecting theoretical and methodological frameworks, navigating issues in various genres of data representation, triangulation, and aligning findings with research purpose and questions.

title: Advanced School Business Management
description: An advanced course in school finance policy and budgeting practices, with particular consideration for hands-on application to school district leadership in Kansas.

title: Case Study in Qualitative Research
description: This is an advanced research course on qualitative case study design and application in social science research. Topics include types of case studies, defining the case, site, and sample selection, data collection methods, within-case and cross-case analysis, and writing case reports. Students will conduct and critique a case study appropriate to their discipline.

title: Discourse Analysis
description: Students will be exposed to the broad and complex field of discourse analysis. Students will learn about different theoretical and methodological approaches to analyzing the various forms and conceptualizations of ""discourse"". The course will weave together theory and method, as it will cover discourse analysis as both method and methodology.

title: Qualitative Data Management and Analysis Using Nvivo
description: Students will have opportunity to explore the relationships between current technologies and the theory and methods of research. Students will learn to use qualitative analysis software, Nvivo, with specific data sets for preparation of data files, management of text and image, creation of codes, memos, queries models, and formation of reports.

title: Advanced Seminar/Educational Leadership
description: These seminars will critically consider recent research in the designated fields. The emphasis will be upon individual studies and small group interaction. Enrollment is restricted to those students who have been admitted to the doctoral program in education and who have completed substantial amounts of graduate study in the designated fields.

title: Differentiated Research
description: Students will complete a research project of their choice by breaking the project into smaller components, setting goals, maintaining accountability, and preparing deliverables towards the completion of the project. Projects can include theory, methodological work, book chapters, publishable papers, dissertation proposal or chapter drafts, grant work, and more.

title: Doctoral Internship in Educational Leadership
description: Studies of and field experiences in the development of programs in cooperating schools and educational or related agencies under the supervision of Educational Leadership graduate faculty members.

title: Research in Educational Leadership
description: Individual investigation in the field of a student's specialization.

title: Careers in Family and Consumer Sciences Education-1
description: With a focus on family and consumer sciences (FCS) education foundations, FCS framework, FCCLA, FCS branding, and professional practice, this course explores the principles in planning and organizing family and consumer sciences curriculum and career pathways.  The course introduces professional practices that support curriculum design elements and instructional methods unique to programs that emphasize technical skill proficiency and competency-based applied learning.

title: Careers in Family and Consumer Sciences Education-2
description: EDSEC 211 With a focus on career pathways, work-based learning, internship development, employability skills, and advisory boards, this course explores the principles in planning and organizing family and consumer sciences curriculum and career pathways.  The course introduces professional practices that support curriculum design elements and instructional methods unique to programs that emphasize technical skill proficiency and competency-based applied learning.

title: Coaching And Officiating Track And Field
Prevention And Recognition Of Athletic Injuries
Social Studies Colloquium I
description: Application and integration of theories and practices of social studies education. Emphasis on rudimentary knowledge of content disciplines and how they can be incorporated in education.

title: Administration Of Physical Education, Athletic, And Intr Prg
Introduction to Physical Education/Health
description: This course includes identification of competency-based, goal-oriented activities appropriate to PK-12 physical education/health. Emphasis on foundations of physical education/health including: exercise science, sport, careers, and challenges for the future.

title: Adaptive Physical Education
description: Emphasizes the principles and practices involved in providing activities for individuals who have disabilities or temporary limitations and cannot participate in or benefit from the regular activity program. This course is designed to prepare physical education teachers to provide safe, appropriate, and individualized accommodations to children with disabilities who are included in general physical education programs.

title: Blick Ii Lab/Content And Reading Methods
Social Studies Colloquium II
description: Continuation of application and integration of theories and practices of social studies education. Emphasis on rudimentary pedagogical strategies and how they can be incorporated in social studies education.

title: Teaching in a Multicultural Society
description: Application of multicultural understandings to teaching in a multicultural society. Strategies for working effectively with students to achieve educational equity.

title: Methods Of Teaching In The Secondary School
Content Area Literacies for All Learners
description: Development of content area literacies across disciplines to ensure meaningful access and achievement for all learners. Grounded in the science of reading and current literacy research, examine how language, reading, writing, and knowledge-building intersect within subject-specific contexts. Emphasis is placed on evidence-based instructional practices, including structured literacy and sheltered instruction, to strengthen disciplinary literacy and academic language development.

title: Undergraduate Research Experience
description: Open to students pursuing undergraduate research projects.

title: Content Area Methods In The Secondary School
Career Pathways in Family and Consumer Sciences Education
Block 2 Lab/Content Area Methods & Field Experience
description: Field-based experience to help the pre-professional teacher practice the incorporation of specific content area with reading methods and demonstrate application of technology into teaching and assessment in the secondary and middle schools.

title: Interpersonal Relations in the Schools
description: A didactic and experiential course designed to develop an understanding of human relations skills in the schools. Provides knowledge and skills necessary to work effectively with students, parents, and school personnel. Particular emphasis is on the basis for interpersonal relations in education, communication skills, the facilitative relationship, working with students in groups, and conducting meetings with parents and school personnel.

title: Social Studies Colloquium III
description: Application and integration of theories and practices of content disciplines into instruction. Emphasis on design and presentation of lessons through lenses of social studies disciplines.

title: Art Methods for Secondary and Middle Schools
description: Principles of teaching applied to art instruction in the secondary and middle schools including: motivation, organization of subject matter, lesson/unit planning, assessment and reporting, organization and management of the classroom, and methodology and materials of the secondary and middle schools.

title: Art Methods Practicum
description: This field-based experience provides the opportunity for students to plan, teach, and evaluate art education lessons within a placement at the middle or secondary level. This practicum also requires students to incorporate reading methods and apply instructional technology in their instruction.

title: Business Methods for Secondary and Middle Schools
description: Principles of teaching applied to business instruction in the secondary and middle schools including: motivation, organization of subject matter, lesson/unit planning, assessment and reporting, organization and management of the classroom, and methodology and materials of the secondary and middle schools.

title: Business Methods Practicum
description: This field-based experience provides the opportunity for students to plan, teach, and evaluate business education lessons within a placement at the middle or secondary level. This practicum also requires students to incorporate reading methods and apply instructional technology in their instruction.

title: Family and Consumer Science Methods for Secondary and Middle Schools
description: Principles of teaching applied to family and consumer sciences instruction in the secondary and middle schools including: motivation, organization of subject matter, lesson/unit planning, assessment and reporting, organization and management of the classroom, and methodology and materials of the secondary and middle schools.

title: Family and Consumer Science Methods Practicum
description: This field-based experience provides the opportunity for students to plan, teach, and evaluate family and consumer science education lessons within a placement at the middle or secondary level. This practicum also requires students to incorporate reading methods and apply instructional technology in their instruction.

title: Language Arts Methods for Secondary and Middle Schools
description: Principles of teaching applied to language arts instruction in the secondary and middle schools including: motivation, organization of subject matter, lesson/unit planning, assessment and reporting, organization and management of the classroom, and methodology and materials of the secondary and middle schools.

title: Language Arts Methods Practicum
description: This field-based experience provides the opportunity for students to plan, teach, and evaluate language arts education lessons within a placement at the middle or secondary level. This practicum also requires students to incorporate reading methods and apply instructional technology in their instruction.

title: Mathematics Methods for Secondary and Middle Schools
description: Principles of teaching applied to mathematics instruction in the secondary and middle schools including: motivation, organization of subject matter, lesson/unit planning, assessment and reporting, organization and management of the classroom, and methodology and materials of the secondary and middle schools.

title: Mathematics Methods Practicum
description: This field-based experience provides the opportunity for students to plan, teach, and evaluate mathematics education lessons within a placement at the middle or secondary level. This practicum also requires students to incorporate reading methods and apply instructional technology in their instruction.

title: Modern Language Methods for Secondary and Middle Schools
description: Principles of teaching applied to modern languages instruction in the secondary and middle schools including: motivation, organization of subject matter, lesson/unit planning, assessment and reporting, organization and management of the classroom, and methodology and materials of the secondary and middle schools.

title: Modern Language Methods Practicum
description: This field-based experience provides the opportunity for students to plan, teach, and evaluate modern language education lessons within a placement at the middle or secondary level. This practicum also requires students to incorporate reading methods and apply instructional technology in their instruction.

title: Science Methods for Secondary and Middle Schools
description: Principles of teaching applied to science instruction in the secondary and middle schools including: motivation, organization of subject matter, lesson/unit planning, assessment and reporting, organization and management of the classroom, and methodology and materials of the secondary and middle schools.

title: Science Methods Practicum
description: This field-based experience provides the opportunity for students to plan, teach, and evaluate science education lessons within a placement at the middle or secondary level. This practicum also requires students to incorporate reading methods and apply instructional technology in their instruction.

title: Social Studies Methods for Secondary and Middle Schools
description: Principles of teaching applied to social studies instruction in the secondary and middle schools including: motivation, organization of subject matter, lesson/unit planning, assessment and reporting, organization and management of the classroom, and methodology and materials of the secondary and middle schools.

title: Social Studies Methods Practicum
description: This field-based experience provides the opportunity for students to plan, teach, and evaluate social studies education lessons within a placement at the middle or secondary level. This practicum also requires students to incorporate reading methods and apply instructional technology in their instruction.

title: Physical Education/Health Methods for Secondary and Middle Schools
description: This course provides students opportunities to study, discuss, organize, and practice instructional methods designed specifically for secondary and middle school physical education/health programs. Emphasis will be placed on understanding of the objectives, characteristics, and activities available to satisfy student growth and development. The course addresses movement education, rhythm games of low organization, body manipulative, object manipulative, and specific sport skills.

title: Physical Education/Health Practicum for Secondary and Middle Schools
description: This field-based experience provides the opportunity for students to plan, teach, and evaluate physical education/health education lessons within a placement at the middle or secondary level. This practicum also requires students to incorporate reading methods and apply instructional technology in their instruction.

title: Technology Education Methods for Secondary and Middle Schools
description: This course addressed the principles of teaching applied to technology education instruction in the secondary and middle schools including motivation, organization of subject matter, lesson/unit planning, assessment and reporting, organization and management of the classroom, and methodology and materials of the secondary and middle schools. This course will also address the technology education curriculum, instructional practices for individual learners and learning, and college and career readiness practices for students.

title: Technology Education Practicum for Secondary and Middle Schools
description: This field-based experience provides the opportunity for students to plan, teach, and evaluate technology education lessons within a placement at the middle or secondary level. This practicum also requires students to incorporate reading methods and apply instructional technology in their instruction.

title: Evaluation And Emergency Management Of Athletic Injuries
Rehabilitation And Conditioning For Athletic Injuries
Seminar In Issues And Administration Of Athletic Train Prog
Art For Exceptional Children
description: Use of art courses and activities to meet the needs of the mentally retarded, physically impaired, emotionally disturbed, or gifted child.

title: Teaching Internship in Music
description: Observation and teaching under the direction of selected music teachers in elementary, middle level, and secondary school music programs.

title: Teaching Internship in Secondary School
description: Observation and teaching participation under the direction of selected secondary teachers.

title: Secondary Science Colloquium
description: This colloquium advances the interrelationships between the naturally occurring chemical elements and Earth Systems Science. Current scientific research in the field of geochemistry is applied to methods and content specific pedagogies to deepen sophistication of reasoning of chemistry concepts in secondary science instruction. The candidate will design, implement, assess, and evaluate a three-dimensional learning experience to address secondary chemistry misconceptions and promote reasoning of elemental properties and behaviors in the natural world. Authentic experiences comprise on-campus seminars, discussions, and a final presentation.

title: Coordination Techniques
description: Acquaints students with techniques in selecting, implementing, and coordinating occupational programs between the school and the business community.

title: Laboratory Techniques in Teaching Science
description: Rationale for laboratory in secondary school science. The design and implementation of laboratory activities and demonstrations in a high school science program.

title: Principles & Philosophy of Career & Technical Education
description: Principles of career and technical education in Kansas and other states and countries; principles and philosophy underlying such education, relation of career and technical education to school objectives and community, state, and national needs.

title: Program Planning in Career and Technical Education
description: The program development and planning process; development of guides for teaching and evaluating reimbursable secondary programs.

title: Adminisrtration And Supervision Of Vocational Education
Organization Problems In Teaching Farm Mechanics
Practica In Business And Office Occupations
Practica In Home Economics Related Occupations
German Culture In Second Language Learning
French-Speaking Cultures In Second Language Learning
Teaching In The Middle/Junior High School
Hispanic Cultures In Second Language Learning
In-Service Educ For Beginning Family And Consumer Sci Tchr
Young Farmer And Adult Farmer Education In Agriculture
The Social Studies Curriculum In The Secondary School
Exceptional Child in the Regular Classroom
description: Designed for general education teachers in meeting the needs of exceptional children. Support strategies for teachers and exceptional children in the mainstream of education will be explored.

title: Exceptional Student In The Secondary School
description: Designed for regular classroom teachers in meeting the needs of exceptional adolescents. Support strategies for teachers and exceptional students in the mainstream of education.

title: American Sign Language - Beginning
description: The manual alphabet and numbers in American Sign Language, and approximately 500-700 signs. Other topics will include the use of various sign language systems used in the United States, the history of sign language, deaf culture, and the discussion of education of deaf and hard of hearing students.

title: Independent Study/Special Education
description: Selected topics in professional education. Maximum of three hours applicable toward degree requirements.

title: Introduction to Autism Spectrum Disorder
description: Designed to prepare teacher candidates with the ability to recognize and build upon the strengths and needs of students with autism spectrum disorders (ASD). Employ various instructional strategies and locate appropriate educational materials for students with ASD.

title: American Sign Language - Intermediate
description: This course is a continuation of the Beginning Sign Language course. Approximately 400 additional signs will be taught. There will be discussion of grammar and syntax of American Sign Language. There will be emphasis on conversational use of sign language.  Other topics include the use of assistive technology and the use of sign language with various special populations.

title: Autism Spectrum Disorder
description: Designed to prepare teacher candidates with the ability to recognize and build upon the strengths and needs of students with autism spectrum disorders (ASD).  Employ various instructional strategies and locate appropriate educational materials for students with ASD.

title: Characteristics of Exceptionalities
description: Provide an overview of exceptionality as it pertains to students.  The processes of IEP development and characteristics of both high and low incidence populations will be examined.  Each area of exceptionality will be reviewed in terms of etiology, diagnosis, prevalence, remediation, and educational strategies.

title: Characteristics Of Emotional And Behavioral Disorders
Remediation Education For the Emotionally Disturbed
Intensive Interventions for Students with Emotional Disability
description: Educational planning assessment, instructional methods, curricular modifications, media and materials, teacher competencies, and model programs for students with emotional disability.

title: Intensive Interventions for Students with Dyscalculia and Other Math Difficulties
description: This course is designed to prepare educators to improve math achievement of students with or at-risk for significant math difficulties or disabilities such as dyscalculia.

title: Intensive Interventions for Students with Dyslexia and Other Reading Difficulties
description: This course is designed to prepare educators to improve the reading achievement of students with or at-risk for significant reading difficulties or disabilities such as dyslexia.

title: The Consulting Process in Special Education
description: A course to prepare special education teachers with skills for consulting effectively with classroom teachers, related services personnel, administrators, and parents about curriculum and program alternatives for exceptional children. Emphasis is upon developing collaborative consultation processes through communication, cooperation, and coordination techniques.

title: Introduction To Education Of The Gifted
Curriculum Development For the Mentally Retarded
Readings/Special Education
description: Readings in research and application in specialized areas in education.

title: Social Emotional Learning and Behavior Management Strategies
description: Theoretical and practical applications of behavior analysis in schools with emphasis on preventing and remediating behavior problems of students with disabilities.

title: Technology for Special Education
description: Designed to help special educators develop an awareness of assistive technology (AT) and related legislative guidelines, which can assist in the lives and learning of students receiving special education. This course will explore assistive technology devices and services and software appropriate for students with special needs. Internet resource and AT evaluation systems related to special education will also be covered.

title: Practicum I
description: Observation and participation in teaching individuals with exceptional educational needs under the supervision of selected teachers in special education.

title: Topics in Education
description: Examination of current topic in specialization of faculty. Varied topics offered each semester so course may be repeated.

title: Field Experiences in Special Education
description: Observation and supervised activities in schools, camps, clinics, or institutions related to student's area of special interest or preparation.

title: Problems in Special Education
description: Selected students are permitted to secure specialized training appropriate to the needs of the individual. The student's project may involve intensive library investigation in a special field or the collection and analysis of data pertinent to a given problem. All work is done independently under the direction of a faculty member. As many conferences are held as necessary to assure successful completion of a project necessary for the problem undertaken and consent of instructor.

title: Practicum II
description: Observation and participation in teaching individuals with exceptional educational needs under the supervision of selected teachers in special education.

title: Assessment in Special Education
description: A study of assessment procedures used in the pre-referral, identification, and instructional phases of program planning for students with special needs.

title: Administration of Special Education Programs
description: The study of administrative units for special education, placement procedures, federal and state legislation, and program reimbursement and funding.

title: The IEP Process in Special Education
description: EDSP 835 is a comprehensive course that focuses on the development and implementation of Individual Education programs (IEPs) in the context of special education. Throughout the course, students will gain a deep understanding of the legal framework surrounding Free Appropriate Public Education (FAPE) and the significance of creating educationally meaningful and legally sound IEPs. They will learn the fundamental components of an IEP and practice writing each section to meet the individual needs of students. Additionally, students will develop skills in assessing and analyzing IEPs, including the use of screening tools and assistive technology assessment. The course also covers transition programming in IEPs and provides opportunities for students to engage in comprehensive case studies. By the end of the course, students will have the knowledge and skills to create comprehensive and effective IEPs that support students with special needs.

title: Interventions: Low Incidence Special Education
description: Curriculum content, methods, and organization of educational programs for children and youth with low incidence special education needs.

title: Special Education in Secondary Schools
description: Educational perspectives in service delivery options, educational planning, general instructional approaches, learning strategies, and adaptations and modifications of instructional materials and settings for the secondary student in special education.

title: Special Education Programming: Parental Involvement
description: An in-depth consideration of the role of home and parents in the educational programming for exceptional children. Emphasis on practical and positive strategies used in working with parents.

title: Interventions: Early Childhood Special Education
description: Strategies, policies, and procedures for the education of young children (0-5 years of age) with disabilities. Areas of emphasis include Individual Service Plans, interagency collaboration, and specific strategies for parents and young children.

title: Transitions in Special Education
description: A study of transition models, curricula, assessment, career development, community resources and agencies, and materials.

title: Interventions: Autism Spectrum Disorders
description: Assessment, curriculum content, educational considerations, interventions, and organization of programs for children and youth with Autism Spectrum Disorders.

title: The Consulting Process In Special Education
College Students with Disabilities
description: This course is designed to familiarize higher education personnel with the increasing number of students with disabilities who now attend our institutions of higher education. The course will examine the legal foundations for students with disabilities in higher education, provide a framework for understanding the unique needs of students with learning disabilities, attention deficit disorders, mental health issues, as well as those on the autism spectrum, and examine the role of higher education personnel in helping these individuals be successful thriving college students.

title: Special Education Law
description: Analysis of legislation, litigation, and administrative rulings related to special education. The course will emphasize the development of legally sound policies and procedures to ensure that schools provide an educationally meaningful and legally correct education for students with disabilities.

title: Practicum in Education of Individuals with Low Incidence Special Education Needs
description: Observation and participation in teaching individuals with low incidence special educational needs under the supervision of selected teachers in Special Education programs.

title: Seminar in Special Education
description: Intensive discussion of research or problems of current professional interest based on study of pertinent original literature.

title: Externship in Special Education
description: The course provides a forum for self-directed development of competencies in Special Education. Students conduct self-assessment and develop strategies for ongoing professional development.

title: Advanced Practicum:Autism Spectrum Disorders
description: Observation and participation in teaching individuals with Autism Spectrum Disorders under the supervision of selected teachers in Special Education programs.

title: Advanced Seminar in Special Education
description: These seminars will critically consider recent research in the designated fields. The emphasis will be upon individual studies and small group interaction. Enrollment is restricted to those students who have been admitted to the doctoral program in education and who have completed substantial amounts of graduate study in the designated fields.

title: Internship/Special Education
description: Studies of and field experiences in the development of programs in cooperating schools and educational or related agencies under the supervision of College of Education graduate faculty members. A maximum of 6 credit hours may be chosen from the areas listed.

title: Research in Special Education
description: Individual investigation in the field of a student's specialization.

title: Workshop in the Vocational Agriculture Curriculum I
Matrix Methods Applied To Electrical Engineering
Advanced Topics In Digital Signal Processing
Matrix Methods Applied To Electrical Engineering
Special Topics In Electronic Engineering Technology
