description: Extensive exploration of a specific problem in political thought. American government, comparative politics, international relations, or public administration.

title: Political Parties And Elections
description: Origins, structure and function of political parties. Dynamics of the two-party system. Roles of third parties. Analysis of election results and voting behavior.

title: Interest Groups and Public Opinion
description: Group theory and politics. Structure, internal politics, and techniques of interest groups and their impact on public policy. Formation and measurement of public opinion.

title: The American Presidency
description: The presidency as an institution, its evolution, congressional relationships, executive organization.

title: Gender and Politics
description: Analysis of the role of gender in political behavior, including gender differences in voting and political participation, legal and cultural restrictions on women's rights and political activity, and women's liberation and other gender-based political movements.

title: Administrative Law
description: Legal analysis of the rule-making, adjudicatory, and enforcement functions of administrative agencies, with emphasis on constitutional framework, judicial review, requirements of procedural fairness, and rights of public employees.

title: Political Participation in the United States
description: A broad overview of the various tools of democracy; public opinion, political parties, interest groups, and elections.

title: Local Government Law
description: Explores the dynamic relationship between states and cities. Major issues may include budget shortfalls, social issues, and sprawl. Focus on the legal framework of state and city powers, including the forms of government, state preemption, and home rule theory and practice.

title: The Legislative Process
description: Legislative decision-making in modern democracy with emphasis on the United States, the concept of representation, and political behavior of participants in the legislative process.

title: The Judicial Process
description: The structure, process, and politics of the American judicial system. Analysis of important issues concerning law and courts.

title: Constitutional Law
description: Principles of the American political system as prescribed by the Constitution and interpreted by Supreme Court decisions, with emphasis on the institutions and powers of the national government, federalism, and property rights.

title: The Constitution, Civil Rights and Civil Liberties
description: The Constitution as a limitation on governmental power, with emphasis on Supreme Court decisions defining fundamental civil rights and liberties.

title: Urban Politics
description: Fundamental problems of political power and decision making in urban suburban governmental settings.

title: Comparative Agricultural Politics and Policy
description: Comparative examination of agricultural politics and policy with emphasis on decision making processes and the socio-political impacts of agricultural policy.

title: State and Local Government
description: The United States system of federalism with emphasis on a comparative analysis of the government and politics of the fifty states and their subdivisions.

title: Western European Politics
description: Comparative analysis of British, French, German, and Italian democracies, political systems.

title: South Asian Politics
description: Analysis of selected political systems of South Asia.

title: East Asian Politics
description: Comparative analysis of selected political systems in East Asia, including consideration of the democratization process in the region.

title: African Politics
description: Comparative analysis of selected political systems of sub-Sahara Africa, including consideration of problems of nationalism and development.

title: Eastern and Central European Politics
description: Examination of comparative politics and policy in the countries of Easter and Central Europe.

title: Civic Engagement
description: Seminar on the role of voluntary organizations and civic groups in American political life and the factors that lead citizens to participate in both electoral and non-electoral activities.

title: Development Policy and Administration
description: Comparative examination of development policy, politics, and administration.

title: Politics Of Russia And The Former Soviet Union
description: An overview of the major factors influencing the evolution of politics in Russia and the Soviet Union. Special emphasis is placed on the problems associated with the transition from a communist to a non-communist more democratic policy.

title: Ethnic Conflict
description: Ethnic Conflict in Comparative Prospective.  Comparative examination of ethnic conflicts across regions with the emphasis on Africa.

title: International Conflict
description: Examines the nature of international political conflicts and the types of such conflicts.

title: Global Human Rights
description: Introduction to human rights in the global system with a focus on the philosophical foundations, historical evolution, institutional development, and important actors in global human rights.

title: International Politics of Europe
description: Relationships among the countries of Europe since World War II. With emphasis on efforts to create a more unified European Community. Among the organizations that will be studied are the former Warsaw Pact, NATO, the European Parliament, and the European Union.

title: International Law
description: Theories of international law, and general problems, such as: recognition, responsibility, war crimes, sources, evidence, codification, and settlement of disputes.

title: International Organization
description: Structure, functions, values, and effectiveness of international organizations with emphasis on the United Nations, European Union, and other regional arrangements.

title: International Politics of South Asia
description: Study of regional problems of South Asia and of international roles and foreign policies of South Asian states.

title: International Politics of the Middle East
description: Consideration of the Arab-Israeli conflict, inter-Arab relations, foreign policies of Middle Eastern states, and the impact of the major foreign powers on the area.

title: International Politics of Africa
description: The course analyzes contemporary relations among African countries including economic and political security, border claims, formal and informal economic relations, and regional groupings. The course also examines the relations between African countries, the United States and the former Soviet Union, and between African countries and the former colonial rulers.

title: International Politics of Latin America
description: Analysis of international relations of Latin America. Examining theoretical approaches to the study of Latin America's relations, United States Latin American relations, and comparative foreign policies of Latin American states. This course also explores special topics such as regional integration and geopolitics.

title: Ancient and Medieval Political Thought
description: Systematic study of ideas about law, politics, and government of great philosophers of Western civilization from Greek antiquity to the sixteenth century.

title: Early Modern Political Thought
description: Study of the development of Western political thought from the sixteenth century to the twentieth century.

title: American Political Thought
description: Political ideas underlying the American union, including the doctrine of rights, the nature of union, liberty, property, and democracy.

title: Law, Politics  and Literature
description: Classic and contemporary works of literature, including novels, plays, short stories, and poems, are used to explore themes in law and politics.

title: Late Modern Political Thought
description: Study of contemporary political ideas and social thought.

title: Religion and Politics
description: Focuses on religious life in America and its changing relationship to politics and government. Examination of the American founding as it relates to church/ state issues, the controversy over meaning of the First Amendment's establishment and free exercise clauses, and contemporary political agendas of mainline and evangelical churches.

title: Research Methods in Political Science
description: Principles of research design, of measurement of political phenomena, and of methods for collecting and analyzing political data.

title: Computer and Quantitative Analysis in Political Science
description: Advanced data management, data analysis, and computing skills involved in conducting political science and public policy research.

title: Public Administration and Society
description: Introduction to the discipline and profession of public administration, which may include the cultural, constitutional, institutional, organizational, and ethical context of public administration. Introduction to central issues facing public administrators, using the related perspectives of management, politics, and law.

title: Comparative Administrative Systems
description: Comparative analysis of public administration concepts and the morphology of administrative systems. Included are U.S., British,and French models and attempts by Third World countries to adapt these to their local cultures.

title: Public Personnel Administration
description: Personnel aspects of administration at all levels of government, including recruitment, selection, discrimination law, pay, and motivation. Emphasis on to those features unique to the public sector, e.g. civil service systems, public unions, and public sector ethics law.

title: Policy Analysis and Evaluation
description: Methods of policy analysis and evaluation. Includes a discussion of the relationship between public policy and the distribution of values in society. Students analyze policies in an area of their choice; e.g., agriculture, business, health, income, trade.

title: Administrative Ethics
description: Ethical issues, approaches, and strategies in public service.

title: Game Theory for Political Scientists
description: Introduction to game theoretic models as applied to problems in American politics, international relations and comparative politics.

title: Comparative Civil Military Relations
description: A look at civil-military relations in the United States, Russia, Germany, and Spain. Primary focus will be on understanding the political role of the military in totalitarian, authoritarian, and democratic states.

title: State and Local Government Management
description: Examines the main operations and important issues in administering governments at the state and local levels and the similarities and differences of their environment, institutions, organizations, and processes.

title: Public Organizational Theory
description: Theories of the structure and mission of public organizations. Explores the use of analytical questioning of various theories, to solve organizational problems.

title: Strategic Management of Public and Nonprofit Organizations
description: Strategic management in the public sector of democratic societies. Provides a set of tools and research skills to focus thinking, judgment and decision making in order to act strategically.

title: Public Budgeting
description: Budgeting as apart of our political system and as a fiscal process assists in allocating scarce resources. Overview of the budgetary decision-making process and the various budgetary approaches.

title: Public Finance
description: Economics of the public sector, addressing the fundamental issue of the appropriate role of government activity in a market economy. Behavioral consequences of government policy. Model construction to explain real world government policy making.

title: Intergovernmental Relations
description: Evolution of the intergovernmental system in the United States and internationally.  Emphasis on federal, state, and local interrelationships.

title: Nonprofit Financial Management
description: Introduction to the major issues in nonprofit management. Highlights the unique nature of the nonprofit sector with government (public) and business (for-profit) sectors. Issues may include governance roles and responsibilities, ethics and risk management, financial management and resource generation, managing staff and volunteers, performance measurements, and the emerging trends that threaten nonprofits' tax-exempt status.

title: The Professional Diplomat and Foreign Policy Formulation
description: Present day foreign policy formulation in the United States government, including the role of professional diplomats and foreign affairs specialists in the State Department and embassies abroad, as well as within other United States government agencies.

title: International Political Economy
description: The course introduces students to the political and historical dimensions of the international economy, dimensions that include trade, monetary systems, foreign investment, aid, dependency, and global interdependence. This course also examines various theories and practices of the international system, the state, bureaucracies, interest groups, international organizations, bargaining processes, and distributive norms.

title: Engaged Citizens: Public Policy Issues and Processes
description: Examines the teaching of the workings of constitutional government through the lens of the public policymaking processes and the roles of individual voters, organized publics, the media, and state and local governments in that process.

title: Teaching the Theoretical Foundations of Constitutional Government
description: Examines the theories (e.g., natural rights, classical republicanism, and constitutionalism) and theorists (e.g., Aristotle, Locke, Hobbes, and Montesquieu) that most influenced constitutional thinking in the United States.  Explores how to teach effectively about these theories and theorists.

title: Teaching the Historical Origins of Constitutional Government
description: Examines seminal ideas, documents, and events in the creation of the United States Constitution such as problems with the Articles of Confederation, the characteristics of good government, the debates at the Philadelphia Convention, the Federalist Papers, and the Antifederalist Papers.  Explores how to teach effectively these ideas, document, and events.

title: Teaching the Development of Constitutional Principals
description: Examines the development of political ideas and constitutional thinking since the Founding.  Focuses on how the Bill of Rights, the amendment process, judicial review, and the Fourteenth Amendment are used to empower and limit government.  Explores how to teach effectively about developments in constitutional thought.

title: Teaching the Institutions of Government
description: Examines the powers, limits, and development of political institutions such as Congress, the President, the Supreme Court, and political parties. Explores how to teach effectively about these institutions.

title: Readings in Political Science
description: Directed reading and discussion of a selected topic in political science.

title: Teaching Citizenship
description: Examines development of the rights, responsibilities, and challenges of citizenship in the United States. Explores how to teach effectively about citizenship.

title: Teaching the Constitutional Principles of America’s Founders
description: Develops constitutional principles; explores perspectives of constitutional interpretation, change, and preservation; raises questions about the immediate and enduring significance of America's founding principles and documents; and introduces sound pedagogical practices to teach about them.

title: Methods and Materials in Civic Education
description: Focuses on the pedagogical aspects of civic education and engagement such as research-based approaches to concept attainment, skill development, discussion, and deliberation as well as analysis of curriculum materials and programs that apply these pedagogies.

title: Problems in Political Science
description: Students will complete a research project and prepare an original paper under the supervision of a faculty member.

title: Topics in Political Science
description: Extensive exploration of a specific problem in political thought. American government, comparative politics, international relations, and public administration.

title: Pro-Seminar in Political Science
description: Study and analysis in various areas of the discipline with emphasis on critical evaluation of political conflicts and issues.

title: Seminar/Scope And Methodology Of Political Science
description: Exploration of theoretical foundations of political science, and critique of various analytical models in the study of political phenomena; construction and application of research designs and techniques.

title: Seminar/American Government Problems
description: This seminar introduces students to the methods and the theories used to study American politics. Topics to be addressed include the constitution and the American founding, political and electoral processes, institutional structure and function, and public policy. Exams and a seminar paper are required.

title: Security Studies Methodology
description: An exploration of commonly used research methods in history and political science. Students will investigate quantitative and qualitative humanities and social sciences methodology through selected readings and limited research exercises.

title: Seminar/International Politics
description: The seminar introduces students to the different school of thought in international relations as well as the different methods used to study the subject. It also analyzes middle range theories on subjects such as the causes of interstate conflict, the democratic peace, and foreign policy decision-making. Exams and a seminar paper are required.

title: Foundations of Security Studies
description: An interdisciplinary introduction to the major historical developments and scholarly approaches in the fields of military history, international relations, and security studies.

title: International Security
description: Surveys traditional issues in the study of international security such as the causes of war, the utility of deterrence, coercion, and engagement, international intervention, and the proliferation of weapons of mass destruction. Attention will also be placed on security and conflict in important regional settings, such as East Asia and the Middle East.

title: Terrorism and Transnational Security Issues
description: Introduction to the general aspects of terrorism and counter-terrorism in order to build transnational security. The course examines these issues in terms of forms, causal factors, goals, and consequences. The main objective is to understand terrorism as part of new international security issues such as failed states, environmental degradation, demographic explosion and poverty, conflict in developing world, post-conflict reconstruction, income distribution, humanitarian emergencies, and globalization.

title: Research Design in Security Studies
description: An exploration of research methods in history and political science culminating in a piece of original research in Security Studies.

title: Seminar in Political Thought
description: A study of the history of political philosophy as well as contemporary scholarship. Political philosophy as a subdiscipline of political science and its relevance for other subdisciplines as well as for leadership and administration. Class discussion and research training will focus on textual analysis, or exegesis. Course requirements include a major research paper.

title: Public Administration Capstone
description: Students integrate previous classroom learning with practical experience. Operation of a government agency on a day-to-day basis. The meaning of public service and communication in the public sector.

title: Seminar/Comparative Politics
description: The seminar introduces students to the methods and theories of comparative politics as well as critical analysis of various topics in political science such as institution and institutional change, social movements and state building, regime and development, political parties and electoral processes, policy making and political performance.  Course requirements include two exams, book reviews, research design, and a major research paper.

title: Seminar/Comparative Ideologies
description: This seminar provides a graduate level exploration of ideologies such as liberalism, conservatism, socialism, communism, and fascism. The tension between ideology and philosophy is also examined.

title: US Foreign Policy
description: Introduction to the methods and theories used to study American foreign policy. Students will develop an understanding of the historical development of American foreign policy, the actors and institutions responsible for making foreign policy, and the study of a range of more specific foreign policy topics, including the use of military force, security cooperation, military alliances, trade, monetary policy, human rights, and foreign aid.

title: Advanced Research Methods l
description: The first of a two course methods sequence in security studies. Surveys the role of theory, hypotheses testing, operationalization and measurement, and research design in social science. Case study and historical methods will be given particular attention. Students will also be provided with a brief introduction to quantitative social science analysis.

title: Advanced Research Methods ll
description: The course is the second in the sequence of two method courses in security studies. It emphasized quantitative analysis in political science and covers regression analysis, violation of regression assumptions, and logit models. The course also introduces students to time series, decision theory, and game theory.

title: Readings in Security Studies
description: Directed reading and discussion of a selected topic in international security.

title: Topics in Security Studies
description: Exploration of a specific issue in one of the fields or areas of international security.

title: PhD Research in Security Studies
description: PhD research culminating in completed PhD dissertation.

title: FAA Pilot Certificate Validation
description: Flight and knowledge validation of FAA pilot certificates.

title: Private Pilot Flight Lab Solo
description: This course provides students with basic flight knowledge and fundamentals for solo flight as outlined by the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA). The successful completion of this course is the solo stage check.

title: Private Pilot Flight Lab Certification
description: A continuation of PPIL 108 after solo flight and stage check. Upon completion of the course, students will meet the requirements for the FAA Private Pilot Certificate.

title: Private Pilot
description: The subject areas necessary for completion and passing of the FAA Private Pilot Knowledge Test are presented.

title: Professional Instrument Pilot
description: A study of the procedures, regulations, and techniques required to safely fly in instrument meteorological conditions within our national airspace system. The course will prepare the student to pass the FAA Instrument Airplane Knowledge Test.

title: Private Pilot Flight Lab
description: An introduction of the fundamentals of flight. Solo flights to include all flight operations and maneuvers necessary for meeting the aeronautical experience for the FAA Private Pilot Certificate.

title: Professional Instrument Pilot Flight Lab
description: Instructional flight training necessary to maneuver the aircraft safely in actual or simulated instrument meteorological conditions within the national airspace system.

title: VFR Pilot Proficiency Lab
description: Instruction and flight training necessary to safely operate an aircraft to meet the Federal Aviation Regulations. This course provides the student the opportunity to review and demonstrate proficiency to satisfactorily meet the FAA regulations for the current ratings held.

title: IFR Pilot Proficiency Lab
description: Instruction, simulator, and flight training necessary to safely operate an aircraft, to meet and maintain the Federal Aviation Regulations currency requirement of Instrument Competency, and maintain instrument currency and proficiency.

title: Aviation Safety
description: This course provides an introduction to the field of aviation safety with an emphasis on promoting a safety culture.  Various safety programs and their relevance in the field of aviation are discussed.  Students will examine numerous accident reports and discuss safety issues facing the aviation industry today.

title: Professional Commercial Pilot
description: The subject areas necessary for passing the FAA Commercial Pilot Knowledge Test.

title: Professional Commercial Pilot Flight Lab I
description: Instructional cross country flight training necessary to maneuver the aircraft safely in actual or simulated instrument meteorological conditions within the national airspace system.

title: Professional Commercial Pilot Flight Lab II
description: An introduction to complex airplane operations and a review of those operations required of a commercial pilot. The completion of this course readies the student to take the commercial FAA Practical test.

title: Extended Cross Country
description: A characteristic of aviation is that aircraft can cover a large geographic area. Aircraft operations in other geographic areas may differ greatly from a student's training base. The experience of long-range navigation can be a great learning experience as well as a confidence booster. Selection of a destination that allows the student to increase their knowledge of aviation also aids in producing a more well-rounded, responsible professional.

title: Mountain Flying
description: A characteristic of aviation is that aircraft can cover a large geographic area. Aircraft operations in mountainous areas may differ greatly from a student's training. The experience of reduced aircraft performance caused by high-density altitudes can be a great training into operations with aircraft exhibiting marginal performance. Learning of weather patterns, hypoxia, and survival allows the student to increase their knowledge of aviation and also aids in producing a more well-rounded, responsible professional.

title: Altitude Chamber
description: This course offers a 1-day aviation physiology course for civil aviation pilots through the CAMI's Aeromedical Education Division in Oklahoma City, OK. In addition to the basic academic contents, this course offers practical demonstrations of rapid decompression and hypoxia in a hypobaric chamber, as well as a practical demonstration of spatial disorientation. Upon completion of the course students will receive a certificate noting that they have completed the FAA's Physiological Training course. The FFA requires a current Aviation Medical Certificate.

title: Glider Towing
description: Instruction and flight training necessary to obtain a logbook endorsement in the techniques and procedures for the safe towing of gliders.

title: Single Engine Seaplane Transition
description: Instruction and flight training necessary to add the seaplane rating to the commercial pilot certificate.  Course requires a one-week trip to a specified contract training location.

title: Preventive Maintenance
description: This course will give the student hands-on experience with the maintenance tasks allowed under FAR 43 entitled preventive maintenance.

title: Commercial Pilot Helicopter Flight Lab I
Commercial Pilot Helicopter Flight Lab ll
Private Pilot Glider Transition
description: Instruction and flight training in the design, performance, operating characteristics, and flight proficiency for the safe operation of glider aircraft that will lead to an Private Pilot Glider certificate. This course provides students the opportunity to enhance and develop their skills in this segment of aviation.

title: Commercial Pilot Glider Transition
description: Instruction and flight training in the design, performance, operating characteristics, and flight proficiency for the safe operation of glider aircraft that will lead to a commercial pilot glider certificate. This course provides students the opportunity to enhance and develop their skills in this segment of aviation.

title: Multi Engine Ground School
description: Ground instruction covering multi-engine aircraft to develop the aeronautical knowledge to meet the ground school requirements for a multi-engine land class rating.

title: Multi-Engine Flight Lab
description: Flight instruction and experience in a multi-engine aircraft to develop the aeronautical skills to meet the requirements to add a multi-engine land class rating to the student's existing pilot certificate.

title: Certified Flight Instructor Helicopter Flight Lab
Multi-Engine Crew Coordination
description: Instruction, simulator and flight training necessary to operate a multi-engine aircraft as a member of a crew. Enhances multi-engine, instrument and cross country skills.

title: Tailwheel Transition
description: Instruction and flight training in the design, performance, operating characteristics, and flight proficiency for the safe operation of conventional-geared (tailwheel) aircraft that will lead to an endorsement allowing the student to act as pilot-in-command. This course provides students the opportunity to enhance and develop their skills in this segment of aviation.

title: Complex/High Performance Transition
description: Instruction and flight training in the design, performance, operating characteristics, and flight proficiency for the safe operation of complex/high performance aircraft that will lead to endorsements allowing the student to act as pilot-in-command. This course provides students the opportunity to enhance and develop their skills.

title: Certificated Flight Instructor-Airplane Ground School
description: Instruction techniques, practices, and procedures necessary to provide skill in organizing and presenting lessons. This course will prepare the student for the FAA Certificated Flight Instructor-Airplane practical test and the Flight Instructor Airplane (FIA) Knowledge Test.

title: Certified Flight Instructor Ground School
description: Instruction techniques, practices, and procedures necessary to provide skill in organizing and presenting lessons. This course will prepare the student for the FAA Certified Instructor Knowledge Test.

title: Certified Flight Instructor Flight Lab
description: The needed flight skills and proper display of teaching ability will be emphasized. The demonstration of flight maneuvers with recognition of common errors in students performing the demonstrated maneuvers is stressed.

title: Certified Flight Instructor Glider Flight Lab
Prop Jet Familiarization And Introduction Lab
High Altitude Endorsement/Propjet Familiar & Intro Lab
Turbine Transition
description: To provide required knowledge to meet FAA requirements to operate as second-in-command in KSU transportation aircraft. Covers systems performance and emergency procedures for turbine aircraft. In conjunction with simulator training, students will be qualified to operate as co-pilots on university transportation trips.

title: Airline Transport Pilot Rating
description: Provides the student with the aeronautical knowledge necessary to prepare for the FAA Airline Transport Pilot Knowledge Test. The demonstration of flight maneuvers, with recognition of proper control of emergencies in compliance of the Airline Transport Pilot Practical Test Standards will be stressed.

title: Crew Resource Management I
description: This course involves using all available resources for a safe and efficient flight.  Aircraft crew performance is reviewed to include the background and philosophy of Crew Resource Management (CRM) communication, decision behavior, team building, workload management, and situational awareness. Students accomplish two flight scenarios using modern advanced jet aircraft training device.

title: Problems in Aviation
description: To provide the student an opportunity to apply aviation education to the improvement of skills previously learned as designated by the instructor.

title: Introduction to Upset Training and Recovery
description: Instruction and flight training necessary to develop an understanding and flight proficiency in basic upset flight attitudes. This course provides the student the opportunity to develop a better understanding of aircraft and safety of flight in other than normal flight attitudes.

title: Be 58 Multi Engine Familiarization and Proficiency Lab
Crew Resource Management  II
description: Utilizing a modern advanced jet aircraft training device, special emphasis is placed on systems knowledge, Flight Management Systems (FMS) programming, flight profiles and cockpit flows.  Students fly Line Orientated Flight Training (LOFT) missions and self-critique their performance.

title: Certified Instrument Flight Instructor Ground School
description: Instrument instruction techniques, practices, and procedures necessary to provide skills in organizing and presenting lessons in instrument flying procedures. This course will prepare the student for the FAA Certified Instrument Flight Instructor knowledge test.

title: Certified Instrument Flight Instructor Lab
description: Instrument instruction techniques, practices, and procedures necessary to provide skills in organizing and presenting lessons in instrument flying procedures. This course will prepare the student for the FAA Certified Instrument Flight Instructor practical test.

title: Certified Instrument Flight Instructor Helicopter Flight Lab
Certified Multi-Engine Flight Instructor Ground School
description: Provides the student with the aeronautical knowledge necessary to meet the requirements for the addition of an airplane, multi-engine rating to the flight instructor certificate.

title: Certified Multi-Engine Flight Instructor Lab
description: Provides the student with the aeronautical skills and experience necessary to meet the requirements for the addition of an airplane, multi-engine rating to the flight instructor certificate.

title: Introduction To Parks And Recreation Profession
Use Of Natural Resources For Leisure
Management Of Water Resources For Leisure
International Politics of the Middle East
description: International Politics of the Middle East

title: Advanced Research Methods II: Data Analysis
description: Advanced Research Methods II: Data Analysis

title: General Psychology
description: An introductory survey of the general content areas of psychology, including methods, data and principles.

title: General Psychology (Honors)
description: An introductory survey of the general content areas of psychology, including methods, data and principles.

title: Drugs and Behavior
description: Effects of drugs on human performance, cognition, and physiological processes will be discussed and the empirical evidence surveyed and critically evaluated in relation to both use and abuse of drugs in society.

title: Psychology of Childhood and Adolescence
description: Survey of behavioral development from birth through adolescence.

title: Innovative Studies in Psychology
description: Topics selected in consultation with the instructor. To be used for interdisciplinary and innovative approaches to psychological topics.

title: Controversial Issues in Psychology Research
description: Covers selected topics across areas of physiological psychology, human development, cognitive and social psychology, and mental health disorders and their treatment. Students will explore in greater depth several controversial ideas and concepts in contemporary psychology.

title: Experimental Methods in Psychology
description: Introduction to basic principles of research design and scientific methodology as applied to psychological sciences.

title: Experimental Methods Laboratory
description: Application of experimental design and data analysis emphasized in the psychological literature, procedures, and reporting of research findings.

title: Introduction to Behavioral Neuroscience
description: A general overview of human brain function using case studies as a primary means for understanding the relationship between behavior and brain mechanisms.

title: Practicum in Teaching Psychology
description: Supervised experience in presentation of psychological concepts in various classes. May be taken only with approval of the instructor of a psychology class under whose supervision the student will obtain this experience.

title: Judgement & Decision Making
description: Covers the academic field of judgment and decision making, its major methods, results, and controversies. Examines the generality of experimental results across various domains including gambling, clinical prediction, perception of randomness, and legal and medical decision making. Focus on practical analytical skills for decision making that are useful in everyday life and work.

title: Forensic Psychology
description: Survey of psychological principles and facts in areas such as perception, cognition, neuropsychology, social psychology, personality, and decision making, as they apply to forensic psychology. Topics include: polygraph (lie detection), eyewitness testimony, jury decision making, and profiling.

title: Applications of Memory
description: Examination of the applications of memory in such diverse areas as courtroom testimony, expert performance, mnemonic procedures, and advertising. Relevant theories and research in each area are examined.

title: Cognitive Psychology
description: A survey of the manner in which people extract and use relevant information from their environment as a basis for behavior. Topics may include memory storage and retrieval, attention, imagery, mnemonic devices, decision making, and other cognitive processes.

title: Cognitive Neuroscience
description: The foundations of the neural bases of cognition and human behavior. A focus on neuroanatomy, hemispheric specialization, brain development, and higher-order cognitive functions, such as language, executive functions, attention, learning, and memory. Additional topics include cutting-edge brain imaging techniques (e.g., electroencephalography, functional MRI, and brain stimulation), lesion studies and examinations of brain injuries and other neurological disorders.

title: Principles of Neuroscience
description: A more in-depth course on brain-behavior interactions. Topics include: neuroanatomy, neuron transmission, sensory systems, emotions and stress, learning and memory, sleep, language and neural disorders.

title: Principles of Learning
description: Introduction to the principles of learning and their relevance to the understanding of the behavior of animals and humans.

title: Fundamentals of Perception and Sensation
description: Empirical and theoretical approaches to phenomena of sensation and perception.

title: Honors Tutorial in Psychology
description: Individual directed research and study of a topic in psychology, normally as a preliminary to writing a senior honors thesis.

title: Honors Project
description: Open only to Arts & Sciences students who are active members of the University Honors Program.

title: Junior Seminar in Psychology
description: Discussion of professional, research, and educational methods and objectives in psychology. Acquaints psychology majors with psychology as a profession, and with the various options available to them at various levels of training.

title: Introduction to Psychopathology
description: An introductory study of behavior pathologies, with emphasis on their etiology and treatment.

title: Introduction to Behavior Modification
description: Study of the principles of behavior modification and applications to human behavior. Emphasis on the learning principles and research in behavior modification.

title: Introduction to Psychology Applied to Work
description: Introduction to the basic theories and practices used by industrial and organizational psychologists to enhance individual and organizational effectiveness and well-being. Topics include personnel selection, training, performance management, job attitudes, work motivation, leadership, occupational health, and group processes at work.

title: Introduction to Health Psychology
description: Psychosocial factors relevant to general health maintenance, recovery from disease or injury, and the achievement of health. Topics include stress-management techniques, personality characteristics associated with disease, cognitive-emotional effects of diet and exercise, and theories of pain and pain management. Concepts of prevention and behavioral medicine are also included.

title: Lifespan Personality Development
description: Theories and research in the development of personality from infancy through old age. Origins of personality in heredity and early experience, socialization practices, life crises and choices at various stages throughout life, and problems of aging.

title: Psychology of Mass Communications
description: The psychological effects of mass communication on behavior and thought, including advertising, stereotyping of women and minorities, effects on children, violence and sex in the media, effects of news on behavior, and the promotion of prosocial behavior through the media.

title: Social Psychology
description: Psychology of the individual in society. Survey of empirical studies and theoretical models of social perception, attitudes, and social behavior (e.g., attribution, ethnic and gender prejudice, conformity). Relationship of these topics to personal and media influence, social mores, and social systems is also included.

title: Cross-Cultural Psychology
description: Introduction to general theories and methods related to understanding the role of culture in human psychology. Focus is on how human psychology and behaviors both differ and are consistent across cultures, and sometimes within subcultures. Topics may include perception, cognitive processes, development, emotions and nonverbal behavior, personality and gender, social relationships, judgments of morality, and mental illness.

title: Psychology of Gender
description: Investigation of psychological processes involved in gender with emphasis on development and major life events. Topics include physiology, early socialization into sex/gender roles, friendship, achievement, motivation, sexuality, romantic relationships, family development, work, and mental health.

title: Consumer Psychology
description: Survey of psychological principles and facts in perception, learning, attitude formation, personality, etc., as they apply to behavior of consumers.

title: Advanced Psychological Research Methods
description: Students will read primary literature, design research studies, employ the instruments and technology currently used in psychological research.

title: Multicultural Psychology
description: Introduce and familiarize students with the concept of multicultural psychology.

title: The Psychology of Ethnic Humor
description: Reviews the structure, dynamics, and social functions of ethnic humor.

title: Psychological Testing
description: Principles of psychological testing in industrial, clinical/counseling, and research environments. Topics include technical issues such as reliability, validity, norming, selection, placement, discrimination, etc. Also covers procedures for selecting, administering, and interpreting psychological tests.

title: Personnel Psychology
description: An exploration of the industrial aspect of the industrial/organizational psychology discipline provides an overview of human behaviors and psychological principles in personnel and selection contexts. Topics include recruiting, selecting, assessing, and training personnel; evaluating job performance; conducting job analyses; and implementing human capital strategies.

title: Gender Issues in the Workplace
description: Psychological experiences of women and men in the world of work, with emphasis on traditional and nontraditional sex-role behavior, sexual discrimination and harassment, and relevant socialization experiences.

title: Psychology of the Workplace
description: An exploration of the organizational aspect of the industrial/organizational psychology discipline provides coverage of relationships between individuals, groups, and organizations; how organizational factors contribute to individual behavior; and how individuals affect groups and organizational functioning. Emphasis on traditional topics such as work motivation, job satisfaction, and other attitudes, leadership, communication, socialization, organization, and job design.

title: Occupational Health Psychology
description: Survey of occupational health issues for juniors and seniors. Provides students with a background in psychologically related health issues occurring at work. The basic processes for identification, prevention, treatment, and well being of workers will be identified and explored.

title: Psychopharmacology
description: Survey of basic principles of neural communication and pharmacology, the etiology and bio-behavioral bases of substance abuse, and chemical treatments of psychiatric disorders.

title: Psychology of Sexual Behavior
description: Study of psychological determinants and consequences of human sexual behavior; roles of personality, attitudinal and emotional factors will be emphasized.

title: Group Psychotherapy
description: Principles of psychoeducational training, strategies, and group norms. The group experience is practiced within the classroom, offering experience as both a group member and a group leader through role-playing and experiential exercises. Research and program planning are conducted to create and guide a psychoeducational group.

title: Basic Concepts in Clinical Psychology
description: Critical analysis of the profession. Review of theoretical and empirical bases of such areas as intelligence and its measurement, personality and diagnosis, psychotherapy, and other modes of behavioral change.

title: Psychology Internship Readiness
description: Exploration of the efficacy of decisions and actions within a professional internship. The topics of professional communication, dress, engagement, timeliness, and conflict resolution will prepare the student for an internship. Additionally, ethics, confidentiality, the duty to warn and self-care impacting the delivery of human services will be explained.

title: Psychology Internship in Mental Health
description: Supervised internship in an agency or institutional setting in the application of psychological techniques to individuals, groups, or organizations. Regular supervision emphasizes relationship between theory and application and the evaluation of outcomes.

title: Topics in Psychology
description: Topics selected in consultation with the instructor, covering interdisciplinary and innovative approaches to psychological topics.

title: Behavioral Neuroscience Lab Skills
description: Provides practical training in the foundations of Behavioral Neuroscience, which is the study of the biological basis of behavior in animals. Students will engage in theoretical work and practical experiments addressing behavioral and physiological processes as well as relationships between different biological and behavioral processes in various species.

title: Cognitive Neuroscience Lab Skills
description: Provides practical training in the foundations of Cognitive Neuroscience, which is the science of the human brain and behavior. Students will engage in theoretical work and practical experiments addressing behavioral, cognitive, and physiological processes as well as relationships between different biological and behavioral processes.

title: Research in Neuroscience
description: Investigation of selected problems within the area of neuroscience.

title: Advanced Social Psychology
description: An advanced look at some of the core topics in social psychology (e.g. the self, social influence, personal relationships, prejudice and discrimination, group processes) with a strong emphasis on applying the theory in these areas to current social problems and students' daily lives.

title: Psychology of Personality
description: Discussion of different approaches to the study of personality.

title: Human Factors Psychology
description: The application of psychological principles to the design and operation of machines, products, and systems, including human-computer interactions, automation, and training.

title: Human Neuropsychology
description: Study of brain-behavior relationships in humans. Brief review of human neuroanatomy followed by a major emphasis on brain function in learning, memory, language, and other cognitive behaviors. Also includes an examination of behavioral alterations following brain damage.

title: Psychology Of Language
description: Experimental study of language, including sentence comprehension and memory, language acquisition and development, speech perception, and effects of context, perception, reasoning, and linguistic structure on processing of language.

title: Neural Mechanisms of Learning and Memory
description: Survey of topics on the neurobiology of learning and memory, including memory formation and maintenance, long-term potentiation, and the role of various brain areas involved in memory, such as the hippocampus and corticostriatal circuits. Discussions of relevant research articles and current topics in the neuroscience of learning and memory.

title: Psychology of Aging
description: The psychological aspects of human aging. An analysis of the contributions of experimental, developmental, and personality-social psychology to the study of aging. The psychopathology of aging and psychological intervention strategies are also covered.

title: Graduate Research and Development Series
description: Development of professional skills and discussion of professional issues germane to the success of graduate students in the Department of Psychological Sciences

title: Psychological Research Design and Analysis I
description: Introduction to techniques of research planning and design, including critical evaluation of psychological research practices and selected research studies.

title: Physiological Psychology
description: An advanced survey of basic technique, theory, and research in the field of behavioral neuroscience, including memory and learning, sensory and motor processes, motivation, and sexual behavior.

title: Industrial and Organizational Psychology
description: Advanced survey of theory and research pertaining to human behavior in work organizations. Topics include selection, training, and evaluation of employees, job analysis and evaluation, work motivation and satisfaction, organizational structure and development, and working conditions.

title: Psychological Research Design and Analysis II
description: Extensive coverage of repeated measures designs, advanced experimental design, and the treatment of common psychological measures.

title: Psychological Measurement
description: The logic and methodology underlying the construction of psychological measuring instruments from the psychophysical estimate of threshold to the scaling of complex psychological variables.

title: Research Methods in Occupational Health Psychology
description: Reviews research methods used in social science with emphasis on occupational health psychology issues.

title: Applied Research Methods
description: Examines research methods and analyses that are used in the applied psychology literature. Focuses on planning and conducting program evaluation.

title: Applied Research Methods ll
description: Provides students with a detailed knowledge of human resource management research methods and analyses. Focuses on how to plan and conduct research in industrial/organizational psychology.  Preparation for the M.S. practicum.

title: Learning
description: In-depth study of empirical and theoretical research on basic learning principles and their effects on behavior.

title: Perception
description: Various systematic approaches to perception, with emphasis on experimental and quantitative data. The role of perception in affectivity, motivation, and personality theory is stressed.

title: Advanced Cognitive Psychology
description: Study of contemporary trends and research in cognition, including memory, language, problem solving, decision making, and human learning.

title: Psychology of Health
description: Presents psychological research investigating illness and related dysfunction, as well as the promotion and maintenance of health. Provides students with the theoretical, empirical and clinical aspects of health psychology from a number of perspectives, discusses leading research and explores personal and practical implications of this information.

title: Personality Theory and Research
description: A comparative examination of contemporary theories of personality as well as research findings relevant to such theories.

title: Developmental Psychology
description: Human psychological development throughout the lifespan. Examination of major theoretical systems relevant to Developmental Psychology.

title: Judgmental Processes
description: Examination of empirical findings and theoretical approaches to decision making and judgment with emphasis on higher cognitive processes.

title: Pro-Seminar In Social Psychology
description: Discussion of empirical findings and theoretical approaches to selected problem areas, such as attitude change, personality and social structure, person perception, small group processes.

title: Proseminar in Occupational Health Psychology
description: Survey of occupational health issues for first or second year graduate students in health and related fields.

title: Seminar in Occupational Health Behaviors
description: Focuses on organizational and individual prevention and coping behaviors relevant to work-related health problems.

title: Practicum in Occupational Health Psychology
description: Provides supervised practical experience with occupational health problems in various business, manufacturing and government settings. Students will prepare evaluative analyses of their assigned sites.

title: Current Issues in Health Psychology
description: Introduction to current issues in health psychology research.

title: Practicum in Health Psych
description: Independent health research designed in conjunction with major faculty advisor plus another faculty member to produce a journal submission and departmental presentation.

title: Seminar in Psychopharmacology
description: Surveys basic principles of neural communication and pharmacology, the etiology and bio-behavioral bases of substance abuse, and chemical treatment of psychiatric disorders.

title: Practicum in Psychology
description: Supervised practical experience in applied psychology.

title: Practicum in Teaching Psychology
description: Supervised experience regularly teaching a college psychology course.

title: Industrial Psychology/Personnel Training
description: An examination of the training of personnel in an organization. Topics include: determination of an organization's training needs, selection and motivation of trainees, design and evaluation of training programs, and examination of several specific strategies for accomplishing the training function.

title: Industrial Psychology/Work Motivation
description: An examination of empirical findings and theoretical approaches to understanding the relationship between worker motivation and job outcomes.

title: Industrial Psychology/Leadership
description: Examination of current leadership theories, research, and practice in the work setting, focusing on situational approaches to leadership, leadership styles, and interactions between personal characteristics and organizational factors.

title: Industrial Psychology/Personnel Selection
description: Examination of theoretical and practical issues in staffing industrial organizations, including recruitment, test validation, and other equal employment opportunity issues (test fairness, adverse impact, etc.).

title: Organizational Psychology
description: An examination of the individual's role in industrial organizations and the effects of organizational variables on the individual worker. Topics include organizational communication, employee socialization, psychological climates of organizations, psychological stress in organizations, group processes and employee performance, and organizational change.

title: Industrial Psychology: Performance Appraisal
description: Examination of data sources, rating procedures, psychometric criteria for evaluating performance appraisal systems, and models/theories of the performance evaluation process.

title: History and Trends in Psychology
description: A review of the contributions of individuals and intellectual movements to the development of modern psychology. A survey of theoretical systems currently of influence.

title: Seminar in Personality
description: Intensive discussion of current problems of theoretical and empirical interest in the field of personality.

title: Seminar in Experimental Psychology
description: Intensive discussion of a problem of current interest based on the class's study of the pertinent original literature.

title: Seminar in Psychological Measurement
description: Intensive discussion of a problem of current interest, based on the class's study of the pertinent original literature.

title: Seminar in Cognitive Processes
description: Selected topics in the study of human thinking and cognition.

title: Seminar in Mathematical Models of Behavior
description: Selected topics in mathematical psychology, and applications of mathematical models to behavior.

title: Seminar in Social Psychology
description: Emphasis on discussion of advanced topics of current interest in social psychology.

title: Seminar in Industrial Psychology
description: Intensive examination of current empirical and theoretical issues in industrial and organizational psychology.

title: Multivariate Analyses of Behavioral Data
description: Methods of working with behavioral sciences multivariate data, including screen data for compliance with assumption and addressing violations of assumptions.  Coverage of common multivariate analyses used, with specific application to behavioral research content and topics.

title: Seminar in Professional Problems
description: Intensive study and discussion of current professional problems in psychology.

title: Introduction to Public Health
description: An introductory survey course designed to provide a history of public health, an overview of the five core disciplines (epidemiology, biostatistics, environmental health, social and behavioral health, and health policy and management), and information about career options in public health. Investigation and analysis of contemporary issues in public health such as bioterrorism, threats to the nation's food supply, the importance of health promoting behaviors in the prevention of disease, distribution of infectious diseases on a global population, and the role of public health in disaster preparedness and management.

title: Health Behavior Theory
description: Designed to provide students with skills for application to health behavior and health promotion within public health settings. Emphasis on understanding health behavior theories and applying theories to health promotion efforts in public health.

title: Methods and Analysis of Public Health
description: Introductory course on conducting and critiquing research in public health through fundamental principles of public health research methodologies. Skills developed include understanding ethical approaches to research and working with humans, and critically appraising data from scientific studies, understand materials and issues associated with the logic of the scientific method, research design, and qualitative and quantitative analysis.

title: Social Determinants and Public Health
description: Examination of social determinants of health such as economic stability, educational access, health care access, built environment, and social and community context. This course explores the nonmedical factors situated in the places people are born, live, learn, work, play, and pray that influence health outcomes.

title: Health Policy and Administration
description: Provides understanding of health policy issues at the local, state, federal, and global levels, with  focus on U.S. health policy-making and law including health insurance, health economics, legal system, health policy and law including health insurance, health economics, individual rights in health care, and health care access and quality.

title: Health Promotion Practice Methods
description: A health promotion course in which the role of a public health professional in community-based program planning will be discussed including the steps of a needs assessment. Additionally, students will learn various intervention strategies that can be used in the public health field to promote health.  Major concepts, theories, and principles of health promotion/education and intervention strategies will form the foundation of this class.  Cultural, social, and developmental influences on health behavior will also be explored.

title: Seminar Public Health Practice
description: Pre-practicum course of field-based observations and experiences to connect academic concepts to practice in public health culminating in a portfolio to showcase accomplishments to prospective employers and peers.

title: Public Health Practicum
description: Course provides students with an opportunity to synthesize, integrate, and apply practical skills, knowledge, and training learned in courses, to gain professional experience in a public health work environment, and to work on public health practice projects that are of particular interest.

title: Capstone Project in Public Health
description: Focuses on integrating public health knowledge, skills, and practice acquired during coursework and the practicum experience, and culminating in a paper and presentation demonstrating critical thinking on public health topics and further understanding in an area of public health practice. This course is typically completed in the last semester of the undergraduate public health program.

title: Robotics Programming
description: Concepts and practices related to programming robotic systems emphasizing the unique input/output (I/O) and concurrency requirements of robotics. Students learn about sensors, actuators, kinematics, control systems, teleoperated operation and autonomous algorithms. Students write programs that run on a robot as well as a drive station computer with network communication to a robot.

title: Industrial Robotics Lab 2
description: A project-based robotics and automation laboratory course focused on industrial robots emphasizing robot operation, programming, and integration with programmable logic controllers, machine vision systems, and other automation equipment.

title: Industrial Robotics Lab 3
description: A project-based robotics and automation laboratory course focused on industrial robots emphasizing robot operation, programming, and integration with programmable logic controllers, machine vision systems, and other automation equipment.

title: Machine Vision
description: Introduction to practical concepts and techniques for image processing and computer vision emphasizing the fundamental algorithms applicable to robotics, automation and unmanned aerial systems. Major topics covered include: MATLAB programming, image acquisition, geometric and intensity transformations, spatial and frequency domain filtering, image enhancement and restoration, morphological transformations, edge detection, segmentation, compression, feature extraction, and pattern recognition.

title: Integrated Robotics Studio
description: Students complete self-directed projects that bring together and extend applications from previous and concurrent courses toward integrated robotics solutions.  Expected topics of focus draw from industrial robot programming and implementation, programmable logic control, machine vision, human-machine interfaces, other automated systems equipment, data integration, and other aspects of contemporary and emerging automation.  Collaborative projects with industry partners are encouraged.

title: Introduction to Robot Operating System (ROS)
description: A foundational understanding of the current ROS distribution and its application in robotics, preparing students for more advanced topics and practical robotics projects.

title: Robot Power Systems
description: A comprehensive study of power systems for robotics, focusing on the design, implementation, and management of energy sources for mobile and stationary robots.

title: Path Planning and Navigation
description: Gain hands-on experience controlling robots using various sensors while exploring advanced techniques for guiding mobile robots. Develop and implement algorithms for efficient path planning and navigation in various environments including both virtual and real-world applications.

title: Mobile Robotics Navigation Studio
description: A practical, project-based course that focuses on the development and implementation of navigation systems for mobile robots.

title: Sensors and Perception for Robotics
description: Explore a wide range of sensors used in robotics along with the algorithms needed to interpret sensor data. Learn to select the right sensors for various applications, apply perception algorithms, and resolve sensor-related challenges.

title: Digital Manufacturing
description: Discover the cutting-edge world of digital manufacturing, where traditional fabrication methods merge with advanced technology to create precise and complex components. Explore the fundamentals and applications of key digital manufacturing tools.

title: Mobile Robotics Fabrication Studio
description: A hands-on, project-based course where students design, build, and test mobile robot systems from the ground up.

title: Industrial Inspection Systems
description: Explore inspection systems used in industrial processes.  Primary focus on vision inspection equipment including cameras, lenses, sensors, and lighting.

title: Advanced Programmable Logic Controllers
description: Study of the applications of programmable logic controllers (PLCs) in industrial environments. Topics include hardware, wiring, configurations, programming, documentation, troubleshooting, and maintenance of PLC systems.

title: Data Acquisition and Monitoring
description: An in-depth exploration of data acquisition systems and monitoring techniques, focusing on their application in modern engineering and robotics systems.

title: Robot Design and Kinematics
description: Gain a broad understanding of robot systems, focusing on both physical and mathematical models. Design and analyze mobile robots, explore the dynamics of mobile platforms, and assess various mobility solutions to optimize robot designs for specific tasks.

title: Robot Embedded Systems
description: Delve into the application of embedded systems in robotics. Develop skills in extending robot capabilities to resource-limited embedded platforms, exploring microcontroller architecture, real-time operating systems (RTOS), and embedded hardware.

title: Mobile Robotics Embedded Development Studio
description: An advanced, hands-on course focused on the design and programming of embedded systems within mobile robot platforms.

title: Robotics Capstone I
description: Application of robotic principles and design methodology to solve a significant design problem in a team context. Includes determining customer requirements, exploring and choosing design alternatives, scheduling, and project management.

title: Robotics Capstone II
description: A continuation of RAS 480 including the implementation, testing, and delivery of the proposed project.

title: Topics in Recreation Resources
description: Discussion of topics and activities of importance in recreation resources.

title: Research Methods In Parks And Recreation
Design Of Parks And Recreation Areas
Russian I
description: Introduction to the structure of modern Russian. Emphasis on the sounds of Russian, the use of the Cyrillic alphabet, and oral drills with added practice in the language laboratory.

title: Russian II
description: Continuation of the study of Russian grammar and oral communication.

title: Intermediate Conversation And Composition In Russian
Russian Culture and Civilization
description: Russia's past and present in the light of principal ideologies with emphasis upon fine art, literature, music, religion, politics, and education. Equal time will be devoted to the Tsarist and Soviet periods.

title: Russian III
description: Completion of the study of Russian grammar. Reading of selected prose on the intermediate level.

title: Russian IV
description: Intensive review of Russian grammar. Exercises in reading selected modern Russian texts in the original.

title: Intermediate Studies in Russian
description: Offered only to participants in study abroad programs. Prior consultation for approval is expected.

title: Russian Literature in Translation/The 19th Century
description: Survey of principal writers of Tsarist Russia with emphasis upon Turgenev, Dostoevsky, Tolstoy, and Chekhov.

title: Russian Literature
description: The development of Russian literature since the Revolution, with emphasis upon Mayakovsky, Sholokov, Pasternak, and Solzhenitsyn.

title: Russian V
description: Reading of Russian short stories of the nineteenth and twentieth centuries, including works by Pushkin, Lermontov, Dostoevsky, and Chekhov.

title: Russian Conversation and Composition
description: Discussion in Russian. Extensive practice in writing Russian compositions.

title: Introduction to Sales
description: This class allows students to see the business world through the eyes of a salesperson. Together, we will explore the sales profession, including how to create value for customers throughout the sales process effectively. Upon completing the course, students will have demonstrated the ability to initiate a sales conversation by understanding how to ask practical questions and utilizing their active listening skills. Specific topics will vary by semester.

title: Fundamentals of Professional Selling
description: Focuses on interpersonal communications between buyers and sellers, both oral and written. The mechanics and intricacies of the sales process will be explored in detail. Sales skills will be further developed via sales role plays.

title: International Sales Experience
description: Embark on an immersive education abroad experience that takes learning beyond the classroom and into the heart of global communities. This course offers students the unique opportunity to explore a foreign country through academic study, cultural engagement, and hands-on experiences.

title: Sales Technology
description: This course reviews sales technology and examines the application of sales technologies to the sales process. Course projects will include role plays, along with the active use of technology systems. Content will include managing leads, prospects, and the sales pipeline using technology.

title: Sales Force Leadership
description: Management of the sales force in non-retail settings including coaching, supervising, providing feedback, training, and other sales management frameworks and techniques.

title: Customer Relationship Management
description: This course introduces students to the strategies, methodology and technology used to manage customer relationships; and support and enhance the sales and marketing functions.

title: Advanced Selling
description: Students apply their professional selling skills through advanced instruction and skill development. Course projects include role plays, along with real-time use of CRM systems, as part of a live sales experience. Content includes prospecting, sales pipeline management, effective sales conversations, and adaptive selling in a real-world sales context.

title: Sales Practicum
description: The student will work as a sales intern in a business environment as a means to gain applied sales experience. A report detailing duties performed and tasks accomplished is required at the end of the internship period, along with an evaluation by the supervisor and a self-assessment. Additionally, the student will complete required reading and assignments related to the reading.

title: Sales Negotiation
description: Students learn the fundamentals of negotiation and how those fundamentals are used during the sales process, with the goal of improving understanding of negotiation theory and practical application. Students are expected to participate in role-playing excercises.

title: Key Account Management
description: Students are introduced to the complex strategies and tactics used in identifying and managing key customer accounts, including those with large multi-national footprints.

title: Cooperation Selling
description: Students learn how to successfully synchronize their selling process with the decision maker's buying process in order to completely satisfy customers and generage more sales. Students are expected to participate in role playing exercises.

title: Special Topics in Sales
description: Discussion and analysis of current and emerging issues in sales, including topics related to new sales models, new technologies, and the practice of sales.

title: Special Topics in Sales
description: Investigation and discussion of a selected topic or group of topics in sales.

title: Introduction to Speech Communication, Theatre and Dance
Sensory Analysis
description: Sensory analysis of appearance, texture, aroma, flavor; physiology of sensory receptors; laboratory and consumer panels; and interpretation of data.

title: Pet Food Sensory Analysis
description: Sensory analysis of food and other products for non-food animals (e.e. dogs and cats with mention of other species) with an emphasis on methods (descriptive, discrimination, consumer, sensory-related instrumental) used in the laboratory and in the field.

title: Descriptive Sensory Analysis:Methods
description: Specification and application of descriptive sensory analysis methods for use in product development, research, and quality control. Practical experiences in conducting these tests.

title: Descriptive Sensory Analysis: Food and Fragrance
description: Identification and use of descriptive sensory attributes as they relate to food and fragrance for product development, research, and quality control. Practical experiences in identifying, describing, and application of food and fragrance attributes.

title: Descriptive Sensory Analysis: Non-Food
description: Identification and use of descriptive sensory methods and attributes as they relate to non-food product development, research, and quality control. Practical experiences and case studies in identifying, describing, and application methods and attributes for research using products such as textiles, cosmetics, and personal goods.

title: Descriptive Sensory Analysis: Panel Training
description: Techniques for training and evaluating sensory descriptive panels for use in product development, research, and quality control.

title: Descriptive Sensory Analysis: Rapid Methods
description: Use and understanding of rapid sensory techniques to descriptively evaluate products.

title: Consumer Research-Fundamentals
description: Fundamentals of consumer research in terms of  organizing and executing studies.  Planning studies, selecting products, recruiting target consumers, and organizing study execution are included.

title: Consumer Research - Qualitative
description: This course provides a deep dive into qualitative research, including the design, application, execution, and reporting.  This course will educate the student on the appropriate tools for qualitative data collection based on the objective, with a particular emphasis on interviews and focus groups.

title: Consumer Research – Observational and Behavioral
description: Study of observational and behavioral research methods as they are applied to consumer product research, including ethics and techniques for such research.  Methods and analysis for both qualitative and quantitative methods for such data collection will be covered.

title: Consumer Research – International and Special Populations
description: Study of consumer product research with international and special populations including children and other special populations.

title: Consumer Research – Attitudinal
description: Study of attitudinal research methods, including questionnaires and facial recognition, for product understanding.  Techniques including emotion, mood, and psychographic research methods will be covered.  Methods for consumer segmentation beyond traditional demographical segmentation will be discussed.

title: Consumer Research – Quantitative
description: Methods and issues associated with measuring consumer responses to products including preference testing, preference ranking, acceptance testing, hedonic scales, and consumption testing.

title: Sensory Analysis Applications of Statistics
description: Application of statistics to sensory data. Emphasis on appropriate analyses, software use, interpretation of data, and reporting.

title: Practicum in Sensory Analysis
description: Application of statistics to sensory data. Emphasis on appropriate analyses, software use, interpretation of data, and reporting.

title: International Research Experience
description: International experience in conducting research. Development of strong collaboration and pre-planning, actual conduct, and reporting of results. International travel is required for this course. One hour of lecture per week and 1-2 week in-country practicum.

title: Sensory and Instrumental Volatile Analysis and their Relationship
description: Use and relationship of methods for measuring and relating volatiles including sensory and instrumental methods such as gas chromatography mass-spectrometry (GC-MS) and gas chromatography olfactometry (GC-O).

title: Seminar in Sensory Analysis and Consumer Behavior
description: Student experiences for professional development including developing and delivering a presentation and navigating the workplace.

title: Critical Experiences in Industry Research
description: Practicum in professional communication in the disciplines including proposal writing, networking and collaborating with professional colleagues, and developing and/or presenting project results.

title: Respiratory Finction In Health And Disease
Interpretation Of Radiologic Studies Of Body Systems
Small Animal Clinical & Critical Care Nutrition
Security, Custody and Discipline in Correctional Institutions
Seminar in Sociology of Small Groups
description: Seminar in Sociology of Small Groups

title: Introduction to Sociology
description: Development, structure, and functioning of human groups; social and cultural patterns; and the principal social processes.

title: Introduction to Sociology, Honors
