title: Bowhunting Equipment and Skills
description: This course covers the bowhunting equipment currently used for hunting. Bowhunting skills to include distance estimation, shot placement, etc. are also covered in this course.

title: Sporting Clays Range Development and Operations
description: This course will cover the history and present day rules of sporting clays, the development of sporting clays ranges and their ongoing operations and maintenance.

title: Trap and Skeet Range Development and Operations
description: This course will cover the development of ""trap"" and ""skeet"" shooting ranges. Operating principles will be covered to include staffing, event planning and implementation.

title: Introduction to Wildlife and Outdoor Enterprise Management
description: This course covers the wildlife and outdoor enterprise management business and profession in North America and worldwide.

title: Fauna and Flora of the Great Plains
description: Identification, natural history, and distributions of fauna and flora species occurring in the Great Plains.

title: Pond and Sportfish Management
description: Examines principles to manage ponds and recreational sportfish populations in North America including: pond design, construction, and maintenance, sportfish stocking and harvesting, life history of common species, fish habitat requirements, aquatic vegetation identification and control, and fundamentals of operating aquatic guiding operations.

title: Undergraduate Research in Wildlife and Outdoor Enterprise Management
description: Undergraduate students work with faculty mentors on projects involving various elements of research in WOEM including identifying a problem, designing and conducting experiments, interpreting and communicating the research results. The completed project must be presented at a professional forum, and students are required to write a final project report on completion of their project.

title: Big Game Management
description: Application of practices to manage North American big game species including harvest management, land management, ethics, laws, species' habitat requirements, natural history, daily and seasonal activities of each species, socioeconomic factors, and basics of managing a hunting operation.

title: Upland Game Bird Management
description: Application of practices to manage upland game birds in North America to include: harvest management, land management, ethics, laws, species' habitat requirements, daily and seasonal activities of each species, natural history, socioeconomic factors, and basics of managing a hunting operation.

title: Waterfowl and Wetlands Management
description: This course explores the natural history of waterfowl and wetland birds and habitat management. It includes basic information on the habitat requirements, management techniques, ranges, migration patters, history, problems, diseases, and identification of the waterfowl resources of North America. Professional principles and practices associated with hunting waterfowl species in North America to include: ethics, laws, species' habitat requirements, and daily and seasonal activities of each species, methods of harvest, dog handling, post-harvest handling and clientele interactions. Includes the following success skills: Communicates Effectively and Maintains Professional Skills and Attitudes.

title: Advanced Wildlife Habitat Management
description: Theory and practice of managing terrestrial and aquatic vegetation for wildlife production.

title: Internship for Wildlife and Outdoor Enterprise Management
description: Intensive, paid practical experience with an approved hunting and/or shooting sports and/or fishing business.

title: Problems in Wildlife and Outdoor Enterprise Management
description: Independent study and/or research project development guided by faculty in Wildlife and Outdoor Enterprise Management.

title: Wildlife and Outdoor Enterprise Management Senior Seminar
description: This course will be used as a capstone course for the Wildlife and Outdoor Enterprise Management curriculum; to include case studies and field experience reviews.

title: Wildlife and Outdoor Enterprise Management Study Abroad
description: Seminar and travel course designed to prepare students before an international study abroad experience focused on several facets of wildlife and conservation, natural resources, diseases, and land management research. Upon completion of travel, students will analyze, critique, and report their experiences. Course will focus on appropriate oral and written documentation of the experience.

title: Human-Wildlife Conflicts
description: This course explores the theory and practice of assessing and controlling damage done by wild and feral vertebrate animals, especially mammals and birds. Content covers the philosophical, biological, and practical basis for managing human-wildlife conflicts. It includes basic information on use of traps, toxicants, repellents, exclusion and other wildlife control methods. Emphasis is on protecting agricultural crops and livestock, forest resources, and property.

title: Resource Selection Methods and Theory
description: Develops graduate students' knowledge and application of theory and methods used to evaluate the resource selection and distribution of wildlife and plant species.

title: The History And Politics Of Family Violence
